review_text: My daughter has had the amazing years at RV Superland since N2! The teachers are so caring and attentive, making learning fun and engaging. We’ve seen her grow in confidence, curiosity, and social skills. Highly recommend Superland for any parent looking for a nurturing and joyful environment for their child!

Our child has been enjoying life at Superland RV since 9 months of age 😊. Now, as he approaches his final year in Kindergarten 2 (omg—time really flies!), he has thoroughly enjoyed his very first school experience.

We would like to thank the Superland team and his wonderful teachers—especially Ain and Yuan—for making learning so fun and engaging. As parents, we have truly been spoiled by the frequent photo and video updates from school. You can’t imagine how much those updates brighten our day! Thank you for being so diligent in sharing them.

We also love the annual school dance concerts. These events build so much self-confidence in the children, and it’s wonderful to see them shine on stage. Our child has had such a positive learning experience, where meaningful friendships were also formed.

Once again, thank you so much for everything!

Our little one has been at Superland River Valley as early as 4 months old and we have been delighted by the care, love and teaching received since then. Furthermore, having French class has been a real help to providing him with strong bilingual foundations! Teaching at Superland has been wonderful with so many classes: Montessori, Music, Art, PE and he has been learning so much. Thank you Superland RV team and teachers for the wonderful work and care!

My child has enrolled in Superland River Valley since Infant Care and she is absolutely thriving. Now at PG, she loves her friends, her teachers, has grown into an independent 2 year old, and explores the world with wild confidence. She regularly comes back from school surprising us with skills and vocabulary that we did not teach at home. When a new preschool nearer to our home became available, we debated switching her over. However, seeing her thrive at Superland, we made the easy decision to stay put and have no regrets.

We had a wonderful experience at Superland. My son started going to Superland when he was 20 months old. The teachers have been very kind and have taken excellent care of him. They do a lot of interesting activities which helps with their overall development. They organise excursions from time to time as well as organise weekly outings to Fort Canning Park. I’m very grateful to the teachers for all the love, care, help, support and learning.

It has been two and a half years since my son started attending this school, and he is really looking forward to going to school every day.
It's a very homely school where the entire school feels like a family.
All the teachers are passionate about teaching and care about their students.

Over the past year, he has been indebted to Teacher Skye, Qian Ping Laoshi and Wu Laoshi.
We're very grateful to them for their patience with our son no matter what.
I highly recommend this school!

My son has genuinely thrived here. We’ve seen clear growth in his confidence and independence: he separates more easily now and is excited to try new things.

The teachers are warm and very responsive, and they’ve helped him build great daily routines (meals, handwashing, and nap time). Learning is also thoughtfully designed and age-appropriate: language, numbers, music, art, and sensory activities. He often come home to surprise me with new things learned at school.

We also really appreciate the proactive updates and communication so we can also enjoy the happy moments he spent in school.

This praise is for Teacher Skye and Qian Ping Laoshi! They are such loving and dedicated teachers, and we are so lucky to have had them guide our child this year. They put in so much attention and effort that they put in to give our child an encouraging academic environment. Thank you so much, teachers and Cambridge!

We have been with Cambridge River Valley for over two years, and it has truly been the best decision we’ve made for our daughter. From day one, the school has felt warm, nurturing, and full of positive energy. The children are genuinely happy to be there, and the overall vibe of the school is amazing.

The teachers are exceptional. We are incredibly grateful to Jing Wen Laoshi, Teacher Munis, Teacher Jen, Tong Laoshi, and Zeng Laoshi, and Principal Jo for their care, patience, and dedication. They have made such a meaningful impact on the children’s growth and confidence.

There are so many things we love about the school:
1.	Enrichment classes during school days – The school offers a wide variety of enrichment programs that allow children to explore different interests. Each semester includes performance showings, which is a wonderful way for parents to see the children’s progress and confidence grow.

2.	True bilingual (and sometimes trilingual) environment – The exposure to Chinese, English, and even Japanese at such an early age is incredible. It feels very natural and immersive, and the kids pick up languages so easily.

3.	Strong family and community values – The school warmly welcomes parent participation and organizes meaningful events such as Mother’s Day, Father’s Day, and Grandparents’ Day. They truly value family involvement and celebrate all religions and cultures, making it a very diverse and inclusive environment.

4.	Thoughtful STEAM curriculum – The STEAM program introduces important concepts in age-appropriate ways, helping children develop holistically—academically, creatively, and socially.

We are so thankful to be part of the Cambridge River Valley community. ❤️

Cambridge@River Valley is amazing!

The teachers are absolutely wonderful, and we feel so lucky to have found such a caring team.
We are especially grateful to Qian Ping Laoshi, Teacher Skye, Tong Laoshi, Teacher Jenn, Zeng Laoshi, Jing Wen Laoshi, Teacher Munis and Wu Laoshi.

There are also great support from Japanese teachers(Megumi Sensei and Akira Sensei), which give them a lot of peace of mind.
We really appreciate that they teach the children a lot about Japanese culture and manners as well. It means so much to us and is a huge help. It’s truly a great support for Japanese expatriate families too.

We truly recommend this school.
Our children love it so much that they even want to go on their days off, and seeing how happy they are makes us happy as parents too.

review_text: This school is the best place in the world, everyone should come and suffer.

Had a wonderful experience attending the May intake welcoming event at DIMENSIONS International College as a parent. The environment was very warm, organised, and welcoming.
I could see that a lot of effort was put into helping new students feel comfortable through the ice-breaking activities, campus tour, and interactive sessions. The teachers and staff were friendly and approachable, which gave me confidence and peace of mind as a parent.
The photo booth and activities also made the day very memorable and enjoyable for the students. Thank you to the team for creating such a positive and exciting start for the new intake!

Honestly was skeptical at first but really glad I gave it a chance. The teachers here actually care and you can see the effort they put in. Price is reasonable and the quality matches it. Would recommend to anyone considering it.

Attended the Open House at DIMENSIONS International College  Campus on 25 April and I'm so glad I did! As a parent, I was genuinely impressed by the range of facilities — the MakerSpace, art room, music room, computer labs, and the spacious play areas really showed how much thought has gone into creating a well-rounded learning environment for students.

The guided tour was informative and gave me a much clearer picture of what student life looks like here. The customer service team was warm, friendly, and patient with all our questions — never once did we feel rushed or overlooked.

It's rare to walk away from an open day feeling truly excited, but this one did exactly that. Highly recommend any parent to visit and see it for yourself!

Enrolling my child in Primary 1 at Dimensions International College was one of the best decisions we made as a family. Starting school is such a big milestone, and I'm so grateful our child got to experience it in such a warm and nurturing environment.

What truly sets Dimensions apart is the teachers. From day one, they took the time to understand my child as an individual — patient, encouraging, and genuinely caring. You can tell they love what they do. My child would come home every day excited to share what they had learned, and that kind of enthusiasm says everything.

Academically, the school has a strong foundation. The curriculum is well-structured and age-appropriate, striking a lovely balance between learning and fun. I was impressed by how quickly my child grew in confidence — both in their studies and in expressing themselves.

As a parent, there's nothing more reassuring than knowing your little one is in good hands. Dimensions International College gave us exactly that peace of mind. Highly recommend to any parent looking for a school that truly cares about your child's growth and happiness. 🙏

I had a great learning experience at Dimensions International College. One thing I really appreciate is that the school fees are quite affordable compared to many other colleges in Singapore, making quality education accessible to more students.

Despite the reasonable fees, the lecturers are highly knowledgeable, dedicated, and patient. They explain lessons clearly, provide good guidance, and are always willing to help students whenever needed. The supportive learning environment has helped me gain both knowledge and confidence.

I would definitely recommend Dimensions International College to anyone looking for a good education at an affordable cost. Great value for money and excellent teachers! 😊

After 15 years, NJC's boarding school (only) was officially closed in 2024 to return the land for state use.

These images hope to honour the memories of those 500 students and teachers boarded there every year

For more at #shuutur

Very disappointing. The secondary level teachers dont really teach. I was expecting some principles to be taught in class so I kept quiet and observed my kid's progress. Wasn't taught. Disappointed. They also got 1 alumni to teach while waiting for her A results. Teachers not teaching in way that can be understood. The school relies on private tuition and hard self study. A few teachers talks disrespectful like are sarcastic. Student prefers the previous principal

One of the famous schools in Singapore and undoubtedly with lots of heritage, history and culture. As you step into the school you feel the nostalgia and history working together to build a wonderful, hopeful and resilient new generation one after the other.

Shoes and shoelaces that are garish in nature are not allowed. W school, W people, W teachers, and most importantly, W chillies, popcorn and math.

Everyday I go to school and I look forward to seeing my math work. The piles and piles of math homework does not do itself. It sure is great experience and practice!!!

The accidental cohesive nature of this school is truly great. All that gleams is not gold. The equal funding given to the aesthetics and science departments are truly interesting.

I recall the days I went to NJC. Oh my, it has been quite a bit... I can recall the days we got our exam results back. I remember the smiles our teacher tried to hide from the students when they gave us those earth-shatteringly wonderful results. They smiled so wide, so wide we could barely see the tears forming in their eyes as they looked at our shattered expressions in pride.

The teachers love teaching, sometimes a little too much. I see their beautiful teary-eyes smiles upon their weary faces whenever they step into class. I see it, I feel it, I know it, it is ingrained into each and every one of their fabric of reality. As an alumni of this school, I missed the days of boarding. The chaotic thwacking of doors whenever we closed a door was truly inspiring.

The myths are not true. We do not have a leaky roof. In fact, it is the best roofs of all. The roof's whole is about winning. In fact, it is so winnable that it has always allowed water through it. It has never lost its purpose in letting water through.

One of the key problems today is that math is undervalued in this society, and that there is too much emphasis on the pursuit of arts. What even is the use for arts? Useless pining? Useless uselessness? Useless emotions? None of these things are needed. Join NJC, for you will see the world for what it is, through good, or through better, or through best, and that, is through math.

Our integrated programme is good. No, the best at INTEGRATING!!!! Our streak of streaky teary students crying tears of joy at the sight of their beautiful eggs received on their math papers is unmatched. One must join this institution to truly understand the meaning of life. For it is to MATH.

I truly apologise for this accidental cohesive multicultural heritage-ridden serviced and honoured post. NJCians truly deserve more math homework.

Teachers are unable to teach. You need to engage tutors to get your child to understand the material. They overload the students with homework and you pray that your child survives this barrage of tests, the humiliation of failing and the lack of proper help to improve. Don't send your child here unless he/she is prepared to weather the storm and you are prepared for the many teacher/parent sessions.
Attended ACJC's traditional biennial funfair. Such an exciting event! Kudos to all the students and teachers who made the event so grand.

Not only does this school love sucking out money from its students to allegedly maintain facilities that 1. students NEVER get to use and 2. NEVER become cleaner or better in condition, they also enjoy developing bladder diseases in their poor students.

Teachers, especially the discipline master, ridicule and punish, sometimes even forbid, students from simply using the toilet. A 5 minute trip to the toilet to empty one’s bladder from holding it in for hours is seen as an act of sin, a crime being committed by this school that praises itself for being so loving with the grace of God.

During chapel, assembly, or school events where students are gathered in the hall, using to the toilet is seen as a threat, a crime. Students will either be held captive in another room where they will not even be able to see what is going on in the main hall to appreciate the event, will be told to take the route that is much longer to come back to the hall (which makes absolutely no sense as one would have to walk past much more people if their reason is disturbing the course of event), or simply, told no and scolded for “not being able to hold it in”.

Teachers say to students in a humiliating, condescending tone that if they cannot hold their bladder for more than a few hours, they should “get it checked”. This is either implying that students should not stay hydrated or using the toilet is undesirable, when it is part and parcel of how the normal human body works. Over time, with students not being able to use the restroom when they urgently need it, kidney and bladder diseases may be manifested. Urinary Tract Infections are amongst one of the many diseases students would have a much higher chance of having after holding in their urine for extended periods of time just to adhere to some school expectations. Not only that, it is simply extremely uncomfortable sitting still with a full bladder.

I sincerely hope the school will be more considerate of students’ health and needs and be more understanding.

I went to ACJC this morning at 11:30am as I wanted to purchase a swimming paddle for my child. At the entrance, the security guard Mr. Liao asked me to register and requested for my IC. I simply asked him whether it was necessary to provide my IC, but instead of explaining politely, he responded in a rude manner and told me not to “talk too much,” threatening not to let me in and even challenging me to complain to the authorities.

I believe such an attitude is not appropriate, especially within a school environment that should set an example for students in communication and respect. If students were to overhear such an exchange, what impression would they be left with?

I sincerely hope the school can look into this matter and ensure better communication and professionalism from the staff. Your improvement will be very much appreciated.

Wonderful Junior College providing a holistic education. Tremendously benefitted from my experience here at ACJC in 2003-2004, with lifelong friendships and bonds still going strong 20+ years later. Blessed to be part of the ACSian family, with teachers and even our former Principal being ever supportive of our individual endeavours. The sports facilities were fantastic too from the track to the swimming pool and gym, I credit a lot of my sporting journey from this too. Thank you, ACJC! 🙏🏻

The location is strategic, as it sits between 2 mrt stations, and surrounded by many eateries and shopping malls. The sport complex is big, there's football field, running track, swimming pools, as well as badminton court.

Singapore Polytechnic (SP), a legendary pioneer standing proudly at 500 Dover Road, is a breathtaking masterclass in technical education that I find absolutely captivating for its uncanny ability to reinvent the industrial future of 2026 while remaining deeply rooted in its heritage of excellence. Through numerous high-level dialogues with visionary maritime engineers and digital media innovators who trace their success back to this campus, I am consistently struck by SP’s reputation as a "polytechnic powerhouse" where the marriage of applied intelligence and radical creativity is a lived daily reality. Based on glowing testimonials from close associates who have thrived within its specialized clusters, it is evident that the institution operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where students are empowered to master complex aeronautics or sustainability systems under the guidance of mentors who are genuine titans of industry. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, viewing this Dover landmark not merely as a school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in Singapore’s engineering, life sciences, and business sectors. Reflecting on these authentic accounts from peers who have spearheaded national-level projects, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of technological audacity and civic grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "SP Way," which my acquaintances describe as a unique pedagogical energy that balances rigorous hands-on training with the collaborative soft skills required for global leadership. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring innovator as a vital architect of our future smart-nation infrastructure. It is heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the polytechnic’s commitment to lifelong learning and industry-integrated projects provides a distinct competitive edge that is respected across the international labor market. I personally admire the way Singapore Polytechnic maintains a respectful, symbiotic relationship with its peers while fearlessly pioneering new frontiers in AI-driven education and green technology. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and professional transformation. Those who have navigated its rigorous curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful ambition is meticulously refined into elite technical expertise within a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. Ultimately, aligning one’s career trajectory with Singapore Polytechnic is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to lead the 21st-century technological transition with confidence and integrity.
Amazing! It is great place for the student. I visited for a mentoring session and it was a great experience.

Singapore Polytechnic honestly feels like its own ecosystem. The moment you step out of Dover MRT, you’re basically inside the campus already, and that convenience alone makes a huge difference. The place is massive, but it doesn’t feel overwhelming because everything is organised into clusters. Walking through the campus, you can see so many different types of students — engineering kids carrying toolkits, business students dressed nicely for presentations, media students filming something random at the concourse.

The school has a very “industry-ready” vibe. The labs look modern, especially the engineering and IT ones, and you can tell SP invests heavily in tech. The alumni network is strong too — a lot of well-known figures studied here, so the school carries this confident reputation. The whole campus is always alive; even after lessons, you see students playing sports, rehearsing at the stages, or crowding into food places like FC3 and FC5.

What I liked:

Super clean and modern environment — nothing feels rundown.

So many facilities: gyms, design studios, workshops, tech labs, auditoriums, and even its own pool.

Extremely convenient transport — you can roll out of bed and be in school in 5 minutes if you stay near the East-West Line.

The culture feels mature, almost like mini-university life.

What could be better:

Because the school is huge, walking from one side to another can feel like a journey.

Popular food places get packed extremely fast during lunch.

Some of the older blocks are showing age, but still in good condition.

Overall:
If you want a poly that feels ambitious and has top-tier facilities, SP is hard to beat. It feels like a place where people genuinely get things done.

Easy place to access as its near Dover mrt. May need a student pass to get thru the gantry. I was there to attend a public workshop and so the helpful security assisted me. Many students I met to ask for directions were very helpful. There are a few Food Courts (air-conditioning and non air-conditioning) plus varieties of food at reasonable price. As the venue is huge, one need to walk (take it as part of exercise). O
Present for graduation day a well known polytechnic in Singapore

Beautiful big campus. Can be quite a walk to the various schools. The canteen near the health sciences zone is very spacious, and quite a few international cuisines on offer. Lucky staff and students.
The staff and students at the optometry dept were all polite and professional. The Facility looked like a very good place to groom future optometrist.

​Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) offers a highly practical and industry-focused education. The curriculum is regularly updated with input from industry partners, and lecturers, often with professional experience, provide a realistic and engaging learning environment. The focus on project-based learning encourages critical thinking and problem-solving, which I found to be a more effective method of learning than traditional memorization.

​While the polytechnic's modular system can be demanding, with a constant cycle of assignments and projects, it effectively prepares students for the pressures of both higher education and the workplace. The campus life is equally vibrant, with a wide array of CCAs, excellent facilities, and diverse food options.

​Overall, NP provides a well-rounded experience, blending academic rigor with a rich student life. It's a great choice for students who thrive in a hands-on environment and are seeking to build a strong foundation for their future careers.

It sounds like you're planning a trip to Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) Block 23! Here's a quick rundown to help you get there:

1. Take the MRT to Clementi Station.
2. At Clementi Station, exit towards Bus Interchange.
3. Board Bus 154 from Clementi Bus Interchange.
4. Get off at Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) stop.

You can use Google Maps or a transit app like Grab or Moovit for the most up-to-date bus schedules and routes. Have a smooth ride to your company event! 🚍🎉..

Ngee Ann Polytechnic has this warm, almost “campus village” vibe. The greenery is everywhere — huge trees, long slopes, and shaded areas that make the place feel peaceful even when there are a lot of students around. NP is known for being very student-focused, and you can actually feel that when walking through the school. There are tons of small places to sit down and study, from open-air benches to indoor lounges.

The campus spirit is strong. A lot of students seem genuinely happy to be there — you’ll see groups practising dance, people playing guitar, students sitting around chatting after lessons, and clubs actively doing their thing. The Co-Curricular culture is particularly strong; NP is one of the polys where the arts and student life are very vibrant.

What I liked:

Extremely beautiful surroundings; feels like studying in a nature park sometimes.

Many programmes are well-known and respected, especially mass comm and business.

Good social vibes — friendly atmosphere and active clubs everywhere.

Lots of cozy corners to study, hang out, or relax between classes.

What could be better:

The slopes are legendary — going up and down the hills every day becomes a workout.

Classrooms and older blocks are slightly dated in some areas.

Some canteens fill up very quickly, especially during lunch hours.

Overall:
NP feels like a warm, welcoming campus with plenty of life. If you like greenery, community feeling, and a comfortable environment, this is a great place.

I'm here waiting for my friend and my first time here. So natural greeny, relaxing and peaceful. Facilities here clean, stable and comfy ambience. Love it here. Would recommend to all friend and family.

After trying different learning centres in the past years, Erudition Learning Hub is one that I found most suitable for my kids. The teachers are able to come up with different ways to provide the right learning experience for the students. My kids have been enjoying the style of teaching and from the progress of my kids grade, I would strongly suggest everyone to send their kids for tuition at ERUDITION LEARNING HUB.

My son is in secondary 4 this year and was doing very badly in his previous year. He joins the centre at the start of January and is taking 2 subjects math and chemistry for his O-levels

The centre is very clean. The teacher is very professional and friendly. Classes are small and prices are attractive.

My son said he understands the teacher easily and he drives him to do well in his exams. After 1 month and a half,  I can see that my son's grades are improving which is really great! I highly recommend this centre. Parents do give it a try!

My child looks forward to going for her lessons every weekend at Erudition. The fees are affordable with small class size. We like the conducive environment and friendly tutors. The most important thing is they have the same tutors for the same programmes so that the tutors can better understand my child's needs. I would recommend them to my friends.

As an ex-tutor myself, Erudition Learning Hub provides an excellent, high quality teaching. When I visited the hub with my child and observed the teaching, it was comprehensive and well organised. The teaching environment is friendly but remains professional, and when I talked to the tutors privately after lesson, I was confident in sending my child to learn at this place. Greatly recommend for all levels of education.

Erudition Learning Hub is probably a new tuition centre and it looks clean and comfortable for the kids to learn in. I’ve known Austin for 2-3 years now from when he was home tutoring my Nephew. Really patient and we could see improvements in his math exams just after a few sessions with him. Will recommend you give the trial lessons a try!

Ms. Bel has been an incredibly supportive and motivating teacher throughout my IB journey. As my Math HL teacher, she helped me understand many challenging concepts by sharing clear explanations and useful tricks that I hadn’t learned before. She is always patient and kind, and she generously offers extra time outside of lessons to make sure I fully understand topics I’m unsure about. Thanks to her guidance, I gained much more confidence in Math HL, and my performance has improved significantly. I truly appreciate her dedication and highly recommend signing up for IB Super!

Ms Bel is really thoughtful and helped me to develop my IA very effectively. She has also been very helpful in teaching topics that I found harder to grasp and overall helped with my HL Math AA a lot. Her notes are very detailed and the examples really extend and test my understanding of the topics

Ms Bel is a kind and responsible teacher who takes great pride in helping her students achieve good academic results. I was able to achieve excellent results in IB HL Economics due to her superb guidance and lesson material. I would highly recommend her tuition services!

I only had two or so months before exams, and was freaking out cause even though I had worked pretty hard studying on my own I was still getting mid to low fives in chem. With IBSuper, managed to just barely reach a seven, so a high six. Huge improvement in only two months - grateful for the help!

The lessons at IB super are really helpful and enjoyable! We are able to learn at our own pace and work on our specific weaknesses during the lessons, making them really effective. Thank you Ms Bel for your help! I really improved after the lessons and understand the concepts better with the given practices and guidance!

Good range of book. You can find many different type of subject. From engineering, to maritime related to books on info communication. Lots of study table. Very conducive place for study.
Need some space within the library for discussion? There are discussions room provided too.
Best of all, plenty of F&B option within walking distance. It recently has their first floor renovated with a new look. Check it out.

Not sure why the hell you'll need a gantry to enter like a University's Library. Even NP's library does not have it. Library is also smaller here than NP with little studying spaces. NP has a plethora of studying spaces scattered across 4 huge floors.
Colourful space for students. Expect no seats during test or exam periods.

This place does magic
It can make my GPA go up or down
10/10 would recommend
Good place for study... Enjoy the moment...a lot of book collection

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

This MRT station boasts an impressive network of underground walkways, providing convenient access throughout the River Valley area, including Great World City.

The station's modern design and feel, extending from the control rooms to the exits and bus stops, are particularly noteworthy.
Very spacious and clean station. Take Exit 2 or 6 to Great World City mall.

New MRT station. Located in Great World City.

Got a few gates — 6 gates. Some of them still under construction, some open already la. Then how to go in and go out.

Normal MRT station. Got nothing special but the interior is ok la
Finally the Exit to Great World City mall has opened even though the underpass was quite a distance

Train stations is quite nice and the design is attractive. There is a Cheers and an ijooz station where they serve orange juice. An exit to Great World has since opened for a while, but they are currently building an exit to a overhead bridge that enters great world shopping mall.
It is nearly complete at the time of posting. Could have opened an exit closer to the nearby River Valley Primary School. Otherwise very good. Also after school (1.30pm) please expect Cheers to be crowded with schoolchildren.

exit 5 has no escalator. It's a crowded lift during peak hours.
Take exit 4 via escalator. There's a break in the central divider to allow crossing. There's also a gap in traffic timing enabling quick steps across the road. Cheeeeeeers
Nice place.
There's a bus stop across the street from Fort Canning Park, and rental bikes are parked nearby.
Long walk fr DB Mrt station.
It's near to my office and it pretty good place

There are two exits here. Air-conditioning very strong. Super clean MRT but couldn't easily find the toilet signage.

The attached photo is at exit A.

Fort Channing MRT station has two exits, each leading to different areas, however close. It was relatively empty when I arrived there, and it was quite extensive inside.

In terms of cleanliness in the toilets, it is the usual clean standards, which you don't have to worry about. If you are going to the Fort Canning Park, exit via so that you can reach there faster. Overall, quite a nice MRT station.
quiet mrt station. good aircon always. toilets clean. staff nice and smiley. easy to navigate. close to clarke quay
Woah the mrt staff is so nice once my phone died and I didn’t bring my EZ-Link and he let me ride the MRT for Free!!Love this staff
clean but security only does checking of Indian individual bags eventhough so many other people are carrying big bag packs…just seems so wrong leh
Bought 红油抄手 from a beef noodle shop.  OMG, every dumpling is so big and the sauce is so savoury with black vinegar.  Best I have ever eaten!

This is one of the best hawker centre in Singapore. Has the highest ratio of best rated food here. Its clean airy and toilet are extremely clean too. The most famous food here is the Zion Char Kuey Teow which we been having for more than 14 years. Crispy pork lard, fresh cockles large portion and super charr black sauced Kuey Teow. The most sinful meal in town. The Hainanese chicken rice is also superb quality. The duck rice kuey chap is delicious. Braised just right tender. Wantan mee also is very good. Prawn noodles as well. Even the carrot cake , beef noodle is also top quality. Spoilt for choice. However the parking is so hard limited and no shelter walk way. You will get stuck if it's rain. Parking is really hard. Come 10 times 9.5 times no parking. However this place is highly recommended. It's the top 5 hawker centre in my list. These days have to come at super off peak timing but many shop will be rest hours.

Great little Hawker by Singapore River. Chicken was good, bowl of miso tasty and Char Siu Pork also very good. The Bok Choi in light soy excellent. Recommend Bandung drink. If it rains heavy no problem.

A relatively small food center.
2km from where we stay & we always jogged here have dinner & walk back .
The famous CKT is "so so" to us , with terrible long waiting time.Not worth the wait ! The wanton noodles is not bad surprisingly! BKT is ok too ! Prawn noodle at the corner has loyal supporters too !
Yet to try the rest ! Parking I heard is a headache  !

Lau Goh Teochew Chye Thow Kway is run by a half deaf and mute couple. There’s a picture on the counter, what you need to do is point the order you want to let the lady boss know. The price are $6, $8 and $10. There are three types which is black, white and yuanyang. I personally prefer the black one. The portion is quite filling.

It’s always so nice to see a young couple who is passionate about their food business 🥰 I’m glad they moved here to RV ~ came here for late lunch and I got the caesar salad, chicken chop with mash, and mushroom soup.

Chicken was juicy and flavourful and the mash was smooth and buttery! They make their slaw in-house so it’s really fresh.
Salad is fresh and crunchy, has sou vide chicken so it’s a nice addition of protein. The dash of parm is a good amount of umami added. They also make the salad dressing in-house!
Mushroom soup is just your regular, familiar tasting appetiser ✨ Theirs seems to not use milk in it so it’s not as rich and less cloying!

Please swing by and support them when you have the chance 💕

Ordered the chicken with pasta. The batter of the chicken chop tasted like there is some spice (not spicy) added into it, presumably the reason for the slightly darker look when served. Pasta was creamy. Coleslaw was good and the heavier kind, so don't try a whole serving by yourself unless you are a big eater. Meat and pasta portions were generous. Coffee is great from the coffee stall there also. Only gripe, place a bit hot in the afternoons.

Amazing restaurant-level western classics!
Chicken chop had a nice, crispy skin and the meat was really juicy. The cutlet was equally yummy and crispy 👍👍👍

Service was also very friendly ❤️ Also the diner is only ~5min walk from Fort Canning MRT:) Definitely highly recommended!

Had lunch at Uncle Lim's Diner and was pleasantly surprised—tasty food and great value. I had the chicken cutlet with mashed potato (would’ve loved some sauce/gravy), and the cutlet was juicy and not oily. The coleslaw tasted fresh, and the mash was smooth and buttery. I finished everything!! The menu is focused—chicken chop, chicken cutlet, and fish & chips with different variants. There was a lunchtime queue, but the wait was manageable. Overall, solid and affordable—I’ll be back to try the fish & chips.

Tasty food by Uncle Lim. 5 star!

Hands down one of the best Western food experiences I’ve had. I usually avoid coleslaw, but theirs was so good it completely changed my mind.

The burger was juicy, tasty, and satisfying, and the entire plate was well thought out with great balance and flavour. Definitely worth trying!

I bought Huraideu's 2 wings and 2 tenders meal for my sister and I and we thoroughly enjoyed it. First of all, I was quite happy the delivery came pretty quickly by Grab despite issues during the Circuit Breaker/Phase 1 period.

The chicken was juicy and with the yangneum sauce had a tinge of spiciness without being too hot. I might just try the super hot sauce next time as I like my food spicy.

I ordered from one of their outlets, RBL Burger and the first time, the food was incredible. I re-ordered with some guests via Grab raving about their Harvest Burger, and how it was realistic. After three bites, I was advised it tasted realistic because it was real chicken and not vegetarian! I contacted Grab who advised me that the kitchen was actually aware prior to delivery that the food was incorrect, but failed to advise us. I have attempted to contact them numerous times, and have been promised follow up, but this has never happened. Obviously a company that does not care about its clientele, but more importantly is negligent and careless with no remorse.
Ordered the Signature Rocket Salad from Rocket & Avo and was pleasantly surprised by how fresh the salad was, food looks good and portion was just right. Will definitely order it again!
I ordered the Classic Beef Lasagne from Publico Express and shared it with my wife. The beef lasagne looks good and it was delicious!
ordered the butter chicken set – chicken chunks were tender and the portion size was generous

Best place in Singapore for value-for-money, authentic Teochew cuisine. (This wasn’t my first visit.  So this review is way overdue.).

We ordered 4 dishes today: prawn with leek, radish fried kway teow, braised platter and bitter gourd fish head.  All were en point. But if I had to choose winners it’ll be the prawn with leek and radish fried kway teow.

In a one-line nutshell, this place is my comfort-food heaven.

First time visiting, and my first impression is that it is a very traditional, old‑school restaurant. The most impressive dish was the 菜脯棵条, which had very strong wok hei. Other standout dishes were the 潮州卤鸭, 清炒雍菜, and 菜脯蛋. The 肉松豆腐 and 啦啦 (上汤) were average. The Yam Paste with Gingko Nut, a must‑eat dessert in a Teochew restaurant, delivered as expected.

Service was good in that all the dishes arrived together, but the service crew lacked warmth and seemed rather indifferent. The owner at the payment counter was a lot friendlier.

Overall, more wows than misses.

The food here is legit authentic teo chew. Like actuslly got standards. Service here is also authentic (iykyk), so dont expect too much. You're here to eat and go.
Parking is terrible!! The parking lot beside the restaurant is tinyyy. My family and I were so grumpy at the parking situation cause we basically parked like 1km away and walked over under the sun. Kinda regretted coming here cause of this. But that was until the food came.. totally flipped a switch and we were impressed. For the price youre paying, they really serve quality teo chew cuisine. So everything else can be forgiven. The leather jacket with butter gourd was a big hit for us. Followed by the oyster omlet cause the oysters were plump and fresh.

I suggest you take a taxi over. Or drop off the elderly at the door while the driver goes to hunt for parking. 100% going to be coming back

The braised duck, chicken, egg and tofu were decent, though the five-spice flavour was slightly stronger than expected. The menu indicated pomfret, but the fish served was regular pomfret.
We checked with the shop and were informed that they only serve regular pomfret. As such, the price felt a little on the high side.

Had dinner at Sin Chao Garden with my girlfriends and it was a very satisfying meal. We ordered the pork ping pan, oyster orh jian, celery dish, and kway teow with chai poh — all were well cooked.
Overall, a good place for a casual dinner with friends, good local-style food, and a relaxed atmosphere.

Good good coffee!! They change around their beans every week so a reason to visit every week🤪

Small coffee place, limited seatings outdoor
Heard good reviews about the coffee and it doesn’t disappoint! Owner is welcoming and took time to explain the flavor notes. Will definitely come back again!

Great coffee stand run out of a home. Serving excellent espresso and filter from own-roasted beans.

If you like a good conversation as well, do drop by.

Have been there as a window customer and also had the opportunity to try their coffee experience on the weekend (you have to book that ahead of time) and loved both so much. I love that this is something that the owner is doing out of simple love for coffee and an appreciation for the little details that make drinking it better. A big fan.
Best coffee I’ve had recently!

place_name: L'Entrecôte Singapore River Valley, The Steak & Fries Bistro
review_text:
Booked myself a table and dined solo at this steak frites restaurant. Location is good and away from the usual touristy area. Well greeted and brought to my table. Order was easy and straight forward,  ordered their angus tenderloin in Bleu. Waiter was worried but I told him I take my steaks rare. Salad arrived and the steak was next. Service is fast and the angus tenderloin is how I wanted it and the sauce is very very close to the famous steak frites in Paris. So i had extra fries and it came with extra sauce.
Highly recommend to come here if you have cravings for  L'entrecôte steak and frites without catching a flight to Paris and waiting 2 hours in queue for it. Just come here its easy and cosy and the steaks won't disappoint.  Thank you Kai!

place_name: L'Entrecôte Singapore River Valley, The Steak & Fries Bistro
review_text: A quaint set up along Mohd Sultan Rd. Good variety of starters but only 2 choice of mains. The terrine of foie gras is rich and smooth, if only it could be enhanced with a bit infusion of alcohol. The accompanying relish also help to bring the dish together. I was advised by the staff to order my steak medium rather than my usual medium rare. And it was advice well heeded, as the meat was nicely medium rare. Had I ordered it medium rare, the meat would have been too rare for me. The meat was good except it was missing a bit of smokey/slightly charred flavour that I always enjoy in my steak. French fries were cooked perfectly. The walnut salad could do with a bit more of dressing though. Profiteroles were also well presented and drench generously in dark chocolate sauce, just that I like my choux skin doughy/soft and not too crunchy/hard. A very relaxed environment with French ballard in the background. The unisex single cubicle toilet could do with a bit of potpourri or air freshener as there is no extraction fan in this windowless space

place_name: L'Entrecôte Singapore River Valley, The Steak & Fries Bistro
review_text: Was craving for steak frites and this did not disappoint. The restaurant was quiet for a Sunday evening so it’s great if you want to avoid crowds on weekends. Their bread came toasted and fluffy, their foie gras was delicious!

place_name: L'Entrecôte Singapore River Valley, The Steak & Fries Bistro
review_text: Been to the other outlet and it was our first time trying the River Valley branch. And it didn’t disappoint. Had a lovely dinner with hubby to celebrate our wedding anniversary. Steak was awesome and the house red wine was equally excellent. Serving time was quick. Bread, butter, fries, olive oil and balsamic was free flow. As they focus only on one signature steak dish, I’d say it’s a safe bet coming here any given day for a consistently tasty, no nonsense steak.

place_name: L'Entrecôte Singapore River Valley, The Steak & Fries Bistro
review_text:
Ordered 2 entrées & a steak to share. I was in shocked of the quick service because they served everything practically at the same exact time. Salad, bread, soup, eggs and the steak all at the same time. It was not busy at the time.

Maybe I have the wrong impression of the restaurant’s standard. Duxton Hill outlet paced the meal with serving salad, bread, entrées & mains in stages. So kudos to the staff there.

French Onion Soup: Was warm. Tasted like beef stock out the box. Barely any taste of caramelised onions, more like boiled softened onions. Cheese was torched, “aburi” style instead of broiled.

Egg Mimosa: I can only describe it as weird and barely had any truffle taste.

Steak: Was the only thing that was good.

Overall, you cant miss with their signature steak.

We shared some bar bites with a non-alcoholic beer. The tofu hummus was well seasoned with the chili oil. As we scooped up the hummus using the wonton skin crisps, we couldn’t stop gobbling everything down! Also chicken cartilage was so well seasoned and not too hard. Good bites overall!

It's my second time here, and I felt I had to leave a review.

The beer selection is fantastic, with an amazing range of brews from Singapore's local breweries. The service is always excellent, especially from Josh and Ryan, both are friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable.

The kaya toast is a must-try. The kaya is slightly different from the usual Ya Kun or Toast Box style, and the contrast between the warm, crispy toast and the cold kaya is absolutely chef's kiss. The soft-boiled eggs were perfectly done too.

We also tried their night menu. The curry mussels were rich and flavourful, and the crispy chicken cartilage was the perfect snack to go with a cold beer.

The atmosphere is great too, especially at night when the lights are dimmer.

Great food, great beer, and even better service. I'll definitely be back again!

fantastic selection of beers / draft cocktails / wines, suuuper tasty food options (both mee tai maks, pork chop fried rice, chicken skewers, gai lan, tofu dip + chips 🤤), great service and recommendations. solid vibes, 10/10!
Came down, got myself the chicken skewers while I got some work done. It was pretty juicy and the sambal mayo was pretty addictive. Staff on shift were really funny and kind too. Enjoyed myself

Had their Hokkien Mee Tai Mak  - This fusion dish was pretty tasteless. The flavors felt entirely one-dimensional, and I couldn't catch any of the rich "gao" prawn stock promised on the menu. It ends up feeling neither here nor there—it completely lacks the robust punch you want from a fried Hokkien mee flavor, and it’s easily outshone by the cleaner, classic versions of Mee Tai Mak soup you can find at regular hawker stalls.

This place is awesome! The facilities are fantastic for art, and they serve really tasty food. We've been here a few times now, and every time we have a great time – the food is delicious, and the kids always love the art activities. There are different levels of difficulty you can choose for the rat maze, from really easy for kids to more challenging for adults. I definitely recommend checking it out!

InMagicNation by Art Werkx is a fantastic spot for creative fun, whether you’re visiting with children or spending time with friends. The crafting activities are engaging, easy to enjoy, and suitable for all ages.

The staff are wonderfully friendly and supportive, making the whole experience relaxed and enjoyable. The food is surprisingly good, which makes it easy to spend a few hours here without needing to go anywhere else.

A bright and welcoming place with great activities, tasty food, and genuinely warm service. Highly recommended for families, groups, and anyone who loves hands-on creativity.

Very good service. The barista is so good that he can make very nice artwork in coffee. Food was decently nice too! You can do some arty stuffs like punch needles, moziac coaster, candles, leather crafting! Nice to chill

Our second visit to Art Werkx, and it’s just as fun! A great spot to enjoy good food while getting creative. My daughter had waffles with a caramel milkshake, while I had a chunky chicken croissant with matcha latte—delicious as always.

We did mosaic art and lino block printing this time, and the activities were engaging and easy to follow. The space is cozy, and the staff is friendly. Perfect for a fun, hands-on experience. Highly recommend for families and art lovers!

This cozy and relaxing cafe not only serves delicious food and drinks but also offers a variety of creative activities, such as hand-poured candles, handmade soaps, mosaic trays, mosaic coasters, wooden name boards, leather crafting, painting, and more!

We had such an enjoyable time here, getting creative and indulging in their delicious food! We made our own coin pouch and beautifully decorated decoupage box, it was such a fun, hands-on experience.

place_name: Common Man Coffee Roasters - Martin Road
review_text:
I’ve been coming to Common Man Coffee Roasters since 2017 (whenever I’m in Singapore), which feels like a lifetime in café years.

What I appreciate most is that the coffee is still consistently solid, the food is as good as I remember, and the place still has that easygoing charm that makes you want to hang around a bit longer than planned.

So many places change, chase trends. Common Man has just quietly kept its quality over the years.

Always a good meal, always a good coffee and somehow it still feels like coming back to a familiar spot. ☕

place_name: Common Man Coffee Roasters - Martin Road
review_text:
We were recommended to visit this cafe and we were so impressed.
Best coffee and the food was perfect.
The service staff were so attentive and helpful.
One of us had mushrooms and avocado on toast which was so good and I had the French toast and it was so amazingly good.
We were provided a bag to take our leftovers.

place_name: Common Man Coffee Roasters - Martin Road
review_text:
Been to the Joo chiat common man roasters few times, but first time here at the original strikes different.
Food is really good no matter what you choose. Perfect for Sunday brunch as it's also pet friendly. It's pricey though and so is the parking here.

place_name: Common Man Coffee Roasters - Martin Road
review_text: I loved this brunch a lot!!! I came by myself on a busy Saturday morning but didn’t have to wait too long. The pancakes were amazing and so was the cappuccino! I will definitely be back next time I’m in Singapore—don’t miss this for coffee or brunch!

place_name: Common Man Coffee Roasters - Martin Road
review_text:
Wildly popular expat and digital nomad destination around river valley. Also popular for Singaporean ATAS families.

Coffee and juice were alright. Place seems very commercial and put together. One step up from the versions they supply to corporate clients.

Personally felt that the filter coffee wasn't as good as I'd hoped. For starters, they supplied it in that instagrammable flask and cup pictured below. As far as I can tell, there's no way to pour the coffee out without spilling a good proportion of it, around 10-15% out onto the table. Granted I'm not an expert in tableware.

Perhaps it were merely an off batch of beans. Chance befalls us all, sure. The juice came in a woefully tiny container. Sure, I get it, prices are high, Iran etc. but come on, it was barely larger than a yakult bottle. Not even vitagen!!

Going the commercial route is fine. And maybe quality suffered because it was crowded. Chye sent huat is a good example of keeping it up.

Anyway, perhaps I'm too harsh. The decor was nice, and the no touching sculpture at the door amused me.
A calm, low-key River Valley neighbourhood mall — more for groceries, cafés, fitness & everyday convenience than serious shopping.

Remember when the mall was a pet-friendly haven? We could bring our furry friends along and enjoy our breakfast / coffee together. But sadly, that’s not the case anymore, “No Pets allowed’s Sign” is everywhere. The mall’s business has taken a hit, and fewer people are visiting. Our“Dog’s Paradise” has vanished 🥲

** Starbucks has banned dogs from its outside seating area. They claim that the building management no longer permits dogs in the mall. However, I’m not sure how accurate this is.

It seems like everyone is boycotting Starbucks now, especially since their other outlets have been claiming to have obtained a halal licence. It’s quite ironic, considering that they’ve suddenly become dog-unfriendly.

A smaller and older shopping centre in the River Valley area. It houses 1 supermarket (Fairprice)
and 1 specialty shop cum restaurant (Little Farms), a few more eateries, a gym, a vet clinic, and a tuition centre.

Heirloom tomatoes are sold at Little Farms. There is the butcher stand.

No seats for shoppers to rest. The seats are all in the eateries which means, need to buy food or drinks if anyone needs to sit to rest or wait for people. It's not meant for tourists. This shopping centre best serve the local community.

If you come to little farm, it is feel ok to find the goods that you want. NTUC at Valley point, has nothing. You cannot find what you want. Finest so more expensive. Food wise only one affordable coffee shop and better q before 11:30 if not crowded. AU croissant is more affordable than Starbucks. Starbucks quality is drop, no quality control and I feel they are not maintain the hygiene standard.

Clarke Quay is one of the most energetic and colorful areas in Singapore, especially in the evening. The riverside setting, combined with the bright neon lights and lively atmosphere, makes it a fantastic place to walk around and enjoy the city at night.
There are plenty of restaurants, bars, and entertainment options, ranging from casual dining to upscale spots with beautiful waterfront views. The colorful canopy overhead adds a unique architectural touch and helps keep the area comfortable even in warmer weather.
Whether you're looking for a relaxed dinner by the river, live music, or just a scenic stroll along the Singapore River, Clarke Quay delivers. It’s clean, well organized, and feels safe — perfect for couples, friends, or even families earlier in the evening.
Highly recommended if you want to experience Singapore’s nightlife in a vibrant and picturesque setting.

I visited Clarke Quay during the day and found it to be a really charming place. It offers a wide variety of restaurants, making it a great spot to explore different dining options all in one area. The colorful buildings and riverside setting give it a very cute and vibrant atmosphere.

Although I went during the daytime, I can imagine it becomes even more lively and visually appealing at night, when all the venues are open and the lights reflect beautifully along the river. Definitely a place worth visiting, especially in the evening for the full experience.

Clarke Quay is a vibrant riverside destination that’s very popular with tourists, offering a huge selection of bars and restaurants for nightlife and dining. The atmosphere really picks up in the evening with the music and light. It is a great spot whether you're looking for light bites or a full night out.

As a local, I appreciate that it’s well maintained and clean. It is a good place for an evening stroll or leisurely walk. While it feels touristy and the prices are definitely on the higher side, it’s still a solid spot to soak in the city’s energy and enjoy a wide variety of food and drink in one location.

Clarke Quay is one of those places in Singapore that truly comes alive after sunset. Set along the river, the area buzzes with neon lights, upbeat music, and a constant flow of people soaking in the atmosphere. Lined with a wide mix of bars, pubs, clubs, and restaurants, it offers something for everyone—whether you’re in the mood for a relaxed drink by the water or a high-energy night out with loud beats and dancing crowds.

The riverside setting adds a charming touch, making evening walks especially enjoyable. You can stroll along the promenade, watch boats glide past, and take in the colorful reflections dancing on the water. There’s always something happening—live music spilling out of venues, street performers adding flair, and groups of friends laughing their way from one spot to another.

Yes, it does feel a little touristy, with busy crowds and popular chains, but that’s part of its appeal. The energy is infectious, the vibe is festive, and the options are endless. If you’re looking for nightlife, people-watching, and a fun place to unwind after dark, Clarke Quay delivers the experience with style and spirit
Nice area to wander under canopy, shelter from the sun, socialise, have light / western food. A great way of spending a few hours in the afternoon. Especially from the bars at the river edge.

The ambience was warm, cosy, and perfect for the season—especially with the Christmas carols playing softly in the background. It really added to the overall festive experience.

We tried the 300g Wagyu sirloin steak ($46.98), and it was absolutely worth it. The meat was tender, flavourful, and cooked beautifully. I especially loved the crispy garlic slices on top—they added the perfect touch. Truly delicious.

The truffle mushroom burger was decent, though not particularly memorable, but the sea salt fries that came with it were surprisingly good and stood out more than the burger itself.

The carbonara pasta was alright—creamy and comforting enough, though it could use a stronger flavour to make it more satisfying.

Overall, a lovely place with a great atmosphere and a couple of standout dishes. Would return again for the steak alone!

Big supermarket run by NTUC Finest at Clarke Quay. They are located around 2 mins from the Clarke Quay taxi stand, along River Valley Road.

They have a range of fresh produce, cooking essentials, meats, cheeses, frozen goods, alcohol and also tourist souvenirs.

What’s interesting is the food court where there is a variety of cuisines to choose from. A stall even allows you to buy the meats from the supermarket and they will prepare it for you to eat on the spot!

A bar in the supermarket also allow you to order drinks by the glass.

A real outstanding find! 💖💖💖

This grocery store is full of surprises, with an amazing collection of food stalls—including a charming pub-style corner serving fresh oysters. The selection of beers and wines is incredible, both in variety and price.

I tried the Italian section, ATavola, and had a fantastic pasta. I loved it so much that I came back the very next morning for breakfast.

Totally loved it. Totally recommended! 💎

What a find at Clarke Quay!
Tucked inside the FairPrice Finest, there's a surprisingly spacious and comfortable cafeteria area where you can order snacks and hot meals. What really sets it apart is the option to enjoy beer and wine at supermarket prices—just a small service fee added, making it significantly cheaper than your average bar or restaurant in the area.

It's a perfect spot to take a break, especially in the mid-afternoon when it's quieter.
Whether you're looking to cool down with a cold drink or have a casual bite, this place offers great value in a relaxed setting.
Highly recommended if you're in the area and want to unwind without the usual Clarke Quay price tag.
Nice place to chill and relax with friends. Huge selection of wine and cheese. For vegetarian, there are fries and vegetarian prata.
24hrs convenience store. From groceries to household items. Price is competitive compared to other big brands supermarket. Friendly staff & service is fast.😊
Small, convenience-store-type of shop. Some ice-cream, drinks, staples. Does not accept credit card.
Good store that works 24h, take cash with you since you won't be able to pay with visa/mastercard
Small supermarket / corner shop. Has lots of stuff.
Good varieties. Friendly counter. Good prices.
Luna is very convenient and is always well stocked, especially for Indian groceries. The owners and the workers are always very polite and helpful.
Convenient location for Indian groceries in River Valley.
Very helpfull and has all we needed, thank you

Dude just closed the shop with a "toilet-reason".
But he is inside the shop and just walking there with headphones, doing something, and completely ignoring customers.
When he saw me he was just shaking his hand and continued to do whatever he did.
It is an example of terrible treatment for the customers
Auntie Stella is a legend and the loveliest 7-Eleven person you will ever meet. Come here today and say hello and make her day!

I’ve been a regular customer at this store for over 10 years. I always appreciate the warm, friendly greetings from the staff whenever I walk in. The store is always clean and well-maintained, with a wide variety of products to choose from.
Thank you, Joey and team! 😊🙏🏻
Auntie Stella is the most helpful and friendly 7-11 employee in Singapore. Always goes out of her way to help customers and make them feel like regulars.
When asked about the 6-8 Nov promotion, the new middle aged female staff was not helpful at all and very argumentative

The food here is a little more expensive than other hawker centres, although I dont really consider Tiong Bahru a tourist spot. Loo's hainanese curry rice is wildly popular here and commands a long queue, although I dont feel that it is that great. Pig organ soup here is pretty clear and the innards are pretty well cooked. Overall, this is a pretty decent hawker centre, that can keep foodies engaged. I do feel that Grandpa chee cheong fun is another solid option if you are craving for porridge and chee cheong fun. Overall, I would say that this is an average hawker centre.

One of the best hawker centres in SG, hands down. Clean, spacious, and surprisingly airy so it’s actually comfortable to sit and eat. Best to come around 8-10am to enjoy all the local delicacies and breakfast menu.

Food options are solid across the board. You’ve got all the classics done well from chwee kueh to lor mee to chicken rice. Easy to just follow the queues and you won’t go wrong. Prices still very reasonable too.

Crowd is more local which gives it a nice authentic vibe. Only thing is many stalls close by mid afternoon, so come earlier.

Great spot for a proper hawker fix without the chaos. Parking available around as well but pretty limited.

An authentic heart of the neighborhood with a wonderful local vibe!
​We arrived by bus and headed straight to the second floor. The entire building is impressively clean and organized. Even the wet market downstairs on the first floor is a pleasure to walk through—full of fresh fruits, vegetables, and beautiful flowers. We visited during lunch, so be prepared to share tables with locals—it’s a great way to soak in the energy!
​Food Highlights: We shared a classic Hainanese Chicken Rice (amongst ourselves, not with the nice folks at the shared table) which was very well-made (the queue was a good sign!). For dessert, Liang Liang Garden was a total lifesaver. Their Cendol and Cheng Tng (Leng Chee Kang) were only $3 each and absolutely hit the spot on a hot day.
​The Vibe: Don't miss the local characters! We saw a Japanese artist uncle painting umbrellas accompanied by his very calm bulldog and two roosters with beautifully dyed feathers. It’s these unique sights that make this place so special.
​A must-visit for the food, the cleanliness, and the community feel.

The atmosphere at this market is incredibly vibrant and authentic, offering a wonderful blend of traditional charm and modern convenience. The facility is exceptionally well-maintained and organized, providing a clean and comfortable environment for visitors to explore. There is a fantastic variety of local food options that are both high in quality and reasonably priced. The energy of the space is lively yet refined, making it an ideal spot to experience the local culinary culture. Service across the various stalls is efficient and professional, ensuring a smooth experience even during busier hours. Overall, it is a standout destination that perfectly captures the heart of the community in a stylishly preserved setting.

On the recommendation of a long time local regarding what is the most locally favoured Hawker location in Singapore, he recommended Tiong Bahru. He was accurate!

We enjoyed looking at options before choosing Flavor Mala Hot Pot! While we were challenged to add the vegetables we desired, the food was delicious and fresh!

This Hawker Market on the second floor if this structure is well worth a visit!!

Im a young parents and i hearsay from others that the stall sell fresh chicken. But i dont agree as whn the uncle see that im young, he took the chicken from his fridge behind him and he told me its very fresh. And alot water was dripping due to the icey water. When i got home, the chicken was stuff with a big block of ice. I realise and check online that its to retain the freshness. If this is fresh , he need not put in so much ice. Well Uncle, u may sell cheap but very dishonest. No integrity.
Good service and affordable price.

A brand new SRT studio that is absolute vibes!  Everything feels thoughtfully designed - clean, aesthetic, and super welcoming. Major plus points for the shower facilities and having bathrooms within the space (it makes everything so convenient).

Every class starts with a clear introduction on the springs and what to expect, and can we talk about the consent-to-adjustment coasters…ooofff such a small but thoughtful touch!

This is coming from someone new to SRT.
Instructor wise:
Deborah is amazing for alignment and engagement cues, you feel every muscle working in the best way. Ouch!
Ian’s Grounded50 is a must-try if you’re looking for something strong and centered. (legs will cry if you’re following his beat)
YT will get you into your “sexy 11 abs mode” (or at least on your way there 😂).
And Brandon… best playlists, don’t expect a chill class because he will not let you take it easy (he is watching hahaha).
I’m looking forward to trying the other instructors too!!

Bonus tip: don’t forget to grab a coffee on them after class! 😉

First time attending Reformer Pilates Sculpt 50 class at XYCO’s new Pilates studio after all that I’ve heard about their close knit community and energetic spinning classes. Attended class taught by instructor John who was impressive, offered his full energy and attention during class and was very encouraging and clear with his instructions and cues. Conveniently located near Fort Canning MRT, the studio is brightly lit, clean and modern. Would love to be back again!

Beautiful and cosy Pilates studio located conveniently in the city centre. Amazing vibes and community 💯

Attended Brandon’s class and for a first timer doing Pilates, it was a great class and I would definitely attend again! +1
Beautiful new studio space with an amazing team. Especially love Ian’s class - he’s my fave instructor!

The staff are always friendly, welcoming, personable and accommodating, and go out of their way to assist their clients. I was feeling unexpectedly unwell today for instance and they made sure that I was alright, before during and after class. Both Xyco Spin and Reformer have great facilities as well! I especially appreciate the thought given to the space for Reformer, as it’s a great environment to even do work and just rest before or after class. For reformer classes I enjoy the pacing and the 2 types of classes they offer. After trying different SRT xformer classes, I find that Xyco’s style fits my preferences best. Both Grounded and Sculpted cater to whether you’re wanting to focus on form and/or slow things down a bit, or whether you want a more intense workout. Overall I am very appreciative of the staff and instructors’ warm and cordial nature which I believe is mostly what makes me fully enjoy each of my sessions there! I’m always excited to go back for another class each time, even if the burn hurts during class🥶 Thank you Xyco Team!! You guys are awesome🙆🏻‍♀️

I’ve been training with Stefan at Performance Gym for over 2 years and couldn’t recommend it more highly. With Stefan’s guidance, structure, and consistency, I’ve been able to hit my fitness goals. Stefan is knowledgeable, supportive, friendly and knows how to tailor each session to keep things challenging. The gym itself is well equipped and perfectly set up for personal training, making every session efficient and focused.

I’ve had a really great experience at Performance by Active Fitness. The gym has a strong community feel — everyone is genuinely friendly, supportive, and welcoming, which makes it easy to stay consistent and actually enjoy training here.

The trainers are great — I especially recommend Andre and Jason for their support and guidance.

Overall, it’s a well-equipped space with a positive, ego-free environment that makes working out something to look forward to. Highly recommend if you’re looking for a gym that feels more like a community than just a place to train.

Absolutely love this gym. I train with Tim for PT and he’s fantastic — incredibly patient, calm and steady, but still pushes you to work hard and get results. His no-fuss approach makes every session really enjoyable and effective.

The group classes are also excellent — small, well-run and very focused on each individual, so you get a lot out of every session.

Such a great environment to train in — highly recommend!

I have been attending weekly personal training sessions at this gym for close to two years now. When I first started, I was dealing with persistent wrist pain that had been bothering me for quite some time. I was not sure if strength training would help or even make things worse. To my amazement, after committing to a consistent training programme, my wrist pain completely disappeared. I honestly did not expect that outcome, and I am grateful for it.
Beyond the pain relief, my overall body strength has improved significantly. A huge part of this is thanks to my trainer, Stefan, who strikes the perfect balance — he is friendly and approachable, yet he ensures that every session is purposeful and challenging. He consistently pushes me to my limits while being mindful of proper form and injury prevention. I know I wouldn’t be able to achieve the positive results if I were on my own. This year even my husband was motivated enough to join me in the weekly sessions. The gym itself is well-equipped and has a warm, welcoming atmosphere, making it an excellent choice for residents in the neighbourhood.

I've been working out with Stefan for more than 12 months now and have achieved results I wasn't able to achieve without proper personal training. He is knowledgeable, encouraging and also pushes me just the right amount to continue to progressively overload with good form. I've also worked out with some of the other trainers and had a similar positive experience. The gym is in a great location and I love how much natural light gets in. It's a pleasure coming here to work out.

place_name: The Ice Bath Club - River Valley
review_text:
The space felt pretty cramped, as there was a running group that had just finished their run. After they left, I felt better.

Facility was good & I enjoyed the plunges & sauna. Electrolytes available as well!

Service was friendly & polite, although it could've been more attentive (perhaps dealing with the mass exodus of people that just left & they were busy washing water cups haha. We had to wait awhile for cup replenishment).

place_name: The Ice Bath Club - River Valley
review_text:
I’ve been coming to ice bath for a while now, but was super inconsistent. Maybe once every 2 weeks. But somehow, I found myself missing that feeling after a dip, this happiness and light feeling, almost magical, is something hard to describe with just words.

Have brought couple of friends here to try as well and they all experienced the same feeling and loved it! Am also a recent convert now to a member, can’t wait for more outlets in Singapore!

Also special mention to couple of staffs that made the experience even better here in the RV outlet. YY, Janelle, Regine, Darryl, Farhan. Always have a great conversation with them and their energy always makes my day somehow better.

place_name: The Ice Bath Club - River Valley
review_text: Amazing place for recovery after an intense workout session… Huge shoutout to the fabulous team here especially staff YY, she gave me such a great tour around the area. i love that she checked on me during my session to see if i’m doing okay (i’m REALLY afraid of the cold), which I really appreciated. Thanks dear! Will def come back again for some coffee, highly recommended!

place_name: The Ice Bath Club - River Valley
review_text:
This is the BEST sauna I have ever been to! I love how TIBC has everything setup from the webpage to the friendly staff walking you through everything you need to know and even the membership options they have. It's such a beautiful interior with clean facilities. I can't wait till I can become a member in Bangkok and use my membership whenever i visit Singapore or Hong Kong!
A special shout-out to Regine for very warm welcome with explaining everything I needed to know and talking me to when I was getting some electrolytes! She is the BEST and I can't wait to visit Singapore just to say hi to her!
I have to come back because I didn't try the cafe. So I see you guys very soon!
Much love,
Memphis

place_name: The Ice Bath Club - River Valley
review_text: Great location and friendly staff. Ice bath is great to recover  after work out! Appreciate the hot sauna as well

My experience from enquiry to lesson is very smooth. The academy is prompt at responding to my queries and coach Briyan is superb! My foundation improved and I am looking forward to lesson every week!
This swim coach has a great personality and makes the lessons enjoyable. I was able to improve my skills and have fun at the same time.
The swim coach was fantastic! They helped me build my confidence and I was able to improve my swim technique. Highly recommend!
The swimming coach was very patient and understanding. I felt comfortable and safe in the water and learned a lot. Great experience!
This swim coach has a great way of explaining things and making the lessons fun. I was able to learn at my own pace and feel comfortable in the water.

Been coming to Orchard Cineleisure since I was in Secondary School many years ago for movies. Glad there are still movies screened here, this time under Golden Village. Comfortable, clean, and usually not too crowded which is great. Staff are friendly and helpful too. Thank you! 🙏🏻

Hall 1 - small sized screen. Not too cold but still cool enough.
Seating not fully comfortable.
