Unobstructed view seating set up.
Audio effects are okay.
But the hall is not sound proof from neighbouring halls.

Visited today, 16 Jan at 1.25pm, Unfriendly staff. Was at Level 5 and saw a Male staff wearing white inside and the barrier was not close so went in and ask if GV has gold class. He was super unfriendly. Gave an unwelcome look and replied so unfriendly. Waiting for him to ask some old folks who also enter inside to leave as well. If he have the guts to do it. He is sitting behind this counter.

Awful popcorn. Ordered a large and received what most cinemas would call “a kids size.” Mostly crumbs too.
The seats were impressively uncomfortable, clearly designed by someone who has only heard rumours of the human spine. And the toilets were an absolute disgrace.

Overall, a properly immersive experience, assuming you're going to see a horror movie.
Hall 6 seats are plushy and comfy with good legroom. Air-conditioning is cold. An intimate setting in an artsy, cosy space.

Such a little jewel of a cinema in Singapore. It has a wonderfully quaint, retro charm that makes the whole experience feel special and nostalgic in the best way. In a time when so many cinemas feel generic, this place has real character and soul. The atmosphere is intimate, cosy, and full of old-school charm, which makes watching a film here feel far more memorable. Definitely the kind of hidden gem that deserves more love and support. A must-visit for anyone who appreciates unique independent cinemas. We really gotta support places like this!
Lovely cinema! Really love the retro aesthetic of the lobby. Glad that Filmhouse is carrying on The Projector's legacy of providing a space for alternative cinema!

I am glad that there's a place like Filmhouse in Singapore to appreciate and support quality movies for the movie-fans. The selection of movies that Filmhouse is choosing to screen seem to be artistic and award-winning ones - both from the past and newly released. This is definitely a place for movie fans to come from time to time. The seats are pretty small and uncomfortable, but good for the old-school vibes. Their toilets are really cool with many movie posters too.

Just one thing; if you are tall (like above 175cm), please do not take first half rows. You will be blocking the subtitles. Be considerate and sit at the back.
Great place, chill vibes, and friendly people. A bunch of cultured cinema goers’ heaven. Classics being screened every so often, definitely recommend.
Good movie theatre with nostalgic taste! And their popcorn is very nice!

Although I live "across the (Upper Bukit Timah) road" from this community club - meaning that my associated community club should be at Cashew Senja, this community club is my go-to for community activities and dining choices (there is a Malay cafe, a Korean restaurant, and a Domino's Pizza here) - for its proximity in the case of the former, and good food in the case of the latter. I also love the community club team here as they are very very friendly and very very helpful - in particular, a Ms Jaclyn Sim.

Nice community club in this premium location. Convenient to grab a bite or drink or have a proper sit down meal. It has the usual facilities in a community centre and still quite busy at 8pm night time though it is very quiet at the eateries.

Saw the interesting "castle" looking structure from a restaurant opposite and came over to check it out. Ended up discovering this heritage alley where we saw old telephone booth and relive and revived some retro feelings of life long past ...
v.clean ++++ CC common areas & toilets. reception was friendly and efficient with enquiries and purchase of tickets etc.

“Elizabeth Drive along Hillview Avenue is where what used to be Princess Elizabeth Estate, an old public housing estate built in 1951 to commemorate the wedding of Princes Elizabeth (later Queen Elizabeth II) to Prince Philip (Duke of Edinburgh).
The current Hillview Community Club is built on the grounds of this estate, where Princess Elizabeth Estate Community Center (CC) used to be…
This special connection to Queen Elizabeth lives on today at Hillview CC where a little gallery (Hillview Heritage Alley on the 4th floor of the Hillview CC) showcases the history of the estate and its vicinity.” - Ms Low Yen Ling, Member of Parliament, on Instagram

Generally speaking well maintained. We have regular game there.
However, I feed back to General Office about men's shower room water drainer not working properly last week. Need to fix it. The lady said noted and thanks.

Today the shower water still won't go down, it's very unhygienic. The dirty water over flow back to my feet.

Please take action immediately. Thanks
My vehicle was parked here from 1pm+ to 5pm+. Fees came to about $21+. Very expensive. Management should be a lot more forth coming about their carpark rates and to warn potential carpark users.
One rim in the basketball court is damaged.  Please attend and fix.  Thanks

Was there helping with the Hari Raya decorations, staff are friendly and helpful.
Together with MAEC chairman and his team we finished in 3 hours.

The basketball court at Cairnhill CC is located on level 3.

Relatively leveled, grippy, and clearly colored flooring is coupled with sturdy rims on both ends, though without nets.

Decent night lights on major corners.

Despite being on the smaller side, the court is overall clean and well maintained (no loose rims with screws falling out, unlike the basketball court in Tanglin CC).

Brought our nieces here and loved the place! Very clean and welcoming. We especially had a lot of fun at the sand pit. Raiin was the staff attending to us and her service is world class. Thank you so much!

Came on a Saturday morning and it was not too crowded even though there was a birthday party happening. Comfortable place, soothing colour scheme, plenty of spots for parents to sit while kids play. Our kids loved the hinoki beads area. There's a large "supermarket" and playkitchen, and even a camping ground "outside". Super fun!

The kids absolutely had a blast here! A lot of varieties: (wooden) sand pit, ball pit, slides, obstacle courses, carousel, musical instruments, kitchens and mini market shops..can even celebrate bday here! Highly recommended!!
Awesome playground! Facilities are clean and well kept. Staff are very friendly, like Maira Mai Hao Lin And Manu. The manager Raiin is also very nice. Recommend to all

I had an amazing time today! brought my 2 year old daughter and the place was perfect for her! the area was catered to younger kids which suited her perfectly. the staffs were are very friendly and welcoming too. Raiin and Mai was at the counter and their service was top notch. super welcoming~

This park is a lot of fun for kids of all ages. They have swings, wooden log obstacle courses, and big slides. It can get crowded, but it's worth it. Even older kids will find things to enjoy. The play areas are designed to be fun and engaging, with different activities to suit different interests. Kids can easily move from one activity to the next, and parents can relax and supervise in the green spaces and scenic spots. The park is a good choice for a family day out, offering something for everyone. You can create some nice memories with your kids here, and the family can have a good time together. The atmosphere is relaxed, and the scenery is nice. Overall, it's a great place to spend the day with the family. The kids will have fun, and you'll appreciate the park's design and amenities. It's a spot that's sure to leave you feeling satisfied after a day out with the family. With its variety of activities and relaxed atmosphere, this park is a great option for families.

🌿Jubilee Park is located at the western slope of Fort Canning Park.
🛝The place has been repurposed into an exciting sand-filled Playground for children with Slides that is built into the slopes of the hill terrain, Swings, Seesaw, log climb, etc, that children can have plenty of great fun outdoors.
Fun note:
🏆Jubilee Park used to be known as King George the Fifth Jubilee Park or King George V Park.
🎎It is name as such to mark the silver jubilee of King George the Fifth  and Queen Mary.
🏆There were a National Theatre, a River Valley Swimming Pool and a Van Cliff Aquarium.
These recreational facilities had all been demolished.

Really nice open space to relax and just take a break. The playground area is well designed and fun, and the views around the park are calm and peaceful. Good spot to just walk around or hang out for a bit.

2/20/2024 I and my husband come in and walk around this area, I think the location of this section is very convenient, close to the subway station MRT, there are many landscape and a variety of plants and flowers. It's a great place to walk, take photos, play with kids, Very good choice! I like the environment and enjoy it. But if visit on the night, you should bring some friends, not only you.

First time checking out this park. Beautiful spacious surroundings with a small public car park next to it.
A fun wooden obstacle course greets us as we walk toward thr park from the car park. Next to it there are a couple of inclusive swing (for the kids who are wheelchair bound) Nice!! And there are about another 5 swings just round the corner.
Further up, there are 5 different slides for the kids to play on. Spotted two sets of see-saw under a partially shaded area.
In case you run out of water, we spotted two re-filling stations around the park. There is even taps for the little ones to wash their hands and feet after playing on the sand.
And if u are hungry after all the chasing and playing, Tiong Bahru Bakery is just nearby. Follow the aromatic smell of bread & coffee. 😉
Note: Do come earlier in the morning as it can gets hot by 1030am.

I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

We had a great time at Fort Canning Park, especially at the children's playground area. The kids really enjoyed the swings and slides, and the playground was well designed for young children.
There was also a wash basin nearby, making it easy to clean up after playing. The facilities were convenient and family-friendly. The location is very close to Fort Canning MRT stop.
The park itself is spacious, green, and well maintained, offering a nice escape from the city. Unfortunately, we didn't have enough time to explore all areas of the park, so we definitely plan to return on our next visit to Singapore.
Overall, Fort Canning Park is a wonderful destination for families with children who want to enjoy outdoor play, nature, and a relaxing atmosphere. Highly recommended for a few hours of family fun

Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

Enjoyed a sunny but hot walk around the park to take in its history and also Raffles Garden. Some good views and lots of plants and trees to admire. The main draw for us though was the heritage centre which contains a fair bit of military history relating to Singapore.

this is definitely a nice place to stay as it is not only convenient, with mrt stations and bus stops nearby, but also has a beautiful design, with some smaller units and some larger ones, depending on which you choose!

Another of the “Old Is Gold” collection when it comes to a search for old apartments with large floor plates. Excellent security in screening for visitors. Well maintained large compound with upgraded lifts, air-conditioned lift lobbies and plenty of parking.

Terrible terrible security, rude to their core. Management is even worse — rude and heartless, and discriminatory. They make the living experience extremely unpleasant even though the residents are very kind and the community is good.
Condo is very nice and many utilities
Nice place which is relatively convenient with good amenities but it might be more suitable for familes as its build is quite old

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

If you love nature, I recommend you doing a hike here. It’s good for beginners and all the trails are colour coded for the difficulty level. Along the trails, there will be sheltered post for you to rest. You can easily spot some common dwellers such as snakes, monkeys and wild boar. If you are lucky enough, you may find some cool birds. I come here almost every week and spend 1-2hrs doing the hike.

Rifle Range Nature Park is a great extension to the Bukit Timah nature area, offering a mix of easy trails, lush rainforest scenery, and interesting glimpses into Singapore’s natural and historical heritage. The park is well connected, with clearly marked paths, boardwalks, and links to nearby nature reserves

One unexpected highlight was spotting a durian tree - and even seeing fallen durians on the ground, a reminder of how rich and diverse the forest ecosystem is. Along the trails, you’ll also find impressive giant trees, dense vegetation, quarry views, and remnants of the area’s past hidden among the greenery

I loved Rifle Range Nature Park — it’s very friendly for children and the elderly. The boardwalks are well-paved, anti-slip, and mostly barrier-free, so it’s easy for prams and seniors to walk comfortably.

The access is super convenient: just take Beauty World MRT Exit A, go to Level 4 of Beauty World Centre, and cross the overhead bridge that leads directly into the park. Highlights include the Quarry Wetland, Colugo Deck, and a fun Forest Exploration Trail for kids. Highly recommended for a relaxed family nature walk.

Nice new park with natural trails and paved paths. Has many unspoilt flora and fauna, saw several durian and starfruit trees along the paths. Not so easy to get to if depending on public transport. Toilets and carpark are available and some paths r equipped with CCTV! Not crowded on a weekday morning.

A nice trek away from the hustle and bustle of the city located in the Bukit Timah area of Singapore. Amidst a lush green rain forest with thriving ecosystems Easy to access and to reach on foot and public transport from the nearest MRT station called Beauty World - Downtown Line (Blue Coloured Line). An overhead bridge links one of the entrances of the park to the roof top Food Court (highest floor) of Beauty World Shopping Centre. Easy enough for amateur trekkers, family outings, a stroll in the park or a jog. There is a main building for meeting area, car drop off and pick up, giant ceiling fans to cool you down, chilled drinking water dispenser, vending machines, maps, loo and a park ranger on duty.  Good place to shelter if it rains There are some slopes on the way up to the cliff top of the old quarry with a stunning viewing Birds Eye area perched right at the top of the cliff. Not a long distance but short slightly steep slopes but totally worth the climb. Saw families with kids around the age of 5 and above at the top so it’s really not that challenging or difficult. Also Not as far as I expected. But do take note of your way back because if you take a wrong turn and end up on Rifle Range Road it’s more than a 1 hour walk to the nearest MRT station especially if you are already tired. However the paths are all well paved and safe. Do note the park closes at 7pm. You can also access this park from the Green Corridor.

About mid range level of difficulty. Lot of monkey though. Place is very peaceful and the lookout area is scenic. In awe. It’s connected to Rail Corridor as well, that one is pretty easy in difficulty level. It’s a literally straight road. Just need endurance for the distance.

Zen vibes in this garden. Don't remember it being done up quite like this when I'd visited in school. They've done a good job here.

Don't need to go to Bali to find that trademark Indonesian half gate. Am interested in finding out more about this type of architecture, and why it's done this way, perhaps there is some religious significance?

Someone chopped off the heads of about 1/2 the doves!

The way the pools mute ambient sound is very soothing as well.

Nestled within the lush greenery of Fort Canning Park, the Sang Nila Utama Garden is a tranquil retreat perfect for relaxation. This beautifully designed garden draws inspiration from the 14th-century palaces of Southeast Asian rulers, offering a serene and rejuvenating environment for visitors to unwind.

The garden features elegant landscaping, intricate Javanese split gates, and calming water features that create a peaceful ambiance. Surrounded by greenery, it feels like stepping into a different era, far removed from the hustle and bustle of the city.

Whether you're looking for a quiet spot to reflect, a place to connect with nature, or simply a relaxing break during your walk in Fort Canning Park, the Sang Nila Utama Garden is an ideal choice. Its calming atmosphere makes it a great spot to recharge your mind and body.

With its historical significance and serene beauty, this garden is a hidden gem that combines culture, nature, and peace in one perfect location. It’s truly a rejuvenating escape worth exploring for anyone visiting Fort Canning Park.

🌿Sang Nila Utama Garden at Fort Canning Park takes on a Javanese style landscape.
⛲The Garden features symmetrical layout, reflective ponds and Javanese style split gates that marks the entrance into a peaceful and tranquil space.
🪻🥀The entire Sang Nila Utama Garden is landscaped with beautiful fragrant ornamental flowering plants that emits a subtle fragrance as you walk pass them.
🪔Sang Nila Utama was an ancient king of Singapore.

Hidden within Fort Canning Park, the Sang Nila Utama Garden is a truly peaceful and tranquil hidden gem. Not many tourists here, making it ideal for a quiet appreciation of nature and history.

This meticulously recreated 14th-century Royal Garden honors Singapore's founder, Sang Nila Utama, marking the site of the first king's palace. You'll notice distinctive Javanese split gates and symmetrical layouts, reminiscent of ancient Balinese temples. Charming statues of frogs, fish, and ducks add to the authentic feel.

The garden's unique architecture and lush greenery make it incredibly "Instagram-worthy." Its unparalleled serenity is a major draw; during my visit, it was blissfully quiet.

If you're in the Fort Canning area, definitely stop by the Sang Nila Utama Garden. It's a refreshing retreat and a beautiful step back in time.
Is a remarkable ancient architecture and structure who show how ancient kingdom once stood on this land. Although it is just part of the garden but can already feel the ancient harmony atmosphere.

It's small, but green and provides shade. A nice area to walk around in connection with the much longer Singapore River walk. Full with early morning and late evening joggers. Unfortunately the bicycle riders are not  showing any respect for people who walk. They ride much to fast.

Just a small park nestled within the park connector network, offering beautiful views of the Singapore River promenade.
Along the way, you’ll spot several old historical bridges that add charm to the walk.
The park also opens up to a vast skyline featuring Singapore’s commercial hub and affluent residential districts — making it a scenic and relaxing spot to visit.

Beautiful. One of the best parks in Singapore. There are many joggers and cyclists. Nearby residents like to bring their dogs for a walk too.

I urge NParks to maintain it and do not allow the upcoming condo developments ruin it. We need a lot of clean and green space for a healthy environment.

Smallish park in between the Alexandra Park Connector and Robertson Quay. You can have a splendid and scenic view of Jiak Kim Bridge, Singapore River, Robertson Quay and the surrounding condos, hotels and architecture from the park. Nice ambience, not crowded

Great place to walk along the singapore river and see otters. You should be able to meet them every afternoon. I usually have lunch in Great World Mall and walk over there. I sometimes walk from Clarke Quay to Great World along the Singapore river which can spend you half hour or 45 mins.
A much cleaner park than I remembered it was 20 years back. It’s really windy till I developed a running nose in the mid afternoon. Great place to chill as not so crowded like east coast park.
Quite nice place. Just the beach needs a bit of cleaning. Saw people fishing and wind surfing with sails. And even a bird catch fish. Not to mention airplanes taking off from Changi airport.

A nice place to be quiet, reflect, and be with loved ones, bustling with children and people cycling. There are also people fishing, selling things, everything is orderly and nice, not far from Changi Village when I was here, ill come back here in the future.

A 7am visit to Changi Beach Park offers a perfectly serene and quiet stroll, feeling like a well-kept secret from the bustling city. As the sun begins to rise, casting a soft, golden glow over the sand and sea, the park is enveloped in a peaceful silence. The only early birds are the dedicated park cleaners, whose quiet work in the morning calm adds to the tranquil atmosphere rather than disturbs it.

The true magic, however, is the unique soundscape. The tranquility is gently broken every few minutes by the deep, distant rumble of aircraft—a powerful yet strangely soothing reminder of the world in motion as planes make their final descent into the nearby Changi International Airport. It’s a captivating contrast that makes this simple morning walk feel both secluded and connected to something larger.

My family's favourite beach. Lovely sun, sand, sea and sometimes jellyfish. Lol..
Nice long stretch of beach to the roundabout point for that Instagramable picture with the finger.

Toilet amenities are OK. If you're hungry for bites, changi village hawker centre is only 10minutes walk away. Parking on weekends can be very limited due to the trending wingfoiling sport.

And oh.. not supposed to tie anything to trees now. No hammocks unless self standing.

Staying 4 night from 17 - 21 Dec 2021, Room 1403.
They will not make up your room until you begging or shouting.

Even I request to reception, lobby and general manager so many times, they never listen.

Refer to Manager Paul, who used to be in reception, they have some priority policy to select the room to clean.
Very expensive deal on food and drinks
Nice ambience waterfront hotel
Nice place
Nice place to shop and relax..
Wonderful walking path,  can walk for many kilometres both directions and on both sides of river. Clean and wide walking path. A must if you are a walker or runner
The Alexandra Park Connector along Zion Road is a peaceful, well-connected urban path, perfect for a stroll after a hearty meal at Zion Riverside Food Centre.”
Very nice PCN for a morning walk
A beautiful green trail for walking, running, cycling, etc. Evening times are the best time to spend here.
otter view point

Our child has absolutely blossomed at Little Village. Their teachers are gentle and wonderful, the school is gorgeous with plenty of outdoor space and a curriculum that is focussed on hands-on learning and fostering independence. Their bilingual programme is also really thoughtful and well-engineered. Recommend this school very very highly.
my kid loves going to school, that tells a lot: great environment, great staff and the teachers are very attentive. The 3 big outdoor playgrounds are what theyl toddlersneed for this age

Both my kids go to Little Village and we absolutely love it. It makes school such a happy place - they learn so much through play and they are always happy going to school!

1) Child-led inquiry and play based approach in a nurturing and positive environment.
2) Genunine, encouraging and thoughtful teachers and staff.
3) Amazing outdoor spaces, classroom design and natural materials (probably the best outdoor space you will find in the area)
4) Great communications with parents by the teachers and the office staff.
5) Kids come from diverse and international backgrounds
6) Most important of all - happy kids who are curious and love learning. They always have a beaming smile when they tell me about the things they’ve done in school.

We cannot be happier! Th school waitlist is very long so make sure to get on the waitlist early (it’s totally worth it!). If the policy is the same as when we started, I think you cannot sign up for the waitlist unless you’ve attended an in-personal school tour. But that could have changed so best to check directly with the school.

Thanks Little Village for being the first school experience for our kids. It’s such an important first experience and we cannot be happier that it is with Little Village!

Was invited to attend an event today and am very impressed by the friendliness and patience of all the teachers.  They really took a lot of effort to set up each individual zones so that the children and parents can have a fun and engaging time together.  Can see that the children attending Little Village are taught by a group of professionals.  Well done and keep up the good work.

My child has attended here for two years now and we are about to send our second child. We have been super happy with the loving and caring environment created by little village, the kindness of the teachers and the small community feel they manage to create. The outdoor space is amazing and the children are having so much fun!

Both our daughter (a few years ago) and now our son have attended The SchoolHouse (RV). The teaching staffs are truly dedicated. They take the time to understand each child’s unique personality and learning style, creating a supportive environment where kids feel safe and encouraged to grow confidently. Also,  the principal (Ms Lauren)is very approachable and clearly cares about the students and families. The admin manager (Ms Jean)is always friendly and helpful, making communication and daily routines smooth and easy.
Kudos to all Teachers in The Schoolhouse RV !

Have you been to school & feel happy every day? This branch of the Schoolhouse is one such school. My daughter has been attending the schoolhouse @ River Valley since she was 3 years old.  She's a happy baby since,  She's 6 now.

They really put into thoughts on how children are developed at different learning stages.  E.g. the deco of this school is based on warm neutral colours. Learning materials are in rainbow colours.  It allows the child to focus better in listening & observing.

The emphasis of " joy in learning " is strong here.  Where children knows & build their confidence too.

The principal & staffs are warm & friendly... it makes the children happy going there.

If I were to choose again, I would choose the schoolhouse again.

Thank you Ms Lauren, Ms Jean & all the teachers & staff for making my daughter the journey of preparation to primary school a great one... Great memories & great learning,  great fun! 👍

My daughter has been with Pats Schoolhouse since she was 18 months old. It has been easy to communicate with the teachers and staff since day one. They are attentive, caring, and always take the time to understand each child’s needs. I truly appreciate the open communication and the sense of trust they have built with us as parents. Seeing my daughter grow in confidence, independence, and joy over the years has been very reassuring, and we are grateful to be part of such a nurturing school environment.

Before sending my elder son for pre-school, I’ve visited more than 10 different schools around my area and nothing beats Pat’s Schoolhouse River Valley. Mainly because of the teachers and staff. I feel that they play such an integral role in the kids’ learning journey, the shaping of their behaviour and social interaction. Most teachers have retained for over the past few years which enhances consistency and the principal Ms Lauren has been so involving with the teachers and parents, was a proven fact that the entire school environment is a great place to belong. Ms Jean who is such a motherly figure ever since day 1 brings so much of warmth and comfort to the kids - one whom they always look forward to seeing every day. Both my kids are now enrolled at Pat’s Schoolhouse River Valley and they’re incredible happy. Kids are happy, I am happy too :)

We sent our kid there for 3 months and moved on. Few reasons:
1) student-teacher ratio is relatively high, that leads to the second issue
2) teachers can't take care of kids with a different learning progress, such as international students
3) comparing our experience attending different kindergartens in different counties, some staff may not be professional enough to deal with and handle young kids' emotion, physiological needs and different stage of growth.
Our summary is that if your kids are international students, we doubt if this would be the right school to take care of your needs.
Old and dated condo complex. Nice spacious units but look old from inside. Needs a complete overhaul. Facilities are good and close access to Great World City and public transportation
Nice fish, clear water
My friend lives hereew:D
old development but well kept,  nice facilities, with good apartment space, suitable for family, near Orchard shopping mall.

Unfortunately extremely noisy neighbours, on daily basis
Swimming pool flood light glaring and doesn’t turn off even after closing hour.

Nice place away from car noise
Love the view from the balcony...
Tranquility in Town
Beautiful condo near Orchard
Nice
Classic

-1 star as heelys are not accepted within the school compound. However, it is a fantastic school as there are many Black-Naped Orioles that come visit us. Principal Andrew Tan and I even share the same name!
a bit out of the way to travel to- 10 mins walk from bright hill so it’s adviced to go bishan then take bus. nice sch with a hill but a lot of construction

In 2016, the general public of Singapore were far from impressed, after the announcement about Singapore's newest junior college, Eunoia Junior College. Jokes and criticism surfaced online. "Pretentious" was among the kinder adjectives used in online reaction to the news, with many saying the name was hard to pronounce and lacked local heritage.

Explaining the naming process, Mrs Wong Mei Heng, principal of Eunoia Junior College, said parents had been invited to submit suggestions online, while students were asked for their input. There were brain-storming sessions to discuss each of the 200 proposed names, all of which were meticulously documented and systematically reviewed.

The Eunoia team met students later again to share their findings after six months of discussion. Only one name that "best captures the collective aspirations of our stakeholders" could be chosen, she said. While it could have been challenging to pronounce, "we were captivated by the enduring meaning the name presented us with".

Language experts have differing views on how the word should be pronounced, Mrs Wong said, adding that the college has decided that it should be "yoo-noh-iea".

"At Eunoia... our aim is not to be seen as high class, but to be known for delivering first-class education," she wrote in a letter, addressing claims that the name was pretentious.

Apparently, Eunoia Junior College (EJC) was set up because there was no suitable junior college at the time to partner three secondary schools, Catholic High, CHIJ St Nicholas Girls’ and Singapore Chinese Girls’ School, for the Integrated Programme ie: Integrated Programme students proceed to JC without taking the GCE ‘O’ Level exams and "have a broader curriculum". The 2010 decision was made even though the Government was aware that cohort sizes would fall, said Senior Minister of State for Education Janil Puthucheary on 3 July 2017.

12 storey tall JC.  Good facilities as it's still relatively new. Convenient now with Bright Hill MRT down the road and also accessible by buses. Personally from my own experience at the 2025 JC, I feel a bit alienated and I don't feel the warmth that I felt at another JC.

I went there for the open house in Jan 2020. The building was so brand new and it really looked stylish and modern. I think the library is the most iconic part of the building- it is located at level 9 and it has long, unbroken bench along its corridor overlooking the Bishan Park! So the students could sit there in groups (the bench is broad enough) to discuss work while enjoying the lush greenery. I wish I could go back to being student and study at this school. The students must be so proud!

Very disappointing experience with the condo management. They are neither responsive nor accountable, which is quite frustrating given the rental we are paying. For this price point, one would expect a much higher standard of service and professionalism.

If the intention is to increase rent, then so be it — but it should at least come with better management. Perhaps it will also help ensure that only residents who respect the community standards stay here.
It is a high rise building, we are presently staying.  Great world Mall is nearby. Bus stop and metro station very near. So all items are available nearby. A good place in Singapore.
Lots of good places around, metro station is nearby..

When I put Leonie condotel, show me the next rd.  Not 2 Leonie Hill rd.
When I put 2 Leonie Hill Rd.  Then show me right way.  The one of road can’ make right turn.  It’s a big deal and map always show me a long and wrong.  You should change the “ Leonie Condotel” on the map.
Gorgeous apartments but overall deserted and not maintained well.

place_name: The Eton Academy by EtonHouse (Great World)
review_text: My children enjoy their English classes at TEA. The teachers are warm, encouraging, and make learning fun. I also appreciate that they regularly keep me updated on what the children have been doing in class and always share their progress in a positive and encouraging way.

place_name: The Eton Academy by EtonHouse (Great World)
review_text: Indeed, the Eton Academy is the best enrichment we’ve ever experienced. I first signed my son up for N2 English when they first opened in Great world (great  location by the way)and subsequently k1 and k2 math. Teachers are very engaging and my son loves all the lessons. He was able to pick up a lot of useful skills and learning concepts. Lastly what we really appreciate as parents was the feedback from the teacher after each lesson. I always tell my friends  that I get more feedback on my child in one lesson than a whole term in preschool !

place_name: The Eton Academy by EtonHouse (Great World)
review_text: My 2 primary school children go to The Eton for 2 years now. We tried many other tuitions, but we just love this place. Teachers pay close attention to children and give us honest feedbacks. They have highly skilled teachers and we’ve been very happy about it. The service is also great. They respond any inquiries immediately and I love how they welcome us. I’d definitely recommend this tuition!! A++++

place_name: The Eton Academy by EtonHouse (Great World)
review_text:
The staff at Great World Eton are very helpful and the teachers are very passionate and dedicated. My 2 boys look forward to their classes every week. Kudos to Eton Academy.

Special thanks and shoutout to Teacher Yi Ting, who always go the extra mile for my children. A dedicated educator, who definitely cares more than just the academic results for the kids, but embraces the spirit of life long learning. Thanks so much!

place_name: The Eton Academy by EtonHouse (Great World)
review_text:
I’ve recently sent my children to The Eton Academy’s Holiday Camps. My 3-year-old son is inquisitive and loves science, so I enrolled him in the Young Scientist class where he learned about The Human Body. In class, he learned about his 5 senses, explored colours (sense of sight) through painting, created more colours through mixing, crafted a mini ukulele, and formed a mini band with his classmates (sense of sound).

At the end of the class, he excitedly showed us the art piece he had painted and asked us to smell it. We were curious at first, but his teacher shared that they added essential oils to the paint to incorporate their sense of smell in the activity. How brilliant!

At the end of each lesson, his teacher gives us a rundown of what they’ve learned and how Kenzo performed at each activity. I have a WhatsApp video of their impromptu band performance in class too. These are classes that make learning fun and interesting for children.

Came across this lovely community library while doing a shoot and fell in love with the vibe. The vast collection of books across so many genres, the cosy atmosphere, and how neat and welcoming the space is — it’s truly special.

The residents have clearly put so much care into maintaining it. Even more amazed after learning about the history behind it from Kae Chee.

Please continue preserving this space. It’s more than just a library — it’s a home!

What an amazing experience at this beautiful community library setup by the local residences  ( wo any govt funding or subsidies ).    Today they organised a free lunch session to enable more socialising opportunities for the aging & needy communities living  @HollandV HDB residences.  Keep up the good work , it’s fantastic 🤩🤩🤩

A hidden treasure gem in the neighborhood of Holland Village. A small, quiet, and charming library, filled with a wide variety of books for all ages, with friendly neighbors keeping the community kampong spirit alive.

This is how HDB void decks could be put to good use: a public area, made meaningful and purposeful by the actual residents, for the community.

Definitely worth dropping by when you are in the neighborhood to see if you can find an old book from your childhood or check out the small collection of random manga in the corner.

Or just drop by and chill with the community cat.

A very homey and clean community library that caters to children, teens, adults and seniors! It was my first time visiting this library after seeing their IG posts. There are many books for all ages and genres to look out for, even book series. The residents and host are very friendly and showed me around the library. You can tell how much effort and care the residents put into keeping the space tidy and comfortable. The kampung spirit is very evident in this estate with regular ad-hoc events happening. Would visit again to see the array of books available!😆😆

I walked past Holland Village Little Library without realizing it

It is and still is a wonderful little corner under a HDB void deck

The residents have set it up themselves despite opposition from Officials SG bodies

It is a ground up initiative where residents gathered to chit chat instead of being cooped up in their urban cement apartments all days.

The place is well kept and breezy

There are library of books available.

There are also food rescue programs, plus gardening too

Gardening helps the human to connect with nature

A community space helps residents to connect with each other and cultivate social grace and a place of cohesion.

It is truly a wonderful place

it was my favourite place to come to for the curry rice n bak chor mee. the bak chor mee remains on point but the beach road curry rice is disappointing. the meat is pathetically thin, and just that few pieces…vegetables..little n a pcs of eggs. The uncle who ordered before was complaining that the ingredients are so little, well i guess…u pay for the location. Never for the food. Convenient for tourists but i would rather the tourists heading to the actual hawker or even going LPS is better.

A small yet cozy food court at ION Orchard. It has stalls such as King Fried Rice, Chef Wei Cheong Fun, Pang Hakka Yong Tau Fu, Beach rd Cury rice & Eunos Bak Chor mee. I've tried the bak chor mee. The mee pok noodles were springy, covered with tangy sauce. Top up with minced meat & crispy pork lard. The soup was super delicious & very tasty 😋. It has the special umami & had generous portion of minced meat.

Review of Hawkers’ Street @ ION Orchard

Hawkers’ Street was added about five years after ION Orchard first opened, and its inclusion fits perfectly with the mall’s premium yet accessible image. I really appreciate that they’ve brought in several Michelin-recognized hawker brands, offering well-loved local flavours in a clean, central, and comfortable setting.

Here are my thoughts on the food we tried:

1. King of Fried Rice 4.5 out of 5
Their fried rice is truly impressive — I’d say it’s comparable to Din Tai Fung’s standard. The use of Japanese pearl rice gives it a premium texture and fragrance, and the option to pair it with either a chicken chop or pork chop adds a satisfying heartiness. The dried chilli on the side also gives the dish an extra punch of flavour that enhances the overall experience.

2. HK Cheong Fun 2.0 out of 5
The rice rolls had the taste and texture I was hoping for, though the presentation was a little messy. I ordered the scallop rice roll ($6.80), but the filling tasted more like fish cake — I couldn’t detect any real scallops at all.

Unfortunately, the service experience here left much to be desired. When I asked for some chilli, the staff — who appeared disinterested and rather unprofessional — told me curtly to “take only what I needed.” The chilli pot was nearly empty and difficult to scoop from, which made the interaction unnecessarily unpleasant. It’s disappointing, as the food had potential, but the poor attitude of the staff dampened the overall impression.

The proportion of ingredient to rice is really sad. You can count the number of beancurd cubes. The ikan bills topping fits in the spoon. When I feedback that’s really little ingredients, the server said she’s the most generous server already….$7.60 for this bowl of Hakka lei cha fan. Taste wise it’s ok.

Good variety of tasty food and we were able to find seats during dinner time on weekday easily. What stood out was staff level of service as I misplaced an item and called your office to check. Cynthia from your team  was very helpful, she helped  investigate and follow up. In the end I didn’t lose the item at Hawker Street. However, I am very impressed with the strong customer service both at your office as well as at the food court at Ion. I will definitely come back again and again! Thank you for taking great care of your customers!
This food court is located at the top floor of Kent Ridge MRT Station. Environment is clean and spacious. Teh was good. However, food were expensive for the average taste and quality.

Not the best food quality in my experience hit and miss in some store.  The BCM store is it a hybrid I never seen a fried meatball in the noodles and the taste of the seasoning not good enough  Cai fan probably the best most friendly keep asking everybody to come because they got a lot of variety the rest of the store not much to offer. Limited hala meal. Some store can’t tell if it’s hala base on offering

The bak chor mee from the stall Noodle Master was not bad but it’s too spicy. The stall operator didn’t bother to ask if we needed it to be spicy, tomato, or plain. Looks like they lack experience. It’s also not a traditional bak chor mee as they added two fried meat balls. The meatballs were nice but one of them was cold inside. The noodles was cooked to perfect texture and that’s a plus point!

Often eat at this food court when go to NUH. Food surprisingly is very good. Hv tried the basil pork rice @Viet Meet Thai stall, curry charsiew noodle @Curry Chicken stall; minced pork noodle @Ah Hwa Bak Chor Mee; mix vegetable; etc.. Very recomended especially the curry noodle & porkball noodle

Clean environment but it’s quite warm. Food is not bad. I usually order Fishball noodles, western, Thai food. Priced fairly amongst other food places nearby. Lunch time during weekdays can be super packed and hard to find seats.
The indian food is really good at affordable price and friendly staffs!
The Indian food stall is quite good. Hot drinks from the main drink stall needs improvement.
Home made kind of food. Good option for vegetarians.
The whole place is halal, even the chinese food. Can easily find a place to sit even though it is peak lunch time.
All food are halal. Area is air conditioned, the most popular stall would be the indian stall. The gram masala tea is really good

Newly opened Grounds on a Hill at Sunset Way is a clean, minimal, coffee-first space. Bright and airy inside, with the smell of fresh coffee carrying through the room. Outdoor seating is breezy and comfortable, anchored by solid wood block benches. Menu is simple with items like sourdough bread, which I didn't sample this visit.

Both white and black coffees were excellent. The house blend leans fruity with gentle berry notes, well-balanced and not overly acidic.

this was my second time at grounds, I had a lovely chat over carefully made coffee, with smiles and a warm welcome by the team. I learnt that this was a family run business, with beans roasted fresh themselves.

this abode in sunset way provides that human touch we need, and a place to really rest and escape for a little!

Very chill cafe that is located further inside from the main road (though the area is relatively small anyways) - but very well worth the walk there as they do serve up great coffee in a cool minimalistic environment!

☕ another coffee that hits the benchmark, the plum flavour really did follow through in my iced white, and similarly for the hot drink as well - not the best of the best but that’s my benchmark for a 5 stars coffee, being able to taste the aroma from my drink

🍞 the sourdough was alright, pretty pricey for the portion though it was a decent snack to be had

Might return to try their tarts in future, and also their other beans too!

Excellent coffee in a very well-designed coffee space, that is dog friendly. It’s one of my favourite places to bring people to. Thrilled to see adventurous coffee businesses bringing specialty coffee to the heartlands and neighbourhoods. Great enterprise by Rio and his daughters Paris and Sydney.

So pleased to have found this coffee shop, a real treasure in the most unassuming of locations.

The philosophy is simple; fantastic coffee at affordable prices. The family that own and run this place have an endearing love of the coffee they make, and they keep it simple - two varieties of filter coffee (the Peruvian Geisha is phenomenal!) and two varieties of espresso, all roasted to suit. You can see the passion that goes into each cup that is carefully & beautifully crafted. A simple but quality food menu as well and that’s it!

There is relaxed indoor and outdoor seating and I all-too-easily managed to pass a couple of hours there with a good book. If you’re in Singapore and you love speciality coffee, don’t miss it!

perfect WFC cafeee~
strong wifi and cozy ambience

Set meal for $20 (main + coffee) - very worth it and various choices!
💎Teriyaki Chicken Sando
buttery perfect toasted soft bread with special mayo and tender chicken thigh
💎 Flat White
decent coffee that's pretty strong and not too milky.

Food was really awesome — absolutely loved the acai bowl.
Drink: 7-Spice Chai Latte — woooo, really wow! The taste was soooo good ☕🔥

Thanks, Jack 👊🏼👏🏻 for the all drinks.
Special thanks to Mandeep Singh — you really bring such a great vibe and create an amazing atmosphere.
Great delicious brunch items. We decided to try out the place after seeing that they were eligible for grab's dine-out offer at the time. Waffles are fluffy and crispy. Good stuff.

Something wrong with the rosti. It's the most popular order according to the menu, but i regretted not to trust the latest google review about it. This is expensive rosti that taste like trash. The oil taste like cheap canola oil. I will not come back ever again. Service and atmosphere are okay.

Had a lovely meal at Prairie. The food was fresh and well presented — the prawn pasta was flavorful, the chicken sandwich juicy with a nice sauce, and the avocado toast with scrambled eggs was creamy and delicious.
The coffee was smooth and aromatic too. Cozy atmosphere and friendly service

A decently-stoked neighbourhood FairPrice but please be extra cautious and check fresh produce before purchasing. We have bought rotten and mouldy pre-packed fruit twice (separate incidents) in a week. The staff are very friendly and welcoming; there was no issue returning the rotten items and getting a refund, but there was hassle on our end to go again. The staff at this outlet is their greatest asset - they are genuinely so nice and helpful.

Staff are always friendly and patient. Always willing to help.
Recently, focus is on using the self check out stations which needs some getting used to.... especially with discount cards. It is good that the updated Fairprice apps now have time merdeka status and CHAS status incorporated  into it. The staff was helpful to show us that.

Why you nvr check age of ppl buying alcohol drinks?? You sold alcohol to my kid!! Didn't get receipt cannot exchange. Staff are polite but they're bots. Always ask if you need a bag but don't see your bag when you show them. Ask for member card when you already scanned it. These staff seem not to mind if they're replaced by AI bots.
After the renovation, it seems like many goods that i used to buy is no longer there. And the fresh vegetable section seems smaller now. I asked the cashiers and seems like im not alone.

I don’t usually leave reviews for supermarket outlets but this is outrageous and disgusting. This incident occurred around 5pm on 18 Jan 2026.

I understand that customers may pick and choose loose fruits. But why is it that people are allowed to open up boxes of mandarin oranges, open up individually packed oranges and pick and choose which they want? And put those unwanted oranges into another box to return to the shelves. As I was about to point this out to the staff, I saw the customer hand off the unwanted oranges to a member of staff, who helped to repack them into another box.

What is going on? What is next? Letting customers pick and choose which eggs they want, or choosing individual strawberries?

Disgusting practice that someone needs to look into.

place_name: Little Farms Market, Valley Point (Grocery store)
review_text:
We had dinner at Little Farm on the eve of Valentine’s Day. The food tasted great — not too oily and not overly salty. The chicken portion was generous and satisfying. We especially loved the chocolate lava cake — it was absolutely delicious!

One thing to note: the coffee bar closes at 8pm. We had planned to order coffee to go with our cake but weren’t able to. Overall, the customer service was good, and we had a pleasant dining experience.

place_name: Little Farms Market, Valley Point (Grocery store)
review_text:
Little Farms Cafe at Valley point has truly become part of my daily routine, and it’s all thanks to the incredible team there. Marina always greets everyone with the warmest smile and such genuine kindness that instantly makes the day better.

And David — what an absolute master of his craft. His knowledge and passion for coffee really shine through in every cup he makes. Consistently smooth, perfectly balanced, and honestly one of the best coffees I’ve had anywhere. You can tell he takes real pride in what he does.

There are days I even drive out of my way just to grab a coffee and see their friendly faces. That alone says everything. Exceptional staff like Marina and David are what keep coffee lovers like me coming back again and again.

Highly recommend Little Farms Cafe to anyone who hasn’t tried it yet. Cheers to the amazing team!

place_name: Little Farms Market, Valley Point (Grocery store)
review_text: This is a small, cozy indoor market that offers high-quality fresh produce. While the prices are relatively higher, they are justified by the quality. The selection isn't huge, but it's more than sufficient. I appreciate that they've now added a cafe right next to the market, which makes for a very convenient, all-in-one weekend affair.

place_name: Little Farms Market, Valley Point (Grocery store)
review_text: Really enjoyed the chicken barley risotto. Came here for lunch on Sunday . Appreciate the suggestions and recommendation from service staff. Also good choice of music that’s playing in the background. Highly recommended!

place_name: Little Farms Market, Valley Point (Grocery store)
review_text: Upon entering the cafe there were 3 empty tables available but the manager made us wait. Me and my 84 years old father who is a stroke patient with walking stick. I didn’t take the wheelchair due to space constraints. We seated for 10 mins but nobody serve us! This will be the 1st and last time back here!!!

We took the escalator all the way up to 5th floor carpark. And the directions to the restaurant is indicated clearly. Upon walking in, the ambience is casual, comfortable and the staff were friendly. We ordered the fried oysters, which was crispy. Lobster taco was good. Tatter tots was crispy too. I guess we should have ordered other things besides fried food for starters. But we didn't find anything else we wanted. Then we ordered 2 mains, butter chicken which was tasty and chilli crab pasta. We all like the pasta more. Thanks to our friendly server who warned us the portions are good for sharing, we ended up with a dessert. The chocolate brioche French toast was good.

I had a wonderful experience at Surrey Hills Grocer at ION Orchard. The atmosphere strikes a perfect balance between elegant and welcoming, making it an ideal venue for any occasion, whether it’s a casual catch-up with friends, a family gathering, a business meeting, or a special celebration.

The food was excellent, with quality ingredients and beautifully presented dishes. The service was attentive, friendly, and made us feel genuinely welcomed throughout our visit. The space itself is thoughtfully designed, creating a warm and comfortable environment where you can relax and enjoy your time.

What stood out most was how versatile the venue is. Whether you’re celebrating a birthday, anniversary, achievement, or simply spending quality time with loved ones, Surrey Hills Grocer provides the perfect setting.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for great food, great service, and a place that truly suits all celebrations and occasions. I will definitely be returning.

I can’t wait to share this review! The dishes were absolutely delicious, especially the Chilli Crab Linguine. This pasta features fresh crab meat tossed in a rich, tangy, and spicy chili sauce, creating a fiery yet subtly sweet flavor. Double thumbs up to the chef and his crew!

The special CNY menu was equally impressive—the Yu Sheng exceeded my expectations and tasted incredibly refreshing.

I am so looking forward to visit the restaurant again... also to share this experience with my family and friends.

Big shout-out to waitress Jee for the exceptional service 👍🏻 She was very polite, welcoming and friendly to the guests. The food came to our table very quickly after our order. All of the food tastes as good as other Surrey Hills outlets.

Reservations are recommended to be made in advanced as the restaurant could be full especially on Fri-Sun.

The restaurant is located at Lv5 of ION mall. Entering via ION Sky, you have to cut through a car park to reach the restaurant.

We had a great dining experience here and definitely will head back to this outlet to try their food again 😁

I came for birthday celebration with family. The atmosphere is nice with cozy aircon in a hot day. The foods are fresh and well prepared to our likings. The service staff are awesome with their service, especially Ms Lee. She came to checked on the food and made special introduction to the food and the restaurant to us. After she knew that it was my birthday, she had made special arrangements for me to redeem all the birthday goodies including the birthday cake. I’m glad that my girls enjoyed this place of celebration.

I visited for a HYROX trial. Even though it was my first time using any of the gear, the instructors were super helpful and patient. It’s a fantastic gym for all levels, whether you're just starting out or a pro.

check in was confusing because it was to be done on this ipad in the corner of the gym. very confusing for first timers. basically a YGIG workout but the way the workout was displayed was very confusing. instructor made an error when setting the timer, so the timer did not go off at the end of the set, and only realised the error when regulars pointed it out. yapped too much during the workout which resulted in the class being 10 mins longer than scheduled. also had to count my own reps - when i made an error in counting my own reps (my fault entirely yes), the response was “eh you never count your reps ah”. honestly i expect, in a class, for the class to be controlled by the instructor. instructor was too obsessed with offering uncalled for and unnecessary adjustments. in my view, modifications should have been offered when briefing the exercise rather than doing the rep, as this caused a lot of confusion during the set. also on a personal POV, i was not expecting a team/pair workout so i was taken by surprise when it was a pair workout. Would have been better if they included this in the class description on classpass, so that i would have avoided it. instructor name was ALEX

experience was so negative that i didn’t notice but i believe there was no water cooler.

would be 0/10 if i could.

sidenote: i got hit on by another attendee of the class (after the class yes). depending on your intention but i am not keen on such actions.
Big fan of the programming and the coaches.  I’m there several days a week and at 43 in the best condition of my life!  Can’t say enough for these folks.
Best gym in town! Great community and coaches !
Always expect good vibes, good energy and an amazing workout from REVL River Valley!

I was trained by Sam, who is an excellent personal trainer! To date I have lost 7kg. Everyone at Flex fitness is very friendly and professional and I appreciate that I can always have my own space to do my exercises as the gym is not crowded. Keep up the good work Flex Fitness! 👍🏻💪🏻😄

My life changed the day I walked into Flex. I discovered it two years ago, conveniently located just across the road from my house.

Since then, Shaik has been training me twice a week, and his expertise and encouragement have been a game-changer. He knows exactly how to push you to reach your goals. When I first started, I never would have imagined I’d be able to take on Hyrox—but here I am!

The studio is a cozy, personalized space with great music and all the equipment you need to achieve your fitness and health goals. I had some nagging back and tendon issues when I joined, but they’ve improved tremendously. Now, I feel fitter and more energized than ever.

Flex Fitness isn’t just a gym—it’s like your second home, but with more sweat and fewer couches! The coaches are absolute legends who know their stuff and make every workout feel like a win (even when your legs are begging for mercy). The community? Unbeatable. Everyone’s cheering you on, laughing through the pain, and celebrating every milestone with you. If you’re looking for expert coaching, good vibes, and a squad that actually makes working out fun, Flex Fitness is the place to be!

The cleanliness and aroma (lots of air fresheners) of the gym is amazing! Music is good…keeps me in a good mood and pumps me up for my workout 💪🏼

The trainer here is top notch 👌🏼 Very motivating and does his best to get me in the best shape

I have been training with Shaik for the past seven months and I can definitely recommend it!

A clean and well-equipped gym, motivating music, flexible planning of the training sessions and, above all, a motivating trainer.

Shaik taught me how to create an exercise program, how to properly perform the exercises and helped me make changes for a healthier diet and lifestyle in general. He also explains the  why behind the approach, so that you really understand how it works.

It's a lot of fun and I'm very happy with the results!

Attended the opening event of Ark Sports Village today and was very impressed by their facilities. There is a BFT gym, 4-5 pickleball courts and 4 futsal courts. There is also available sitting area between the pickleball courts for resting. Also another open space outside the toilet and gym area for activities or cooling down. Good setup that makes full use of the space under the highway!

It was very dark when we played from 12–3pm during the day. The glare from outside combined with the insufficient lighting made it quite difficult to see the ball clearly.

We raised the issue with the staff on site and asked if they could switch on the lights from the other courts to help reduce the glare, but we were told the lighting is controlled by a central system and they were unable to adjust it.

Overall, it’s a nice place, but the glare is a major drawback for me.

This is the largest sports facility in Singapore under a flyover with purpose-built futsal and pickleball courts, gym, and even shower facilities, transforming "dead" underutilised space into a vibrant, weather-proof, and accessible sports hub for the community.

The best pickleball court you can find in Bukit Panjang. The flooring is great, courts are spacious, ceiling is a bit low but still lobbable. Toilets/showers are clean because it’s new. The lights remain on for 10 minutes before/after booking time, which is nice. But there are several issues that make me hesitant to come back:

1. Court fees are way too expensive and don’t really serve the community. $30-45 an hour for a court is hardly an alternative to the open HDB courts that the Town Council banned. No wonder even with the opening promotion the courts are not fully booked. The VIP courts charge several extra dollars for a lounge table/chairs that no one cares about. Very unnecessary in my opinion.

2. I’ve tried playing in both the morning and evening. In the morning there is glare from the field that makes it difficult to see the ball, and in the evening there are LED lights at eye level that are also distracting (as shown in photos). This could and should be remedied with blinds/screens.

3. No water dispenser, only vending machines that sell different drinks. You’d think at this price range they’d at least provide free water…

4. There’s a smell of fertilizer/manure coming from somewhere. A bit unpleasant until your nose gets used to it after a while.

Hopefully some of these will be resolved since the courts just opened and I understand they need time to work things out.
Nice pickleball court near mrt and carpark but can surely do better with water dispenser! 🙏

Only 4 units per floor. Great location! Gate directly in front of bus stop and Great World MRT Exit 5. Super helpful security and admin. Great home and community even if there’s not a lot of amenities.

Location perfect, very centralised yet quiet. Walking distance to Takashimaya (10-12 mins). Older house therefore floor plan spacious. Project lacking in facilities, horrid open air carpark, mostly rental expats or older folks that have lived here forever. The vibe here is unfriendly. Guards have worked here forever (think 10-20 years) and knows everybody/everything, you would think them to be friendly but no. Feels like returning home to a prison cell with guards watching/stalking your every move. Once, a friend came over to pass me something, the guard just stood right beside us, staring, listing in to everything. Strictly for short term rentals, definitely will not consider purchasing with such a management/neighbours. Unless you are not bothered by such things, then the location is wonderful.
Home
What kind of management charges money for using the Table Tennis table?
Older building but nice apartments

Nice but some parts are currently under construction and closed or removed. The ship climbing structure with the winding slide is boarded away and the log section has been removed. So not much to do but the swings for smaller kids.

Still a nice place but the slides in the hills are big and quite hot as they aren’t in the shade.
Excellent playground with swings, slides and sandpits. The climbing nets and the middle of the wooden obstacle course are currently under repair.

Really nice work and playground. The swings are great for parent and child. Water to wash feet and drinking water provided. Bathrooms close by.
Ideal for a play day with kids!!!!
Nice mid size playground with well kept equipment. There is a big sand pit. Lack of public toilet nearby so this is a challenge for many families with kids to visit this place.
This is a really nice place to come and play after the kids finish their classes.
Playground in jungle but really nice done. For small and big ones. Space to rest, lots of shade but sad they closed a caffe on the top of the hill.
It was an OK playground for kids below 12 years old. It consisted of some local themes such as a giant Chempedak and saga seeds. Would be great if there is at least one F&B nearby.

COMO Adventure Grove is not just a place for kids’ activities and fun, but also a wonderful spot to enjoy nature. 🌳 For us, it turned out to be a great area for jogging and walking and refreshing, spacious, and relaxing. It’s a nice balance of play, exercise, and nature all in one place. Definitely worth a visit whether you’re here for activities or just to stay active. 🚶🏻‍♂️🏃🏻‍♂️✨

An interesting playground for older kids. A sand pit with a very large durian-shaped structure and a Saga seed themed sand filled play area. There are swings and interesting slides. But the downside is, it can get rather hot on sunny day.

An adventure playground area located within the beautiful Botanic Garden and specifically in the Gallop Extension section. Kids would love the options like sand pits, swings and slides which are quite extensive. While the walking path is sometime shady, this part of the playground is in the open so plenty of sun.
A very safe, comfortable, clean and beautiful residence, you can see the amazing sunset from here

There's no reason why this development can be left in shambles, and common areas are so poorly lit. The parking lots are dim, and the staircases aren't lit. I fear for children's safety, and for my life, in any case of emergency. Are the residents paying the management agency to snooze?

This is the most comfortable apartment I've ever lived in,Leasing Agent Christine of the apartment is very serious and responsible.The public facilities of the apartment are very clean and hygienic every time I go there, and my roommate and I often exercise in the gym.The location of the apartment is the best in my living experience in LIC
Beautiful place to lived with a very loving people's and really appreciated the immigration staff as they are a very loving and a helping personalities
Very good view level up to 24th and hve 2 block A and B.

Maplebear one tree hill has been an amazing school for our son. It ticks all the boxes that we were looking for in a school. The teachers and management have been so lovely and pleasant to deal with and we are so pleased with how our son has progressed over the years at the school.

The outdoor space is also a bonus - the play areas are well thought out and it’s so nice to see that the kids have ample space to run around in!

Our kid has been attending Maplebear OTH for the past 1.5 years and the experience has been amazing. He has met his milestones and thrives in their environment. Teachers are patient, friendly and communicative. I love the small family-orientated vibe of the school. They have sensory playtime, wonderful activities and excursions to keep the kids engaged to experience and learn new things! Teacher to kid ratio in his classes have been great and there's more than sufficient attention given to each kid.

Maple Bear One Tree Hill has been a wonderful experience for our little one. The teachers are loving, approachable, and continuously come up with creative and engaging activities for the children.

We especially appreciate the stability of the teaching team. There isn’t frequent turnover, which gives the kids a strong sense of security and familiarity.

The bilingual programme is another big plus. It allows children to be naturally immersed in both languages in a way that feels fun.

MapleBear is a wonderful school for our little one. He thoroughly enjoys the interactive play/leaning syllabus and comes home smiling everyday. They do a lot lessons in mandarin which has really expedited his adoption.

The school has a great facility with a nice outdoor area for games, PE classes and a bicycle track on the premises where the kids get a great experience and exposure to riding together and navigating each others traffic.

The teachers are very friendly and welcoming to all the kids and parents. They are good at communicating with us and have a nice app so we keep up to date our kids progress on a weekly basis. The school also organises great learning excursions and has a good holiday program in place.

We are very happy with MapleBear - One tree hill.

My two boys both attended MapleBear at One Tree Hill, and they had an absolutely wonderful time. It’s a cozy, charming conservation bungalow with a lovely outdoor playground and area that the kids adored. The school goes above and beyond by organising activities for every possible occasion, but I think the children’s favourite was always Tuck-shop Day! they loved opening a stall and 'selling' treats to their friends. What really makes the centre special is the community ❤️ the teachers know every child by name, and kids from all levels play and know each other too. We truly made the best memories at MapleBear One Tree Hill. Thank you so much for everything! ❤️

place_name: The Orange Tree Preschool ( River Valley)
review_text: My son has been with Orange Tree RV for a year now and he loves it. I don't think there is a better compliment than your kid wanting to go to school every day. The management team led by Ms Christine has been making every effort to keep kids and parents happy. Our kid loves the gourmet kitchen time and the hydroponics set up. The bi-lingual set up is also well run, we are kept up to date via app and communication directly with the teachers is encouraged. Happiness!

place_name: The Orange Tree Preschool ( River Valley)
review_text:
My child joined Orange Tree when it was just established in October 2 years ago. She adjusted to the new environment and teachers with ease,  thanks to the tight knit team of management and dedicated staff, the open communication with the parents, as well as the conducive and caring environment created for the preschoolers.

I especially like the planning of the timetable, with students assigned to 1 language teacher for the full day.  This creates a strong immersion of the language, extending to the daily activities not limited to the classroom. There is a good mix of outdoor (exploring out of the school) and indoor activities daily.

The gourmet lab was designed for cooks with small hands. Lowered tables and chairs, and mini stoves with small frying pans for each child to experience the joy of cooking.  The hydroponics farm where vegetables are grown is a wonderful way for children to learn how plants grow and reap the rewards of farming.

I believe my child puts it best when she goes around hugging the teachers before she comes to you at the end of the day with a big smile on her face, saying “I had fun today!”

place_name: The Orange Tree Preschool ( River Valley)
review_text:
My son has been at Orange Tree for 12 months and we love it! As an Australian living in Singapore - the standards of Orange Tree are truly excellent. The staff are professional, caring and talented. The curriculum is broad, field trips are organised in line with what the children are learning about and they are very good with cleanliness and health related scenarios.

Our dealings with Ms Christina and her team have been nothing other than pleasant and professional.

I'd recommend Orange Tree to any parents looking for a fun, happy and strong learning environment for their children.

place_name: The Orange Tree Preschool ( River Valley)
review_text: My child comes out of the school everyday with a big smile and tells me about all the fun activities they did. It’s a good school and we are happy. The negative reviews are by parents who didn’t necessarily send their kids to this school.

place_name: The Orange Tree Preschool ( River Valley)
review_text:
Parents, keep all screen captures of your messages, and record your conversations. Christina will forget everything then deny and refuse to take any accountability, blaming parents and her staff- anyone except herself.  All we’ve asked during this ordeal was for the school to find a fair, reasonable middle ground instead of rejecting arrangements outright that was offered by their own staff and leaving parents to deal with the fallout.

Christina calls parents to meetings to change the offer again, only to later retract by email -- even when the child has not started yet. She hides behind excuses like “legal rights” such as the one-month notice policy, showing no regard for what’s fair, honourable, or ethical.

I genuinely believe Christina did this deliberately. During the in-person meeting she called for, she confirmed the conditions with us — even though it’s now clear she already intended to cancel the whole offer. There was no interaction or follow-up between that meeting and the sudden notice, which makes the timing all the more deliberate. By waiting until later to send the formal cancellation, she wanted to purposely hurt us.  It’s hard to interpret that as anything other than a calculated move meant to leave us stuck.

No parent should be treated this way — especially after agreeing to all their changed offers, and trying to work with the school reasonably.

After Christina deliberately changed the offer 4 times on us, we also have to believe that Brent and the rest of owners and management is all in this together, choosing to entrust someone that acts in bad faith.
Doing additional duties like Tentage and MRT with limited manpower.
Lost my way.
Singapore Polytechnic Gate 1 will lead to Open Carpark A.
Great gate

Very clean and well maintained. I went there for a seminar on 30th May about Machining Learning and AI at NUS School of Computing.

The event was held at COM 1 building and was well organised by both NUS and EMERITUS.

Besides SOC, I visited NUH and NUS School of Design and Engineering as well and was impressed by its cleanliness.

Can’t wait to possibly start a postgraduate program here 😶‍🌫️

Had a great experience today at NUS Open House 2023! Although everything was only taking place at UTown, it was an extremely vibrant and enjoyable experience. There were so many activities and booths around, and it was such fun and pleasure to be at the place!

Thoroughly enjoyed my 1-day outing to the campus, where students and staff alike are very welcoming, friendly, and awesome! Thank you NUS :)

P.S. it’s a great time to visit on weekends just to walk around and admire the architecture of the campus!

Your application website is trash. I don't know who wrote your website but the person did not pass computer science for sure. Your website logs out every 30 minutes and the problem is you don't save the progress that students have made. In the end they have to refill everything again and you know how long it takes to do that. You better solve that asap otherwise you will lose  amazing students.

Amazing place for students worldwide. Top notch facilities for everything you care about. really interesting lot of students to hang around. You'll love this space , the Campus is attracted all over the city of Singapore. For vegetarians, it's a bit tricky to get the desired food. Amazing public transport and AQI less than 50.

I was amazed when I visited this campus back in 2014. Still I'm wondering how an university campus can be so beautiful. This campus is the combination of the any modern shopping mall (e.g. vibocity) and a decent forest. One can enjoyed these both feeling once he/she inside of it. Everything is heavily organized and the campus is so bug that you can not visit the entire campus in a single day. The campus is full of stairs and that's why sometimes it is called national university of Stairs.

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text: My son used to score below average in Mathematics during primary school and began to struggle even more when the syllabus became more demanding in Primary 5.
