After enrolling in Mr Eio’s classes, we saw a remarkable transformation. His understanding and confidence in Mathematics improved significantly, and he went on to consistently achieve A grades throughout upper primary and secondary school. We are immensely proud that he attained double A distinctions in his double Maths in the O-Level examinations. None of this would have been possible without the dedicated guidance and excellent teaching of Mr Eio.

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text:
My daughter, Denise has been with Mr. Eio since Primary 6. Within just one year, her Math improved tremendously—from a C to an A. Mr. Eio is a great motivator and has a wonderful way of helping students understand Math more easily and quickly. He is truly an excellent teacher.
My daughter has just received her O-Level results and achieved A1 for both A-Math and E-Math. The teachers and administrative staff are all amazing and very supportive. I highly recommend this tuition centre—don’t hesitate to enrol your child here.

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text: Mr Eio is a really great teacher to have if you wanted constant improvements in every lesson. Being very detail-oriented, he focuses on the process and steps on answering difficult questions, while ensuring complete understanding from his students. Due to his diligence is guiding me, my Math results has improved from a B4 to A1 within one exam in Sec 1. Thank you so much Mr Eio for making me realise my love for Math and Amath🤍

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text: My girl doesn’t like Math, but she loves Mr Eio’s lessons. His teaching style makes difficult concepts easy to understand, and his jovial personality makes lessons very interesting. Eventually, my girl improved, and did well in her O-Levels ✨

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text: Mr Eio can tame a lion. My boys are very active and easily distracted if the lessons are boring. But they love to attend Mr Eio’s Maths classes and shown huge improvement from AL 3 to AL 1 for P4 boy and consistently AL 1 for P6 boy ❤️🥰

To be fair,.coaches are approachable and committed. However, dealing with the admin is an extremely frustrating experience. Was told in mid April that my weekend classes will all be conducted during weekdays(4 days in a row) for the June holidays. Feedback that timeline is too short to make arrangements. As working parents, there is a reason why we send for weekend classes. Not realistic to expect that we take leave for 4 days to send kids for class. Instead of addressing my concerns, admin just kept offering dates and even sending a receipt for payment made to me. Feedback that perhaps if there are such special arrangements, to provide schedule way in advance or provide 2 week break for the holidays since they don't offer credits. Again, ignored.
Enjoyed the art and chemistry workshop. My son had lots of fun.
Coach Siew fang is very nice and attentive and create lots of fun times with the kids

instic is a great tution centre the coaches are super nice you should definitely join instic i love math because of instic
i ❤️ instic i am instic's number one fan

We have had an excellent experience with Instic. The teachers are incredibly dedicated and committed to helping my child succeed. Thanks to their support and guidance, my child achieved his academic goals. I highly recommend this tuition centre for anyone looking to provide their child with top-notch educational support!

Very very torn because I love this library so much and there are some genuinely helpful staff in there.

However, I was unfairly profiled by a staff member. Very depressing since Singapore is the last place I would think I'd have a racist experience in, much so in a library for Southeast Asian studies where our diversity should be celebrated. It was an unpleasant experience that I don't wish on anybody.

It's such a shame because I really hoped to do my research there.

Can be a bit of a trek to get to, but once you reach the place, it is heavens.  Very quiet and conducive place to research on Southeast Asia.  Great collection and friendly, helpful staff. Just approach them with your query.  Open to the public, but needs to show them your ID or membership card.  Also the standard library rules are applicable, no bags (store in lockers), no food/drink allowed.  Do note the opening hours though, don't visit outside of standard office hours or on PHs.  Remember to check out the exhibit galleries on level 1, especially the Archaeology Unit one, like a mini ACM, very interesting.
The library staff were kind and very helpful towards me, a first-time visitor. The ambience is definitely conducive for reading and studying and the space is overall, very pleasant to be in :)

The staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful. I got a library orientation the first time I went, and was impressed by the extra mile they go for library users and the personal touch the library has.

Appalled to see that people are giving this place a one-star rating on Google reviews. Come on, this is one of the very few places in Singapore for SEA research materials, moreover with such an extensive collection! I also clearly remembered how helpful the staff were during my few visits there.

A lovely visit to NLB Central with my family and some youths for the Book Bugs event. The session was engaging and thoughtfully organised, making reading fun and interactive for the children. The space was welcoming and well-maintained, and the staff were friendly and helpful throughout the event.

My kids and the youths we brought along enjoyed themselves and it was meaningful to see them excited about books and storytelling. It’s a great initiative by the library to encourage a love for reading.

The only reason for 4 stars is that the area can get quite crowded during events, but overall it was still a very positive experience. Grateful for the effort put into organising such family-friendly programmes!

Perhaps the largest public library in Singapore. Hard to find a librarian for assistance, and bookings for seats are required if you do not want to be asked to leave just because someone has booked the seats. A really stupid concept for those who would casually come into the library if they are in the area. Throughout the years, NLB has done nothing to enhance the library experience. What the public has been slapped with are rules and regulations which makes visiting the library a chore.

Came purely for the kids section of the library and it sure lived up to our expectations. The little one thoroughly enjoyed himself amidst the aquarium themed library and the endless literature around the area. Very thoughtfully, they also offer floor cushions where you can take and just find a spot to read and chill out on. The hit area with the deck chairs is the central of the kids section but was unfortunately pretty much always filled with people. Great place to spend the weekend letting the kids immerse themselves. Parking is also pretty reasonable at $1.20 per hour on Sundays.

The Central Public Library in Singapore is a vibrant space blending innovation, design, and functionality. Located at the National Library Building, it offers an extensive collection of books, digital resources, and themed spaces catering to all age groups. Highlights include the eco-friendly My Tree House, the world’s first green library for children, and its lush ambiance. The library’s modern facilities, cozy reading nooks, and seamless technology make it an ideal place for study or leisure. Regular workshops and events add to its community spirit. A hub of knowledge and creativity, it’s a must-visit for book lovers and lifelong learners.

Beautiful architecture building.  Lobby has two grand pianos, one is for public performances, another one is for anyone who wants to play!!  3rd floor has a cafe, very quiet and suitable for meetings and discussions.  The rest floors are for books and resources.  An atrium area is large and windy for who doesn’t want strong AC.  Thumbs up!!

🟤 Took the train from Orchard Station (➡️ TEL line aka Thomson East-Coast Line) to Great World Station several times to shop at Great World City Mall.

🟤 Photos shows you the directions on how to walk to Great World City mall + I also took pics of the train station's directory, platform etc

(💛✅️  I take photos so that I can remember things better).

Had been here accompanied escorted my dad, it seems I were successfully balloted for a pair of NTUC NDP Bay Celebrations tickets. The images also indicate that the collection point for these tickets is at Great World, L1 Atrium. The nearest MRT station is Great World MRT (TE15) on the Thomson-East Coast Line.

This was my first time visiting the Great World MRT station where I got directions 4 weeks ago on Sun, 13 Jul, I likely loved the experience because it was a seamless and convenient way to get to the ticket collection point. The station's proximity to the Great World shopping mall, where the atrium is located, meant a short and easy walk to collect my tickets without any hassle. This positive first impression of a new MRT station would have made the overall experience of collecting my NDP tickets even more enjoyable. 😉🎉
Nice new MRT many exits have great staff and it is very clean. Although takes a long time to get to the platform from ground level. Nice place and area as well.

This is such a convenient service. And the station is wide and spacious.

Countless directional signs and maps.  Accessible topup Machines.

And even voice over guidance.

It’s fascinating how some people find something to complain about and even write negative reviews for this station.  🤣🤣🤣

Check out the low star reviews and see what they rant about 😂😂😂
Newly opened brown line mrt is modern, convenient and spacious in design.  Less crowded compared to red and green lines, very comfortable
bus stop to find if you're going to Indonesian embassy,.
Bus stop with only 75 stops

FC3 has been through a lot. After SG’s 2020 Circuit Breaker during COVID-19, many food stalls closed down.

New hawkers came into the picture, but still leaves much to be desired and plenty of room for new tenants.
Singapore Poly's FC3 is the secret hideout of one of the top 10 chicken rice places in Singapore.  See pictures.

Recommend wok stall's salted egg chicken rice and sambal chicken cutlet fried rice. Waffles shop is good too. Generally prices are very reasonable. Food court closes slightly later so it is a convenient place for those with night classes.

The food court is open public. It is fairly big with plenty of seats. Well ventilated with ceiling fans. Good variety of food to choose. Local favorite chicken rice, nasi lemak and nasi pengyet, western, japanese, korean, vietnamese, salads and sandwiches and waffles. Prices are reasonable.

Has a nice variety of what I can only assume to be good food, looking at the queues. A lot of stuff here looks pretty appetising too, like the chicken rice, pasta, mini wok and the salad shop, which had all caught my eye. I've only tried the salad from the Crowded Bowl so far and that was pretty good.

I’m a regular work here
Great place for fresh croissants and pastries. The display was nicely arranged with many choices, and everything looked freshly baked. Tried the croissants and they were buttery, crispy outside and soft inside. Staff were awesome, friendly and helpful, with smooth service. Nice cosy atmosphere too. Will definitely come back again.
New addition to the RV area.   Good croissants and desserts to choose from.   I went when it was closing but will be back for sure.
They have a wide variety of breads, from savory to sweet, all freshly baked. The staff are also very nice and friendly.

My house is near to valley point mall. Then i saw new bakery shop open. Shop looks so pretty and cute so i walk in order black coffee and crossiant. Crossiant is definetly good like their shop name and coffee also nice. Slighlty have sour taste but i like sour coffee. Anyway hot black americano and crossiant is good combination. I love thier almond crossiant, coffee bun, egg tart. If u like hard bread, try thier sour dough and rye also!
Cosy little corner place for work and leisure. Great coffee too. Thank you 😍☕

@eats.withshan

I can't believe I just visited this place when I'm literally around the area every single week!

This place is so chill with indoor & outdoor seating.

Loaded Avo Toast with Sous Vide Egg ($15.8) - so generous with the fresh avocado with chewy sourdough!
Flat White ($6.1) was good and not that tiny.

Stuck here cos of the heavy rain so I decided to order a banana bread (the loaded one) - soo good, with all the choco chips & walnut texture!

Goodness this is such a hidden gem for some one that just moved to Singapore, worth value of money, simple french toast but the balance between how they toast the bread and blueberry and yoghurt blends perfectly, not to mention their honey glazed cheese toast!

Oh the ice long black is as smooth as tea, love the laidback atmosphere too as this is amongst the residential hool.

Would definitely come here again

I like the coffee here and come here every other weekday just for the long black. Food is generally good but sometimes inconsistent. I like the granola and yogurt here as well as the French toast. Recently I tried the pescatarian breakfast but found it to be a hit or miss - it was very well done the last time I had it a week ago, but today it was underwhelming. Had asked for eggs to be scrambled without the yolks but it was not an option so I asked if they could do sunny side up and it was also not an option but I was given the option to have it poached but it turned out to be soft boiled eggs. As it’s partially cooked, I was not able to consume it. This time, there was no butter with the meal and the salmon slices were slightly less than what I had previously gotten. I will still come back but probably not order the mains for a while.

Visited with two friends — we loved everything. Perfect flat white, great desserts, and the spicy grilled cheese with honey is a must-try. Spicy, sweet, super satisfying. Cool social vibe and a nice spot to get some work done. Will be back.

I had the hot honey grilled cheese and I find it a little too hot (spicy) for my taste. At first the hot was interesting but it lingers for too long. The banana cake with mascarpone cream is nice and moist and will come back for only this.

The wait was too long for the food though. Almost 40 min wait. The table next to us waited 1 hr for a heated banana bread

Visited during lunch and it was so warm that we were perspiring despite seated indoors, so we didn't really have a comfortable dining experience 🥲

Food was served fast and came in huge portion especially the nachos!! The baja fish tacos was what I was really looking forward to, but turns out underwhelming as it was heavily covered with sauce with a slightly bland/ under-seasoned fish. Flavours of habenaro chix wings were also not to my liking, turns soggy quickly too.

I will recommend the nachos & beef quesadilla for sure! Overall, it's great place to hang out & chill (but highly recommend to do so in the evening as it can be super warm in the afternoon).

First time trying Huevos, after they accept reservations. Waited a while at the door to be served, and a tall Indian young man eventually came. He asked for our reservation and signaled us to enter, and leaving us standing at the front door while he went out to greet a friend!!! That was rude!!

Food was not super! Only the Loaded Nachos is worthy and it came quickly after ordering. The Baja fish tacos were super salty, and the beef quesadillas were also heavy on salt. The prawn tacos were acceptable. Our churros never came, and was the spoiler for the night.

We were seated on the 2nd floor, super stuffy, swamped with body odors and badly in need of ventilation.

We waited for more than 30 minutes for the elusive churros. Checked with the cheerful waitress 3 times, and she assured me each time that they are preparing it. I paid for the meal trusting that our churros would turn up, and that was a mistake. When the churros was a no show, I decided to cancel this order and seek a refund for the paid churros.

As this place only accept QR quote payment, refund is in cash and it can only be done by presumably the supervisor. The same young Indian man appeared, visibly agitated, and confirmed that I wanted a refund. No apology was made, the only excuse he gave was they only have 1 fryer!!! He went downstairs to retrieve cash and handed me the refund, but the amount was not correct. I highlighted to him the wrong amount, of which he apologized, and when I asked him why the MIA churros, to which he rebutted with “I have already apologized, what else do you want!!!”.

With such bad attitude, not sure if this young man can persist in this trade.

Had the Baja fish, chicken chipotle quesadilla, bira tacos and a couple of Mexican beers. Honestly everything was spot on.

The Baja fish was crispy, fresh and super flavourful. Easily one of the better ones I’ve had.
The chicken chipotle quesadilla was loaded and really well-balanced. It's smoky, cheesy, and just the right amount of heat.
The bira tacos were juicy and packed with flavour; you can taste the effort that goes into them.

All of it with an ice cold Mexican beer for a perfect combo.

Solid place, great food, and I’ll definitely be back.

In my opinion, this is one of the best Mexican-style food spots in Singapore, especially around town. I’ve been here multiple times and have also ordered via Grab so far, it’s the best Mexican-inspired place I’ve tried, and I’ll definitely keep coming back.

This time, we dined in and also ordered takeaway. We had the mango ceviche (fresh and refreshing), Baja fish tacos (so spicy but I love it!!!! ), pork chorizo quesadilla (rich and comforting), birria beef tacos (still flavourful even as takeaway), and the churro ice cream sandwich with dulce de leche, the ice cream is sooo good!!!! . Some dishes are really spicy, but I personally enjoyed the chilli and the bold flavours.

Just a small note on billing: I did ask for the takeaway to be on a separate bill. Although a separate receipt was given, it was still placed under the same table instead of being billed independently. As a result, service charge was applied to the takeaway items, so service charge was applied. After I noticed it, I asked the staff to redo the bill and remove the service charge, which they did.
All good in the end, just a heads-up to be a bit more careful with takeaway billing next time.

Heard quite a few times before this is the best spot for mexican food in Singapore. While I don't know if that's true it's definitely very nice. I went here for dinner with my partner and we shared fish tacos and a quesadilla carnitas. Total was 43$ which is more or less okay. The tacos are flavorful and filled v generously. The fish is crispy and tender. The quesadilla is filled very rich and it's dripping when u bite in. Always a good sign. It's juicy, cheesy and equally flavor packed. Overall it's just a great spot for a meal. Service was friendly too.

We went on a Thursday, 5 of us.
We ordered 2 bottles of Fratelli Senio Chianti, very reasonably priced at $37 each; an easy drink which went well with the food.

Most of the cooking was done in slow cooking, the meatball was very soft and light, the tomato paste was not too rich, so you could taste the pork.

The Chicken Parmigiana was a real surprise, very soft, tender and crispy. I couldn’t really taste the chicken. Maybe that’s how they intended it to be.

Steak was good, medium by default, beef was juicy and tender, great value!

28April2026. 6.30pm to 8.30pm

I had such a wonderful experience at Little Farms Valley Point, and I truly want to highlight the exceptional service from Pauline and Iwan.

From the moment I walked in, their warmth and attentiveness stood out. Pauline was incredibly friendly, patient, and knowledgeable—she made thoughtful recommendations and ensured everything was just right. The gentleman Iwan was equally impressive: calm, professional, and always one step ahead in anticipating what customers might need. Together, they created a seamless and genuinely welcoming experience.

The food, as always, was fresh, high-quality, and beautifully presented. Every bite reflected the care that Little Farms consistently delivers. The ambience was another highlight—relaxed yet refined, making it the perfect place to unwind, catch up, or simply enjoy a good meal.

What truly made this visit memorable, though, was the people. Service like this is rare, and it deserves to be recognized. Thank you, Pauline and team, for making my day!

Highly recommend and will definitely be back.

We absolutely love coming here for their 1-for-1 small plates during happy hour! It’s such a great deal and makes for a perfect evening. The alcohol prices are also very reasonable for the quality and location.

• Spicy Prawns: Highly recommend! We tried this based on the waiter’s suggestion and it was delicious—great flavor and a nice kick.

• Cheese Platter: The selection of cheeses was fantastic and very yummy. It pairs perfectly with our red wines.

The service here is good. The staff are attentive and they served us plain water without us even having to ask, which is a small gesture we really appreciate.

We had a great time and will definitely be back again for more small plates and drinks!

Note: cafe closing at 9.30pm.

Pauline and Iwan made our visit. Pauline was super warm and had great food recommendations, steering us well when something on the menu was OOS. Iwan was attentive throughout and made sure we were well taken care of. Great food, even better service. Will be back!

Would have given 1 star but service was ok and cafe vibes was nice. They also provided water FOC which is rare these days.

Ordered their Eggs Ben with Ham. For $28.78 (after GST and service charge), the Eggs Ben came without any sides. The hollandaise sauce was saltier than the HAM. And poached eggs were overcooked. Waited 25 minutes for the food, was not worth the wait.

Once and done, unfortunately..

Male staff, wearing spectacles, has no customer service. Unfriendly, unapproachable. He made me feel as if i owe him a living - but I am just buying things there. I believe management needs to send them for customer service training.

chinese lady cashier of older age attended to me on 3/8/25 at 12.37pm. she was extremely rude and unprofessional, if i could give zero stars i would.

when asked to redeem a voucher for payment, she asked me if it was qr code or paywave. i answered that it was a barcode voucher to scan.

auntie pulled up the nets payment QR code and when showed her my voucher to redeem, she began grumbling and saying that she had asked me earlier if i wanted to use voucher (which i stated upfront). she grumbled multiple times that it would take very long to cancel the transaction.

in the first place, i have seen auntie around and know that she is a longtime worker in this store. i am sure she is aware of how vouchers are redeemed (can vouchers be redeemed via scanning the nets qr?).

if it was a honest mistake on her part, i do wish your staff can show more patience and a better attitude to their customers in future.

do better please 7 eleven. i can’t imagine a patient or relative visiting a loved one who is sick in NUH being treated as such.
Rude! Rude! Rude! The worst 7 Eleven I have ever visited. Indeed the older lady is Rude and so is the big guy in glasses. They should stay at home if they hate their work so much.

Chinese cashier lady of older age is extremely rude, choses customers to pick on, and friendly towards her favourite regular customers, this shows she has capability to be service-oriented but chooses not to. When about to show the staff pass; she scolded, 你要敢敢的给我看卡!! 不然我不会给你discount (YOU MUST BRAVELY SHOW ME PASS IF NOT I WONT GIVE YOU DISCOUNT). show or don’t show also she find problem with you. 7 eleven please retrain the staff that you hire because honestly the business will be negatively affected when service is poor.

The Cashier was so rude and unprofessional, big chinese guy with glasses. I bought something and I gave him a big note because obviously, I don't have any change? Then he proceeded to tsk at me at an annoying tone, my bad didn't know I was here to receive attitude. Anyway, then he proceeded to just leave the item when I needed a bag and was like "DONE ALREADY LA" in such a rude tone..

The 7 eleven store upstairs was closed at 6pm so I had to come here instead. Really glad the outlet here at the one north MRT has longer hours. It is a good place to get some drinks at a good price. There's also a microwave for convenient meals, hot water and a coffee machine inside.

The staff there was quite friendly and handled my purchase without any problems 🙂👍

I visited the store today to buy an eye drop. Since the price stated for the eye drop was visibly displayed there as $7.20, i brought it to the cashier and paid for it. When I saw $9.10 deducted from my card, i asked her to confirm the price and the cashier, thinking the payment didn't go through, asked me to tap again. I told her the payment has went through, then she started replying me quite harshly, "Since you have seen the price, why ask me again?".

I tried to tell her once again that I wanted to confirm the price since the price indicated on the shelf and what I paid did not tally. She took the price sticker from the shelf to check the item code again and this time she confirmed it was $9.10.

Since I have already paid for it, i just told her,  "It is ok then. But please change the price indicated on the shelf." However, instead of agreeing so, she just said this loudly in a defensive manner,  "It wasn't me. I am just in charge of cashier!"

I wasn't accusing her or saying she did it, but since she already knew the price, it should be the right thing to update the price to avoid misleading other customers. She had instead gone defensive. Overall, compared to the other branches I had visited, this one provided the worst experience.

If I could, I’d give ZERO star and call this outlet "The INCONVENIENCE store".

One of the worst 7-Eleven stores I’ve ever visited. The older aunty at the cashier is consistently rude and careless. Out of five visits, at least four involved mistakes — and not once did she admit or apologize.

She scans the wrong items (somehow, the product from the previous customer ends up in my bill). When I politely pointed out the error, she dismissed me with, “Correct ar! These are your items!” I had to explain that one of the items wasn’t even on the counter before she reluctantly removed it — no apology by the way.

Another time, I bought a drink in an opaque bottle. She eyed it suspiciously and asked, “What’s inside the bottle?” in a tone that sounded more accusatory than curious, as if I’d hidden something in it. You could literally hear the liquid sloshing inside. Then, when I scanned my Yuu member QR code from the app, she said, “Your Yuu card got registered or not?” — after she just saw me reached over to her cash register and scan it myself. She didn't even bother to turn her scanner towards me. The "inconvenience" that I have caused.

The only time I’ve had a smooth transaction there was when someone else was manning the counter.

I hope 7-Eleven management can review this outlet’s service quality and ensure staff are trained to treat customers with respect and professionalism. It would make a huge difference to the overall experience.

Had the worst encounter today. Asked the cashier to scan my yuu app upon paying and she gave me this dirty look for 10 seconds expecting me to reach over her cash register and scan it myself. She then flicked the scanner towards me angrily which was so unnecessary and rude…. I scanned it and asked for paywave, i paid and it said approved so I took my bread and while I was about to walk away she shouted at me “ eh u haven’t scan! “…. Confused and annoyed I just walked away. Gosh ruined my morning. 😡
Horrible service. Counter staff chatting instead of serving. Ask you to use self service machine. Why not just replace her ?

The Redhill Market Food Centre holds a special place in my heart, filled with a deep sense of nostalgia every time I visit. It has a charming old-school atmosphere that reflects Singapore’s rich food heritage, with stalls that have stood the test of time. Despite its age, the quality and variety of food remain exceptional, offering a wide selection of local favourites. What makes it even more appealing is the affordability, allowing anyone to enjoy delicious meals without breaking the bank. It is truly a hidden gem that captures both tradition and value in one place.

Old School Retail | This nostalgic era of traditional retail is destined to fade away forever, overshadowed by technological progress, ongoing conflicts caused by human choices, and a growing disinterest from local communities, new citizens, and younger generations.

Best market every featuring mala, western food, soya sauce chicken noodle, chicken rice, various drinks shops,desert shops, cai fan, nasi lemak, fish all noodles, prawn noodles, duck noodles, I could go on and on but why don't you take a visit at night which is the best time and see if I am lying.

Redhill Market offers various food options at low prices. The quality of food is generally nice, with the economy rice, carrot cake and mung bean soup being highlights. However, the dining environment is in desperate need of a makeover, as the cleanliness of this place is just horrible.

Good for cooked and uncooked food items.
Also, has a set of other utility shops next to it. Can meet most of the daily needs.

My usual go to gym in town very spacious, fully equipped
They just did renovation recently and upgraded some machines
Also engaged my PT Fairuzi from here he’s very professional and engaging
10/10!
Jia is extremely helpful too
Just renovated with many fresh and new equipments. Best gym especially for legs day

Anytime Fitness ValleyPoint has to be the nicest outlet I’ve ever been too! The newly renovated branch has so many machines that I can try. What I love especially is the huge shower toilets that they have!

Been a member of AF for 6 months and love this place even more after the recently equipment upgrade. More legs equipment and a very clean gym. Really enjoyed their new equipment!!!!

Staff were amazing and the ladies at the gym were very friendly. But a middle age gentlemen in the office speaks very loudly and gives unnecessary comment about my workout. Seems like a high ego men and didn’t want to entertain him.

My home gym - a unique place different from other AF’s, with ceiling to floor glass windows, which let natural light through. Great views of traffic passing below, as well as condos surrounded by greenery. This nice ambience makes my workouts much more enjoyable than exercising in a concrete box, and time passes more quickly.

Management are fairly responsive and they keep improving the equipment; lately they added a bunch of new machines so that now, there is literally a machine for every body part you want to sculpt. Beauty of this gym is its spaciousness & sunlight flooded premises - so they should be careful not to overcrowd the floor space with too many pieces; they’ve just about reached the limit.

The elderly cleaning lady is brilliant - super diligent, warm and friendly, she keeps the place spic & span despite her age (80+). An awesome worker worthy of specific mention.

Removing 1 star as I was bullied by a nasty man some time ago (screaming aggression directed at me & violent weight smashing) so I requested a callback from the owner, plus a warning letter to the man, which they refused. They should take ladies seriously when issues of harassment are raised, and actively take steps to protect women, who are more vulnerable than men. Shortly after the incident however, they did include TV messages about being courteous & complying with rules.

I've been training with Theo at Level Up Gym in One-North for more than a month now, and it's been a really good experience. The gym is clean, well-equipped, and never too crowded—perfect for focused sessions.

Theo brings a wealth of experience, is always very encouraging and motivates you when you feel like giving up. What I’ve appreciated most is his attention to form and injury prevention. I came in with a history of lower back injury, and Theo tailored my program to gradually build strength and confidence without pushing recklessly.

Highly recommend if you want smart, no-BS training with a coach who knows his stuff.

While is Singapore, Lloyd was my PT & he transformed the way I looked after my body & increased my confidence in using weights.  Lloyd’s expertise, attention to detail, tailored programme & strong communication (in addition to our enjoyable conversations) made me look forward to our sessions.  Now back in the UK I miss my PT sessions with Lloyd but we keep in regular contact.  I definitely recommend you consider Level Up… you don’t know what you’re missing 😁💪

I began my fitness journey with Coach Matthias at Level Up Gym in January 2026, and it has been an incredible experience.

His patience, expertise, and attention to detail - especially in ensuring proper form, helped me grow stronger and build confidence in my training.

I’m truly grateful to have him as my coach and would highly recommend him to anyone looking for guidance on their fitness journey.

I trained with Lloyd prior to my wedding and all I can say is that he is FANTASTIC! Lloyd is super knowledgeable, extremely personable and I hit all my goals in record time training with him. The gym space is high end, discreet and private. Overall Level Up and Lloyd is the premier combination in Singapore to smash your fitness goals and work on injury recovery! Thank you Lloyd!

My partner and I joined Level Up Gym to get back into exercising after quite a long break, and Coach Matthias Tan has been amazing from day one. He’s super attentive, patient, and always makes sure we’re doing each movement safely and correctly.

What we really appreciate is how open he is to feedback and how he customizes our workouts to match our fitness goals. Every session feels fun, motivating, and just the right challenge for us.

If you’re looking for a supportive place to restart your fitness journey, I can’t recommend Level Up Gym and Coach Matthias enough! 💪

This is the main building of University Sports Centre, also known as USC. Located opposite Yusof Ishak House, this three-storey facility boasts features like swimming pools, squash courts, a gymnasium, and a rock-climbing wall. It’s also effectively a sheltered path for those travelling to Valour House from the direction of YIH.
Home of Team NUS. There are plenty of sports facilities here including swimming pool and squash courts.

The sports centre houses a variety of sports facilities, including two olympic-sized swimming pools (indoor and outdoor), gym, squash, among others.

The swimming pool is generally clean, and may get busy at times. I also particularly appreciate the clean changing rooms/toilet.
Well managed sports centre with vibrant community.
Sports complex for basketball, swimming, squash, table tennis etc. for NUS students and staff.

Pitch is new and a few more months to run in as there are patches of irregular surface (non flat). Parking is good as Orto is just next to it with many F&B options as well. Other feedback from fellow Futsal mates is that the pitch is long with narrow width hence need a bit of getting used to by changing certain strategic formation. Overall, good pitch that will certainly be better as time gets by

Great environment to enjoy a game of football.

Would have been better if there are water coolers on site.
Nice place for soccer games. Equipped with vending machines for drinks and a small snack bar.

Chef sharif let me try the pizza coming soon on the menu. Very good amount of cheese. And not too heavy. Would like it more if the dough had a bit more burn.

Looking forward to the menu!
To find free attractions in Singapore nearly impossible. This is place is one of the free one. And very beatifull. It is only one rain forest which is located in a city. Don't miss.
It’s a great swimming pool with a paradise-like experience! Visitors welcome here too!
Very windy, bad for lap swims

The outdoor training pool opening hours on Saturday on website, outside notice and inside notice are different. Website said opening from 9am. Notice outside said 9am, only Olympic pool is 10:30am onwards. But handwriting notice inside said both outdoor pools are 10:30am. It is quite confusing.

I asked it said outdoor training pool is closed every Tue, Thu and Sat 9am to 10:30am.

Please update both websites and notice boards outside.
Olympic sized pools at University sports centre, NUS. Entry is by NUS student or staff id card. Water is clean and good facilities for shower, changing etc.

It’s located in the NUS Sports Hub. Only for staffs and students. But staff/students can purchase tickets in the NUS Reboks system for friends and families. Went there during lunch time and we are  only allowed to use the small outdoor pool and the indoor pool. The standard outdoor pool in the photo is out of bound.

Pool is well maintained and free for NUS students.

If you're a student I strongly suggest you use it since sport fees are most likely included into your school fees.

review_author_name: Surf Life Saving Vietnam Co., Ltd
review_text: Such great pools.

First time at EagleWings Cinematics, after having a great dinner at EagleWings Loft a few months back.

First impressions were excellent. The staff warmly showed us around the lounge and explained how we could enjoy the place before the movie. We were especially excited to order the lobster risotto again, which we had been craving.

The lounge experience was affected when a rowdy group arrived before what seemed to be a company movie screening. Not the cinema’s fault, but it would help if staff could gently guide such groups to available seats instead of allowing them to block walkways and inconvenience other guests.

Our sides arrived quickly, though the risotto took a little longer. Staff were attentive throughout and checked with us on whether we preferred to dine in the lounge or in the hall.

The Platinum Hall was beautifully furnished and comfortable. Our food was neatly arranged at our seats when we entered. One thing to note is that the top-row “sofa” seats do not have centre armrests, unlike the lower rows, which may matter depending on seating preference.

Before the movie started, a family of three occupied two sofa sections, and the child was climbing around and making quite a bit of noise. Thankfully, it stopped once the movie began after the finally parents stepped in. It did make me wonder whether there are checks to ensure guests are seated according to their bookings.

Overall, it was still a very enjoyable evening, and I would gladly return again, hopefully without another large corporate screening crowd while in the lounge, or rowdy children in the theatre.

Made a trip today to try out EagleWings Cinematics for the first time, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. While we initially encountered some hiccups with the lounge service, the manager and team were proactive, sincere, and prompt in addressing our concerns, which we truly appreciated. The theatre itself was well-maintained, with spacious and comfortable seating. Dining in the theatre was enjoyable, and the Loft Menu offered good food and snacks at reasonable prices. Given the many positive aspects of our visit, we look forward to returning.

Everything was good but the service of the food counter staff on duty today. We ordered popcorn from the QR code in our seats, but after 15-20 mins it still had not arrived. We went out and saw that your staff Madison who was at the popcorn counter was playing with her phone and did not take notice of our order. Re-training highly encouraged.

Amazing experience. We booked in advance the premium lounge. My son and I used their massage chairs. That alone was worth the experience. It was like getting a deep tissue massage. They seem to have upgraded their massage chairs recently. Oh and free flowing drinks (hot or hold). (I recommended u come at least 45mins before the cinema start time). Then we went in, each two people can sit together on a couch which reclines and allows you to stretch your legs. It even comes with a fresh blanket in a pouch. Then u can scan the QR code and order as if you’re at a restaurant with a garment menu. Optionally you can also order popcorn 🍿and pay as u order. or press a separate call button to order more free soft drinks.

Royal experience. We watched supermario galaxy movie and the sound system and the screen size was suitable for the size of the room.

I realized that we picked the best cinema in SG when we kept talking about how awesome the experience for 10mins while we walked back to the MRT. We started planning our next visit as soon as we walked out.

Oh and the kindness and the hospitality is something else. This great level of treatment of customers is a rare treat in Singapore.

I wish I can give it higher than 5 stars
⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ it’s obvious whoever came up with this idea loves cinema, food and wants to share their passion with other people in SG. I wish this place more success and expansion in SG.

Kitty and I have been loyal patrons of EagleWings Cinematics for years, and we absolutely adore this cinema.  It's a truly unique experience that we keep coming back to again and again.

EagleWings Cinematics is located within the KAP Mall, and it stands out from other cinemas in Singapore because of its intimate and luxurious atmosphere.  Each theater is cozy and designed to hold a small group of people, like 12 to 20 people, making it feel like a private screening. It's so comfortable and relaxing!  The seating is fantastic, and we've tried all their classes—Silver, Gold, and Platinum.  Our favorites are Gold and Platinum, which offer plush reclining sofas with blankets to keep you warm.

But the real highlight is their amazing service and amenities.  Right next to the cinema is a fantastic restaurant where you can order a wide variety of food, from local favorites like laksa to international dishes like steak and fish and chips.  You can even order beer if you choose Gold or Platinum class.  It's a far cry from the typical cinema snacks! You can also enjoy their lounge one hour before your movie starts, with free-flowing coffee and soft drinks, and even two massage chairs to relax in!

The service is impeccable.  They efficiently deliver your food to your table in the theater, and the table lamps are perfectly positioned to illuminate your meal without disturbing other guests.  The sound system is excellent, and the air conditioning is just right.  It's a comfortable and enjoyable movie-going experience.

We love how EagleWings never feels crowded or rowdy.  The atmosphere is always so peaceful and relaxing, perfect for enjoying a movie.  We've recommended EagleWings to many of our friends, and they've all become addicted to this amazing cinema.  From that point on, they've been watching movies at EagleWings exclusively!

The overall experience is always positive.  It gives you a holistic experience, where you can enjoy your movie on the comfortable sofa seat and have delicious meals in the cinema.  The food we've tried over the years has always been above average.

EagleWings is also incredibly convenient.  All the theaters are on level 1, and if you're looking to book a private function, you can head to level 2.

We highly recommend EagleWings Cinematics to anyone looking for a luxurious and memorable cinema experience.  It's a great option for a romantic date night, a family outing, or a night out with friends.

chill and cozy place for couples to spend some time together.

The room feels warm the moment you step in. You can tell the owners put thought into the decor. Soft lighting and a pleasant scent. Room feels clean.

The staff was friendly and helpful. When i needed help with the TV, she provided it promptly and added some time back for the technical delay, even though its not their fault.

Definitely enjoyed my experience.

Visited CosyPlace Bukit Timah on 6 May and initially had an unpleasant experience due to the attitude and tone of one of the staff members onsite. However, I would like to acknowledge that the management team handled the matter very promptly and professionally after receiving my feedback.

They responded quickly, took the issue seriously, and provided reassurance that corrective actions were taken. I appreciate the efficiency and professionalism shown in addressing the situation and improving their customer service standards.

Thank you to the management team for handling the feedback responsibly.

Love this location at first sight. The tv was huge. Booked to watch concert and feels like im in the concert. Very cozy and clean. The host was so nice and friendly. Will definitely come here again. There is a lot of foods here too.

This is truly a premier spot for private dates and birthday celebrations! Having booked here several times now, I can confidently give it a 10/10. The space is consistently clean, the sound system is excellent, and I love having full access to entertainment like Netflix and Disney+. To top it off, the prices are very affordable, and they offer great discounts for students. Highly recommended!
Room is quite spacious, sofa very very comfy, big enough to lie flat on, air conditioned and tv+sound system are perfect!!
Pretty nice functional place. Interesting sheltered basketball court with giant fan for outdoor activities. Lovely little playground for kids.
The badminton court is wet & slippery. Can the CC staff take note. Badminton players might slip & fall. Plus the hall feels like oven. There's aircon but never on. There's no air circulation.

A covered basket ball court and an older outdoor court, three Badminton courts, Table Tennis, kids play area, indoor play areas and restaurants and classes and activities for the residents. A pretty well equipped Community club for the Ayer Rajah residents.

There's a TenderBest outlet in the community centre, which allows us to have a great meal together with our family or even before sending our kids for classes too. There's a gym on level 3, a karaoke room, badminton courts, a huge basketball court at level 1 & many more. 😊

Relatively modern, lively community club in the West part of Singapore. A few F&B places, tenderfresh, ice cream and yoguet shops etc. Many activities and programmes for the residents nearby. Bonus factor is that there is also a POSB ATM.

Park at Pixel (free parking on Sunday) and walk 2mins to the playground.
There are shades around the playground for the adults while the children can play on the sand covered playground under some shades too.
A good place to spend the Sunday morning.

A good small playground for young kids, maybe up to lower primary level. There are a variety of equipment that the children can play with. Some part is sheltered by trees, but it is still best to come in the morning or near sunset so that it would not be too hot.

A rustic and beach-vibes kind of playground. It is beautifully designed. Sadly, it seems unmaintained and not widely used. There is so much to explore here for younger to older kids upto 12 years old. I wish it doesn't get neglected.
Very nice playground but over run with ants! I wish it weren’t so. We could not stay long cos the ants were getting into our shoes and biting our feet.
Nice

One of the largest playground for kids from 1 to 10 years old. Very beautifully designed and kids will love the playground. The two play items that stand out most is the Viking Ship and the Flying Fox. Do not miss out visiting this playground if you have kids!

A great place to bring the kids! There are many playground areas, equipment (swings, slides, climbing structures, etc) and sand areas to keep the children occupied. There are also play equipment for the younger kids and toddlers too!

Not to forget the huge green spaces to play sports and games and run around.

Very nice playground with good amount of shade. Well maintained park. Playground has wide range from age group 3-5, 6-12, and 12+. Big sand pit that is not dirty. Recommended time to visit after 5~5:30pm to avoid the heat. The playgrounds are behind Mcdonalds

🛝Which kid can resist a Pyramid Space Net!
🛝And all the more, with two Slides at different heights.
🤼Kids can have endless fun time playing Catching (local term for Tag) on the Pyramid Space Net and taking to the slides.
It's always the first facility I visit in West Coast Park, it upholds my childhood, and I'm glad that this facility is still here!
Home to some of the beautiful wild life. I love this place as it's less crowded compared to other famous park. It also has very nice scenery to the southern sea area or to the city area as well.

Kent Ridge Park is one of those underrated gems in Singapore that beautifully blends nature, fitness, and history into one experience

The park offers a surprisingly diverse terrain — expect plenty of stairs (a great leg workout!) and scenic walking paths shaded by lush greenery. It’s perfect if you’re looking for something more challenging than a flat park connector. Along the way, you’ll find multiple fitness corners, making it easy to mix in a quick workout with your walk or jog

What really sets this park apart is its historical significance. This was one of Singapore’s last WWII battle sites — the Battle of Pasir Panjang — and there are informative markers that add a meaningful layer to your visit. It’s not just a park, it’s a place where history comes alive quietly in the background

There’s also a tranquil pond area and several lookout points where you can pause and enjoy views of the surrounding greenery and even parts of the city in the distance

I went here around 4:30 pm on Saturday. There were not so many visitors. The place was queit. There were some photo spot especially the one which face the harbour. My favorite was the canopy walk.
I enter from the north side (the pond) and then going down through the canopy walk and then pasir panjang road.

It was an okay place for walking or exercising.
Great place to travel here. Look at the plants and museum. And for people who do training, good place to run hill fresh air. Nice environment and good place. Not so excited but peaceful and relaxing
Nestled near the southern part of the island, perfect high-point to enjoy the surrounding view that should be enjoyed until the hills are cleared for development!
A small but completed and well maintained part within the urban city area.

Forest Canopy Boardwalk area is a small bird sanctuary located at one-north Park, Fusionopolis South.
🐦🦜🐦‍⬛It's therapeutic to walk around the serene lush greenery Boardwalk listening to the birds calls.

Wonderful little park! Comes with this party corner, where people like to sit, but fail to find the trash bin. Behind the wall is not for trash! The ants seem to love it though! I made a photo story to show you the way to the bin, it is no more than 50m away, just around the corner. For a challenge they made the bin camouflaged-green, but once you found it, you'll always find it. Might be worth to add stairs to the "Please keep our parks clean" note.
green and peaceful park. I like to walk one round there every afternoon. Walking in the park is like walking in the jungle, a lot of trees and birds chirping, lovely place
Great park for some green scenery in the one-north area... and a photograph or two...

Very nice place for active treking if you want to escape from the concrete jungle of Singapore. All trails are easy and in very good condition. If you hike or trek in the mountains, you will do this trail in 25% of the estimated time from the info boards. There's a lot of animals there, monkeys are calm, not like these stupid ones from Krabi in Thailand. I really enjoyed a half day there. Take drinks and food with you, since there's no shops near by to purchase anything. You can refill the water at the kayaking station in the south east area of the trail.

Beautiful nature park that links to many trails and walks. Awesome flora and fauna, with a shady canopy most of the way. Convenient parking just at the entrance. Not a big crowd usually. Not as popular as other nature parks such as McRitchie, but not bad at all. Nice cafes and other food options nearby at Upper Thomson too. Would definitely recommend

Found the perfect weekend mini adventure/walk-about in Singapore - a comfortable 5km round trip but extendable for those who wants to go further. Totally immerse in nature throughout the walk, with well spaced intersections of options for a quick turnaround. Highly recommended for low to mid level workout on a sunny weekend.

Just visited Windsor Nature Park and was blown away by how peaceful and lush it is! The trails are well-maintained, with clear signage to guide you through the forest. Loved walking under the canopy of tall trees – it’s so refreshing and a perfect escape from the city buzz. There are also informative boards along the way to learn about local flora and fauna. Great spot for families, hikers, or anyone looking to reconnect with nature. Highly recommend adding this to your list of green spaces to explore in SG!

Good place to relax yourself and regain some positive energy. The walkway is mostly flat, consider safer, but still need to be careful, as there are alot of people exercise/ jogging. You can see greeny plants and cute insects, as well as animals around. And the stream is very clear and clean, can see many fish. Love the air inside, so fresh. Suitable to go alone or in group. And the most important, less mosquitoes inside.

Palm Valley Lawn is one of the most beautiful open spaces in the Singapore Botanic Gardens. The wide green lawn framed by towering palm trees creates a very scenic and peaceful atmosphere, and it almost feels like being in a tropical resort or movie set

I really enjoyed the sense of openness here compared to the more densely forested parts of the Gardens. It is a fantastic place for a relaxing stroll, quiet picnic, photography, or simply sitting on the grass and enjoying the surroundings. The iconic Shaw Foundation Symphony Stage in the distance adds to the charm of the landscape

The lawn is exceptionally well maintained, and the variety of palm species around the valley makes this area feel both grand and tranquil at the same time

The Palm Valley holds many genus of the Garden's palm family. The Palms trees were planted on both sides, leaving the mid section of the long lawn with well-kept lush green grass.
This spacious Palm Valley Lawn is a popular venue for picnics and for watching outdoor concerts. Scheduled concerts, usually on weekend evening, were performed on the beautiful Shaw Foundation Symphony Stage.

Love the lawn. Miss the times when there was live performances.

Great spot for a picnic and hanging out with our kids and pets.
Massive picnic spot that never gets old. Serene tranquility.
Wonderful, Beautiful like haven lawn..........and what a green ...Peace comes down to the eyes
Nice scenery of the west coast while enjoying a breezy cycle ride 👌 👍 😀 🚲
This's best location for general public who want to have a close up view of some offshore marine services related vessels and equipment.
Beautiful trail with lots of wildlife
Beautiful sunsets
You need fresh air...?!? Coming here. It's good for healing

Lived there for four years (2015-2019) it was such a nice place to live in very peaceful and less noise from the outside especially when  it come inside the sizes of the Units which was 2,000 square feet ones were specious even there you could not hear a person at the other end of the Unit. The gym was a bit spacious and not very cramped the swimming pool was slightly big. Like the place but miss it still.

The apartments are spacious, clean, and modern. The area is quiet, but it’s close to Orchard Road for shopping and food. The pool and gym are great for relaxing. It’s a comfortable and convenient place to stay. Perfect for families or anyone!

Went there for some online purchases,
Mr Wong the Security officer was very pleasant.
I see plenty of security officers everyday and a person like Mr Wong is hard to come by
He was patient and understanding whilst executing his duties.
office numbers are displayed for fun called 4 times and nobody picked up
Nice place ..bcoz neqr orchard..they call city..but not convinience for me..coz no bus ho to lucky plaza directly..we walk so far till mrt..

This particular Park Connector rans along Sungei Pandan Kechil section of West Coast Road to West Coast Highway.
It's a short 450 metre paved path.
Despite its short length, it's heavily utilised.
Residents living around the estate would walk or jog up and down this short stretch of the Connector in the morning and, in the evening.
The path would be exposed to  full sun in the early morning hrs. And seniors just love walking or do stretching exercises in the sun.

A very pretty park connector that’s perfect for running and cycling, linking West Coast Park with Clementi Woods Park. I often see people jogging here, and with the new HDB and elderly home completed, it’s even more convenient. It connects residents to nearby bus stops, the community centre, and other places easily.

A transition between new and old HDB flats of about 30 years in Teck Whye Estate.
A nice run track along the Pang Sua Park Connector.
My daughter is so happy here! She brings home Challah every Friday and is proud to celebrate Shabbat with her family! She especially loves all the painting and messy play they do throughout the day!

Ganenu provided our family with a truly memorable experience during our summer in Singapore. The staff was exceptional, creating a warm and welcoming atmosphere for our child. The range of activities and the quality of education surpassed our expectations. Most importantly, our child's daily enthusiasm to attend Ganenu is a testament to the nursery's ability to make learning both fun and engaging. Highly recommended!

My Daughter had the most fun and educational summer in Ganenu!
The teachers gave so much love and patience to my daughter, and she was thrilled to go there every morning.
Great jewish education, and the best facilities you can find!

Amazing  caring staff, and modern facilities with wonderful learning through play and unique projects! my son loved attending every morning. I saw so much growth during his time there. Thank you for everything
The school staff is nothing short of amazing! They really care and truly want the best for your child. Fantastic school!

My son went to York Infant Care from when he was 4 months old, up until he recently graduated at 18 months old.

He hit many of his milestones here, grew with them, and always enjoys going to school! Thank you so much to Teacher Inie and her team of teachers for making it fun for him, being creative with activities/events, and sparking his curiosity! He misses all of you and still randomly names his old teachers and friends when I ask him about school.

Our son attended this childcare full day from 3 months to 18+ months, and we had a wonderful experience.

The staff are extremely kind, caring, and attentive, and our son truly loved going every day. Many engaging activities are offered, and we always felt he was very well cared for and supported.

A special thank you to Teacher Inie, who has been absolutely wonderful with our son. We are very grateful for her dedication and kindness.

Highly recommended!

Our twins have been attending the infant care since 5.5 months old, and now at 18 months we have to leave the center, it is truly bittersweet for our family.
At the beginning, we had so many worries and uncertainties—it wasn’t easy to entrust such young babies to others. But over time, that initial anxiety gradually turned into genuine peace of mind and trust. This transformation is all thanks to Teacher Inie and the entire team. Their attentiveness, patience, and professionalism made such a meaningful difference. They supported our children with so much care and also helped us navigate the separation anxiety phase with reassurance and understanding.
What means the most to us is how happy our children have been—walking into the center every day with a smile and have so many happy laughs in the activity photos. Knowing our children feels safe, loved, and excited to be there has been the greatest comfort.
As we say goodbye, we feel a deep sense of gratitude and reluctance to leave. The thoughtful card from the teachers touched us deeply—it truly reflects the warmth and sincerity of the entire team. Thank you, Teacher Inie and everyone at the center, for giving our children such a beautiful and secure start.

We have had a lovely experience at Camelot. Due to an unplanned relocation both my children were in need of short term learning and child interaction. The Camelot team were so helpful in taking on the children and onboarding them immediately. The children have loved their time there, the team were all so friendly and gave us all such reassurance in an uncertain time. I recommend to anyone.

Our son had an amazing experience in the infant care program here. While the center may not look fancy, the teachers and overall environment are incredibly warm, caring, and nurturing. We truly appreciate how flexible the school is and how they focus on meeting each child’s individual needs rather than strictly enforcing rigid rules. The teachers always ensure smooth transitions and make sure our child feels comfortable and happy at school. We especially love that the school emphasizes teaching good values, not just rule-following. Our son enjoys it so much that sometimes, when we come to pick him up, he wants to ring the doorbell just to go back inside!

place_name: Berries World of Learning School (River Valley)
review_text:
I recently visited here for a trial class for my daughter. I actually just walked in and the counter staff named GUOJIN was the one who attended to me. He was very patient and took the time to answer all my questions. He also showed me around and explained the learning environment and how the lesson works, and sharing me a lot of details which I really appreciated.

On the trial day, another staff, JILL also helped us a lot and was very supportive throughout my visit, I found them always willing to help us. The teacher also took the time to share with me how my daughter did during the class, which I really appreciated.

Before this, I had already tried a few different Chinese centers, but Berries really gave me a very good first impression, especially their service.

place_name: Berries World of Learning School (River Valley)
review_text: Teachers are extremely friendly, my son always came back with a smile on his face after class! The free gifts are also extremely well designed! I love their iconic school bag

place_name: Berries World of Learning School (River Valley)
review_text: No regrets sending my child here! one of the best chinese enrichment class for my kids. Teachers are super patient and kind!

place_name: Berries World of Learning School (River Valley)
review_text:
As a parent, I don’t usually review the enrichment courses or tuition centers my children attend, but I would like to write this review to especially thank Guo Jin from your center.

Despite the inconvenient location, I’ve seen him the one who always pick up and drop-off my child at the drop-off point you provided. Sometimes while I’m queuing, I always see him doing his job properly, no matter is hot sun or rainy day and always got smile and say hi to me, very friendly.

Please keep up the great work. Thank you!

place_name: Berries World of Learning School (River Valley)
review_text: My child learn here since P3 now she is P5 and here is convenient for kids to learn mandarin

I had the pleasure of staying at this serviced apartment for two months, and I can confidently say that it exceeded all of my expectations.

The apartment itself was spacious, comfortable, and exceptionally well-equipped such as a washing machine made my long stay which felt very much like a home away from home. The room was well-maintained, cozy, and thoughtfully designed for both short and extended stays.

The location was another major advantage—strategic, convenient, and well-connected to everything I needed during my time there

However what truly made my experience outstanding was the service from every member of the team. Warm, friendly, and always willing to help, even with the smallest requests. Their genuine hospitality made me feel welcomed and cared for throughout my entire stay.

I would also like to give special recognition to RK, the manager, who was incredibly responsive and proactive whenever any issue arose. RK consistently went above and beyond to find solutions quickly and ensure that guests had the best possible experience.

I would not hesitate to recommend this property to anyone visiting Singapore, whether for a short visit or an extended stay. If I have the opportunity to return to Singapore in the future, this will undoubtedly be my first choice again.

Thank you to the entire team for making my stay such a memorable and enjoyable one. Highly recommended! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

We really enjoyed our stay here. The location is perfect—it’s a convenient 10-15 minute walk from three different metro stations, making it very easy to get around the city.
The building has some great perks:
Refreshing pool: Although the water was quite cold, it was a nice way to wake up in the morning.
Outdoor smoking area: A dedicated spot for smokers, which was a plus.
Check-out convenience: They offer luggage storage and even let me take a shower after checking out, which was a lifesaver before my late flight.
Overall, a very practical and comfortable choice. I would definitely stay here again!

Excellent location and service

We spent 8 days at this apartment, and it was so close to being a perfect 5-star experience. The location is unbeatable, with Orchard Road just a 10-minute walk away, and the apartment itself was bright, clean, and offered plenty of storage space.

The service was truly exceptional. When we discovered the fridge was broken upon arrival, the staff responded immediately, and a brand-new one was installed within hours. This level of efficiency is rare and much appreciated!

However, there were a couple of drawbacks that affected our stay. The kitchen was very poorly equipped—there were no plates at all and very few glasses and utensils, which made it difficult to use the space as intended.

The only reason I am not giving this a 5-star review is the persistent issue with moisture and odor. There was a strong smell of dampness and mold throughout the stay. Although an air purifier was running on its highest setting when we arrived, we still had to air out the apartment daily to manage the smell.

If the management can resolve the humidity problem and properly stock the kitchen, this would easily be a 5-star stay. As it stands, it’s a great place that just needs these specific improvements.

(And the night view from the rooftop is amazing!)

Our family (2 seniors, 2 adults, 1 toddler) enjoyed our vacation here. Aside from the reasonable sized room (we booked a 1 bedroom unit - they provided us with 2 beds which we really appreciate), we loved the amenities that the guests/residents can enjoy (esp. the water dispenser). On top of all those pros, the location is perfect! Our senior parents did not have a hard time walking to the bus stops, restaurants/hawker houses, and grocery store (Fair Price).

We enjoyed our 9 days stay, and we will definitely stay here again when we return.

Special shout out to Caden (I hope I got your name correct) for being very helpful and accommodating to our family. In a new city full of strangers, for tourists like us, a friendly face makes a huge difference.

Thank you again!

Our returning stay after last year's 8 nights stay.
Deluxe 1 bedroom. Okay for 2 adults+ 2kids.
Another full double bed was provided(not sofa bed). :)
Nicely updated resting area near the reception. Quite comfy.

The lecturers here are fantastic and genuinely helpful. I wasn't 100% sure about my direction at first and was skeptical about the course fee as it's really affordable compared to others that i have enquired for, but the consultation really helped me clear the fog and set me on the right career path. If you're looking for an MBA that's actually worth the investment, this is it

I had a great experience with my application with SHRM. Seb guided me on my career as i was lost in my career. they don't push you to take courses that doesn't benefit or in my interest. very grateful. Thank you SHRM
Very good experience with the consultation, very patience and guided me according to my work experience and my career goal.
I am student in this school and i think this school very nice, good environment to study i recommend for who want study about hospitality go here to study
这里是英国威尔士一所公立大学在新加坡的分校。这所学校是wrexhamglyndwr（维克汉姆格林多） university

place_name: Chapter One English Boutique Studio @ Oxley
review_text: Teacher Michelle is one of the best teachers my child in upper primary school (up to PSLE) has had the privilege of being taught by! She has extensive and thorough knowledge of the English curriculum & is especially outstanding in supporting the children in their composition and oral exams. What I am most grateful for is her patient, nurturing & caring approach, and I can see her students all love her teaching which is fun & engaging. Thank you Teacher M!
There is a bicycle rental store nearby the bus stop.

This hawker center was the first place I visited right after I landed in Singapore. Personally, I cannot say that this was the best in Singapore since I have not yet tried other places at the time.

As a first time traveler, Newton Hawkers were so welcoming! I had a great time with the vibes by the uncles and aunties offering their products to me. Majority of the crowd are also local, which is a plus for me. This specific stall (in the picture) was the best (service-wise).

In the photo, I have made friends with Auntie Nico and Uncle John. I don’t know their relationship or if they own the store but they treated me so well and even offered to show me how they cook their food (I told them that I own a restaurant and was curious about how they cook their food).

In the end of the night, I asked them to take a picture with me and they did. Auntie Nico also used her phone to get a selfie with me lol. Fun times!

There’s always something beautiful about hard working people and everytime you get a chance to talk to them, do it!

WILL ALWAYS VISIT HERE EVERYTIME I GO TO SINGAPORE!

Newton Food Centre is a must-visit in Singapore. Lively, authentic, and packed with incredible food options, it’s everything you want from a classic hawker centre.

The variety is outstanding — from local favourites to late-night comfort dishes, there’s something for everyone. Prices are very reasonable, portions are generous, and the flavours are bold and authentic. It’s the kind of place where you can try multiple dishes in one visit and still want to come back again.

The atmosphere is busy and energetic, especially in the evenings, but that’s part of the charm. Despite the crowds, there’s plenty of seating and the space is well laid out. Being open 24 hours is a huge bonus, whether you’re after a late supper, early breakfast, or anything in between.

Great spot for travellers wanting a genuine local experience, groups with different tastes, or anyone keen to eat well without breaking the bank. Casual, vibrant, and full of character — exactly what a Singapore hawker centre should be.

🍜 Huge variety of food
🌶️ Authentic local flavours
💰 Affordable prices
⏰ Open 24 hours
🎉 Lively atmosphere

Highly recommended.

• Food
The food selection at this center is a reliable choice for local flavors. The chicken wings and satay are particular highlights, especially when paired with a cold beverage. Another notable mention is the Gong Gong, which is a dish that often prompts a visit to this location. The flavors are authentic and provide a satisfying taste of local seafood and grilled items.

• Service & Atmosphere
The atmosphere is lively and typical of a popular open-air food center in Singapore. It offers a vibrant setting that is suitable for casual gatherings. While the environment can get busy and crowded, it remains a practical and energetic spot for a late-night meal or a social drink. The service varies by stall but is generally efficient and functional.

Well known hawker centre that once upon a time catered mostly to tourists.

But locals are now crowding the place because of the good food and the variety and because the Singapore Tourism Board has successfully clamped down some seafood stalls touting and scamming patrons, especially tourists, with exorbitant prices.

This is one hawker centre where you will find people from all over the world savouring local street food of all races in Singapore which originated from China, India,  Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand,  etc and even the US.

Beer by the jugs are served here.

Most stalls are closed for lunch. But it is usually quite difficult to get a table during dinner time, especially on weekends. Sharing table is the norm.  So be willing to do so.

Queuing at some stalls to collect your own order is also the norm.

This hawker centre, including the free toilets, are very clean and airy. There are basins all over for patrons to wash their hands before and after meals
meals.

Pay when you order. In cash or via your phone transfer.

There is an adjoining carpark. A huge one. But it can still be hard to find a parking space during dinner time.

The Newton MRT is very near by.

A true foodie paradise, with flavors from across Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, and China all coming together in one place. You’ll find yourself eating alongside both locals and tourists, which adds to the energy and authenticity. Not too crowded on a Monday night, making it easy to explore. Highlights included the fresh sugar cane juice, black pepper crab, Singapore chili stingray, fried squid, and the classic Singapore “carrot cake.” A great way to experience the diversity of the region’s street food culture.

The opening hours is wrong.
Im now 3pm sat eating here after meeting at bizhub.
Tried the lean meat soup. $6
Uncle very friendly,
I say 1st time come, what is nice, he intro me lean meat soup.
Soup very tasty, non msg type.
Really can taste bone boiled soup.
Next time if i come again will try his kway chap.
The rice he gave me got kwaychap sauce. Taste great.

This is my first time writing review as I felt his behaviour was really uncalled for.

I went there during lunch time around 1pm, we got lucky and found seats.

I went to buy my food, my colleagues was sitting at the table with our belongings. This guy (I assume is the drink store owner or the food court owner) came over and told them some stuff, I could not really hear as I was at the food store. I only visibly saw him push the water bottle across the table, almost dropping to the floor.

I was shocked, when I walked over with my food, he already walked off. I asked my colleagues what happened? They said he mentioned cannot have outside drinks or food, which I acknowledged and did not touch my water whilst eating the whole time.

Half way when we were eating, he came over again. He started raising his voice almost to the extent of shouting and visibly looking annoyed. He said you want me tell you how many times cannot have outside drinks. I got a little pissed as we were happily chatting and he came over shouting (super uncalled for).

I straight told him I did not drink my water the whole time after the first interaction with my colleagues. He say ya never drink but we sell mineral water too (mind you, I took my water bottle down from my office to take medicine after eating, and I did not know cannot have outside food and drinks, but after I know from him, I did not even drink my water whilst eating).

Then he proceed to continue and say then hide it, keep it. I was like keep where?? I did not bring a bag, just my phone and a jacket which I am wearing due to the cold weather and feeling down the weather. He proceeded to say on the floor.

I was utterly shocked, my water bottle put on the floor?? Even when I did not even have the means to drink it while eating there?

Before I can respond, he took my bottle and left without saying anything else. Guess where did he put? On top the return tray shelf where everyone return their dirty utensils, bowls, plates and tray there. I felt so disgusted. I left the place without taking my bottle back (thank god is just a disposable mineral water bottle).

From this I am utterly turned off by his rude attitude and unhinged behaviour. Taking people's belonging without permission is considered theft already (when I already acknowledge to him I never drink) and speaking to customers with such rude attitude is not it. Honestly, we would not have came to eat here if it was not because it was raining heavily outside.

Those coming here for the first time, beware of this coffeeshop’s policy of NO OUTSIDE DRINKS. I learnt it the hard way. Came in after lunch hours (2-ish), sat down and bought some food. I had a drink brought in from outside but it was inside my bag. I took my drink out on the table, but only after when i wanted to reach out to drink, an auntie working at the cleaning station appeared suddenly in front of my table and tried to stop me from drinking outside drinks. At first there was some confusion as i couldn’t register what she was saying in Mandarin, then she repeatedly and vehemently spoke in English that outside drinks are not allowed in that coffeeshop. She was so rude, raising her voice at me. Ok - chill auntie. Don’t be a police. Some people are here for the first time and don’t know the “rules” being enforced so tightly there. Just be careful, if you bring your own personal bottled drinks, i don’t know what might happen in this case (if she chooses to enforce this). As for the food, can’t expect much choices at 2pm - but i was hungry, ate and left. Taste and price is ok, maybe a bit expensive. Probably will think twice about coming here ever again. Also, the drinks stall must have been so threatened by outside business if they think “no outside drinks” is a good strategy to maintain their business. Maybe try reflecting first on your attitudes, pricing, and customer service.

Classic Lontong for breakfast. Nasi Padang for lunch. Good spread and generous serving too. Nice and green veggies well seasoned and skillfully prepared. Chicken tasted like chilli crab. Shiok.

Back again with bee hoon base for lunch. The Beef Rendang is so tender and tasty. Shiok. Not cheap but generous serving.
