Google said it's open but it's closed. Me & my small daughter waiting for the bus n suddenly it was raining.We noticed the door was open.  We just go inside n asked whether can sit or not. One of the guy said can but after a min a old lady came and chase us. She was very harsh and not in good manner. All the express bus company should not choose this place as a pick up point since we don't have any place to sit n wait.

One of the most underrated food courts right in the city centre! Not your usual SG-style setup, this spot brings in 7-8 legit street food brands straight from KL. Think Pork Noodles 猪肉粉, Bak Kut Teh, Fish Head Noodle 鱼头米粉, Wanton Mee 云吞面, Hokkien Mee 福建面, Nasi Lemak and more - all done the proper MY style.

For MYsians, it’s the closest thing to a taste of home without flying to KL.

For everyone else, it’s like a mini getaway for your taste buds - familiar dishes, but with that distinct KL twist you won’t find in your everyday food court.

Price wise, average $10-15 per pax (drinks included). Slightly above the average food court price in SG but value for money, as they give a lot of liao!
We loved this food court for the Malaysian foods served. They are authentic and tasty. We had pork noodles, hokkian mee and wanton mee. We come here frequently whenever we are in Singapore.

The fish soup with beehoon was not bad! The soup itself was quite tasty and tasted a bit like an instant noodle soup that I really like, so you can be rest assured that the soup has flavour. As for the fish, there wasn't any fishy taste (which was a relief) and the texture was okay. Overall, it's not bad and I wouldn't mind eating this again if I'm in the area.

Ordered the kaya toast set from the drinks stall. To be honest, didnt have any high expectations as it was from a food court. Would normally eat kaya toast set from yakun. However, after having this, it is equally amazing. (Or even better)

From the picture itself, I have never seen such huge and well cooked half boiled eggs. Just look at the yolk. The bread is also toasted perfectly and the teh o peng is thick with enough flavor. Come give it a try and you will never regret.

It is a nice area to sit down and dine. There was not a lot of crowd even though it was lunch time. The Bak Kut Teh is not the peppery kind. The service crew told me that it was alright to ask for a refill. The you tiao tasted really great after being soaked in the soup and then dipped in garlic.

I was in the area for lunch with my friends and was drawn by the high reviews here!
Very chill vibes all around albeit limited seats! It's also a nice escape from the heat.. Tried the hot mocha and it was decent, though I would definitely return again to try the coffee.

disappointing.

The coffee itself tastes normal, but the whole composition + place + customer service makes it go mid or below. Go aware you’re paying full price for half cup of watery coffee. It’s nothing special, there’s nothing to be cherished, then don’t make it that small just to make it look expensive product. It’s not like i’m drinking a small, sweet and iced cup of dirty latte with infusion of flavors.

As for the customer service, that’s the first thing you should ask yourself before starting your business: “will i deal with people? do i like people?” if 2nd is NO, then just don’t. I exchanged almost no words with barista and i felt like i was talking to a 30yo uncle. I understand many orders was being taken, people don’t need to be in a good mood the whole day, we’re humans right? I’m also not asking for chit chat, but i can’t simply use this excuse while many other places just care their customers with excellence, empathy. I’m also a human btw

My review would be better if i ordered using a machine and there was a wall between the coffee bar and saloon

final review:

for a business in cbd i’m aware of the average price. i’m willing to pay, explore, try, but there are many other options that are worth the price and i’m not only talking about the cup of coffee
Really good coffee!
I’ve visited on several occasions. Decent coffee and location, but unfortunately the service (depending on who’s on shift) can be unpleasant.

Coffee was not bad.
Not to me, but I saw a customer walked out because the staff could not help to take order. Only machine can serve customer.

place_name: Xiao Long Kan Hotpot Clarke Quay 小龙坎火锅克拉码头
review_text:
Had a really enjoyable meal at Xiao Long Kan. The staff were all very friendly, attentive, and had great attitudes throughout the dinner. They even warned us about the spice level in a very funny and engaging way, which made the whole experience more enjoyable.

The food was fresh and tasted great. Honestly, the spicy level was still quite manageable and very shiok. Service was efficient and the staff were always checking in on us without being intrusive.

Only downside would be that the prices are on the higher side, but the good service and quality of food made it worth it. Will definitely be back again for more.

place_name: Xiao Long Kan Hotpot Clarke Quay 小龙坎火锅克拉码头
review_text: It was very delicious and the staffs were so friendly, will come back here again for other gatherings!!

place_name: Xiao Long Kan Hotpot Clarke Quay 小龙坎火锅克拉码头
review_text: Foods really fresh, and the service is excellent. Great place to spend time with family & friends. They have cocktails at this Clarke quay outlet as well.

place_name: Xiao Long Kan Hotpot Clarke Quay 小龙坎火锅克拉码头
review_text:
We enjoy the new broth - masutake mushroom. It does have rich fungi taste that is sweet.

Soon hock : is incredibly fresh. After cooking, it melts in mouth!

Prawns are fresh and sweet.

Spicy pork ribs are as good as as ever, it is a must order.

New dish- beef with Thai basil is interesting. Beef is tender, the Thai basil makes it feels like deconstructed beef pho.

We like the pickles. The acidity helps to cut down richness. Their condiment now have new addition - fish mint which adds an authentic Chong Qing touch.

Special thanks to the manager. She is gregarious and attentive to our needs.

place_name: Xiao Long Kan Hotpot Clarke Quay 小龙坎火锅克拉码头
review_text:
Cosy n nice atmosphere.
Friendly service and yummy yummy dishes. A great place to hang out.

Clarke Quay Central is a great mall with a fantastic view of the Boat Quay riverside 🌊🚤✨. It has a wide variety of shops to explore, and you can even find some discounted items at the first-floor lobby—perfect for a little bargain shopping 🛍️💸.
What I love most is its convenient location 📍. After shopping, you can easily walk over to Boat Quay, Fort Canning Park, the National Museum of Singapore, and other nearby attractions. It’s a nice spot to relax, shop, and enjoy the scenic surroundings all in one place 🌿🏙️.
Overall, a convenient and enjoyable place to visit if you’re in the area 👍😊

It has been a while since I was last here - at this, in the Chinatown area, shopping mall that is patronised by locals and tourists alike. While I have always found the layout and connectivity inside the building to be at times, somewhat "confusing", I can see that it is still fairly popular - helped by a Don Don Donki and its food stalls at its basement level one. In fact, I was here this morning to check out its recently (?) opened food court - Sinfoodie, and I was impressed by the choice of food stalls thereat and the dining area; although admittedly, I was there well before the peak lunch hour and I did wonder what it would look like then. Anyway, I see myself returning to this shopping mall for Sinfoodie.

​Clarke Quay Central is undeniably awkwardly built, featuring a quirky layout that defies standard mall logic. The placement of shops and odd corners often cuts off the natural flow of foot traffic, making spontaneous discovery quite a challenge. It isn't the best place for a casual stroll if you’re looking to explore new stores.

Clarke Quay Central is a vibrant mall popular with both tourists and locals, thanks to its prime location next to the nightlife scene along the Singapore River.
The mall offers a good mix of cafes and restaurants, with some dining spots featuring scenic river views.

In the basement, you’ll also find the bustling Japanese supermarket Don Don Donki, which is a draw on its own.
Conveniently situated near several hotels and directly above Clarke Quay MRT station, it’s especially lively and atmospheric from the evening onwards, when the riverside lights come alive.

Went down last week to Taiyo Bread Shop at level 3 for a afternoon tea break. The cake is sweet & delicious. Price reasonable for this area. Environment is quiet for chill & relax for solo ranger cafe hopper like me 😃

This store offers a wide range of North and South Indian groceries, including fresh vegetables. It is relatively new, well-maintained, and offers competitive pricing for the area. They also provide convenient delivery services.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
Tried some of their food during boutique fair 2025. It was ok, but it’s really nothing special considering the price. The har gao and siew Mai are quite mushy. Kong Bak Pao isn’t fantastic either, just normal. Noodles and dumpling are alright.

Tried their food again at their original location and still not impressed with it especially the Har Gao. Skin is too thick and meat is not substantial. The rest are also mediocre.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
Bought the festive premium pork feasting platter for christmas and the only thing that is premium is the word premium printed on the label. I followed the instructions on the packaging for preparation. Instructions were easy to follow, but that was the only good thing in this meal.

The ribs were extremely tough - worse than the ones you get at cold storage. Under seasoned and had to add my own bbq sauce to make it somewhat edible. Ate 2 ribs and threw the rest away.

The porchetta is fatty and bland. Ate one mouthful and threw the rest away.

Sausages were too salty.

Meatballs were ok, but you can probably get the same quality from the super market.

For $82, you’re better off spending your money elsewhere. I dare say Mcdonald’s and Saizeriya tastes better than this.

This might be the worst Christmas meal I’ve ever had in my life. Perhaps maybe even one of the worst meals I’ve ever had in my life.

The company should stop selling these sets immediately.

Would have given -100 stars on google reviews if google allowed me to, but I guess I’ll have to settle for 1 star which in my opinion, is too generous.

Thanks for ruining my Christmas. You guys should rename your company to “The Grinch”.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text: Ah Ma’s handmade Yong Tau Foo it’s really bad. I couldn’t even go for second bite. The fish paste inside was really hard as well.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
First time online ordering from them. Usually I went to the physical store.

Have to compliment them on how well the items were packed. The green were in a recycle bag And there’s ice pack in between each layers of frozen items.

My daughter loves their Siew Mai and I love the garlic chilli. 🥰 Oh! The pork ribs are really fresh too. Used it to cook bbq ribs and it’s really yummy.
Trying some new items this round. Looking forward to more purchase with them.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text: The Signature Pineapple Tart tin is not sealed. Inside it is a ziplock bag (also with no safety seal) containing the tarts. Quality of the tarts is below expectation too, since the small tin is priced at almost $28. Feels like this shop had bought some cheap tarts and repacked it for resale.

Went to the outlet (Tagore) to place an order for CNY, to make sure that delivery would be in place for reunion dinner.

Food was delivered just before the scheduled delivery time, which was exactly what we needed. Quality was good and tasted fresh. The fishballs and beef balls were especially well received.

Will use them again next time we do a steamboat.

What a great wet market, combined with Sheng Siong, lots of independent small stores, food court. The prices are fantastic compare to NTUC Fair price at the wet market. There’s heap of hawker food too, especially busy were prata, chicken rice, Lor mee and bee hoon and curry chicken. Nice atmosphere in the weekend and old nostalgia where your ah-Ma would love to shop here. Just wished it had more meat and vegetable stores in the wet market section. Good place to show the kids and spend the morning in the weekend.

I highly recommend this butcher!  Prime Pork ribs are very high quality, smooth, tender and fresh.  Their minced pork is the perfect meat/ fat ratio, very fresh.  No rancid smell at all.  I’ve also purchased their deboned chicken (quarter) 500gm pack many times- used it for grilling, steaming, braising.  Also recommend their pork meat balls- very tasty, has cuttlefish in it.  They deliver the items in an insulated box, full of ice packs to keep the meat cold and safe.  Everything is vacuum packed.  I’m so pleased with their service.  Their pricing is also competitive.  The thing with going to the wet market physically, you still need to repack into your desired portions upon coming home, so ordering from quan shui really saves me a lot of time and energy.

I always love going to the wet markets but cannot afford the time to wake up super early to get the freshest produce! Love that they deliver to your doorstep and even give the option to recycle the ice packs/boxes. The selection of produce is great, quality is really good and worth every bit of the slight premium!! Fruits & meat are super fresh! Ready to eat section is great for quick meals too! 100% will recommend!

We cooked with the wild caught balai threadfin for porridge, Silverfern short rib shabu shabu for Korean beef Bulgogi, silverfern ribeye steak for Beef bowl. It was amazing! 👌

We’ve been buying from Quan Shui Wet Market for a few times, and each time a satisfying one. Their items are fresh and clean, reached us in good condition and packaging. Complimentary delivery is reliable and good! Joyce from customer service is friendly and helpful. So far we’ve purchased almost all types of what they are selling and we’ll still purchase from them for long. Thank you Quan Shui Market!

BFT RV is one of my happy places.
The session is less than one hour, I can switch off my brain and get the workout done. It’s very efficient and effective.

The coaches are all so friendly, kind, and encouraging. The members are fun and friendly. Love the community.

Douglas is always patient and helpful. Camey is the best! She pays attention to everyone, remembers our strengths and weaknesses, and pushes us to level up. It’s impressive. I really appreciate her dedication and hard work! 💪💙
thanks to all of you!

Smells really nice here and has a very pleasant and homely vibe! Want to commend Jeff for his knowledge and willingness to help too. Camey brings the vibe too

In the 1990's this was only a sports hall for Badminton and a football pitch, if memory serves me right. Visited it recently for my 1st pickleball game, and was stunned at how the facilities have grown with an Olympic pool, proper fenced pitches, 6 pickle balls courts, basket ball, gym, improved indoor badminton courts and more. They have done a stellar job, with the old and new coming together, and constantly upgrading the facilities, never letting this get too run down. Toilets are the testament to how well/run down a facilities are. Lockers and water coolers are also well situated to make this a facility where we would even invite overseas friends to come use. Great Job Active SG.

Fantastic facility. Perhaps the best ActiveSG facility I have been to. Sky high bar has been set. There are about 6 pickleball courts, a futsal court, a hockey pitch, fitness gym, full length swimming pool, Mcs, and that is probably only half the place (didn't explore the other half). Thank you, ActiveSG!
Played pickleball there multiple times. Great courts, though court 2 needs some maintenance - surface is delaminating in patches, causing a few dead spots. Otherwise, all good.
Went there to take my body composition test and good service and fast!
Hockey pitch is decent

A special mention to RK and Allan, who were extremely helpful and provided excellent housekeeping support along with outstanding customer service throughout our stay.
Thank you to the entire Oxley team for a wonderful experience. We will definitely recommend this place and look forward to returning again.

Been watch movies here for 30+ years. Always enjoy the movie experience at Shaw. Happy with the newly renovated upper halls. The lumiere grand is very comfortable and aircon is like winter, must wear jacket. Newly renovated toilet. However overall 4/5.

Both are great for families, but I prefer Lido for its bigger space and side cushions for kids. Balestier has a small play tent, which is nice but more compact overall. Note that Lido gets fully dark when the show starts.

Overall, both offer a positive experience.

*No vegan options or non dairy milk alternatives for premiere guests.* :(
I have visited this Cinema twice. I saw Avatar Fire and Ash on the Imax screen and it was a really great first experience. It has a giant screen and very comfortable seats.
I recently went to watch Project Hail Mary on the Premiere screen. The staff are very friendly and helpful, the seats were comfortable and private. However, I was very dissappointed with the food and drink offerings. Food and drink was supposed to be included up to 15 dollars. I am vegan and there were not any vegan options. I asked for a Matcha latte with Oat milk and was told I would have to go downstairs and purchase one. I ended up having the natcha with hot water only and it made me feel sick, so I couldn't drink it. It would be great to considor having alternative, non dairy options and perhaps a little vegan menu or a few options, as guests have paid for this in their ticket.

Watched Hail Mary and it was worth it. The picture quality and sound was good. They also have McDonalds and coffee shop inside the theatre. Overall a good movie experience.
Well connected too as it’s in middle of the town.

I recently caught the 11:15 PM screening of Devil Wears Prada 2 at Shaw Lido Premiere, and it was a bit of a mixed bag that ended on a very high note. Here is the breakdown of the experience:

The Pre-Show (The "Lounge")
We arrived early at 10:45 PM but were told we couldn't enter until 11:00 PM. I’ll be honest: the "Premiere Lounge" was a letdown. It’s marketed as an exclusive perk, but it’s essentially just a basic waiting area with standard tables and chairs. There’s nothing particularly "premium" or luxurious about the decor or the vibe while you're waiting to go in.

The Cinema & Seating
Once 11:00 PM hit, the experience pivoted completely. The staff were incredibly courteous and guided us to our seats. If you’ve flown SIA Business Class, these seats will feel very familiar!

Comfort: The leather seats feature full electrical adjustments for the headrest, back, and footrest. You can slide down into a full bed position.

Amenities: Each seat comes with a plush blanket and a dedicated food tray.

Tech: The screen was crisp and well-lit, and the audio is easily among the best sound systems I’ve experienced in Singapore.

F&B Experience
The ticket comes with a $15 F&B voucher, which is a nice touch. Ordering is seamless—just scan the QR code on your tray.

What we ordered: Popcorn, chicken sliders, and a Mocha.

Service: Everything was delivered directly to our seats. The food was surprisingly good for cinema fare, and the sliders were a highlight.

Summary
While the lounge needs a serious upgrade to match the "Premiere" branding, the actual in-hall experience is top-notch. If you want to watch Miranda Priestly's return in absolute comfort, this is the way to do it.

Pros:
Business-class style reclining seats (full flat bed).
Excellent sound and projection quality.
Attentive and kind staff.

Cons:
Underwhelming lounge area.

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

Very clean and movie quality very clear and good!👍🏻✨

Overall very nice, staffs also very friendly. Bought tickets through machine easy to use.

Nice country club along Changi coast road . There are 2 locations so many guest are a little confuse or got lost Check the address . Huge carpark which is free

Looking forward to see some improvement of this precious court.

Great community club to go to. Many activities and lessons available such as martial arts, guzheng classes and ceramic art class which I attend is extremely popular. This community club is located in a historical building, and the interesting architecture is not like any old community club. There is a badminton court and basketball court too

Nice amenities with quite clean toilets. The Badminton Hall's floor is tiled tho. It would be good if it was laminated wood. The centre also closes quite early compared to other Community Centres. It would be better if the centre opens till a later time.  The hall's cleanliness could be improved as there were many shuttlecock feathers that littered the place. Overall the centre is located very well near the central area of SG. The rate is slightly more expensive compared to other community centres in the west which is definitely acceptable. The entrances are glass windows, which is definitely eye pleasing. We played for about 3hrs long, it is quite ventilated which is excellent. Overall a good experience and might consider booking again for friend meetups. Recommended!
Given the small space.. Sometimes I wonder why they even bother building one. And since it's built without any shelter, the slides are burning hot during sunny days.

Very small,  fence around to keep children inside.
Clearly sunshine was not considered when built up the play groun. Even before lunch time,  the slide is burning hot. Parents be careful

It was a breezy morning on December 8, so we decided to take a walk along the Alexandra Park Connector again. The canal was exceptionally clean, beautifully reflecting the surroundings like a mirror. Kudos to the dedicated groundskeepers for maintaining such a pristine environment and keeping Singapore the clean city it’s known to be. Appreciation also to the Friends of the Park for their continuous efforts in upkeeping this lovely space.
Nice pathway for walks - saw several flora and fauna along the way.

It was a nice walk from Queenstown MRT to Robertson Quay via the Alexandra Park Connector. The stretch of Alexandra Park Connector is not long, and the portion starting from the condominium The Crest is very scenic next to the Singapore River. The Park Connector was not crowded and we had good weather resulting in a pleasant and easy walk which took slightly more than an hour

Wonderful jogging 🏃‍♂️ and cycling 🚴‍♂️ path along the canal and river; a picturesque path to get from Redhill to CBD along the Singapore River.

At the end of the Redhill Tanglin Road end, there is a tranquil park built on top of the canal and is a great place for personal space exercises or reading a book.

If you need food & beverage, there are many options throughout the several exits.

Alexandra Park Connector connect to Queenstown on the West and all the way to Clarke Quay on the East. Along the way you will enjoy the green scenery of the florals and fauna’s as well as some modern skyscrapers. This way can also lead you to Gardens by the Bay and all the way to East Coast Park.

I am currently 6 months into a part-time software engineering diploma, and it has been an eye opening adventure. Although I haven't reached the finish line yet, the journey has been filled with growth and discovery. This program is a fantastic fit for anyone curious about the digital world; while a little coding background helps, the most important requirement is a willingness to learn.

Because many of us balance this course with full-time jobs, the lecturers are incredibly supportive. They truly "get it." I’ve found the environment to be one where curiosity is celebrated; no question is too small. The instructors are remarkably open, always searching for the most creative and clear ways to help us grasp complex ideas.

Even as I continue working toward my completion, I can already see how much this experience has reshaped my thinking. It’s more than just learning to code it’s about finding new ways to solve problems.

Clean and modern environment.
Great atmosphere for students.
Good and clear motor vehicles routes map.

Is Republic Polytechnic Closed Down?

Lack of response to my enquiry. I sent an email days ago.

In addition, I tried calling the main contact number and was left on hold for over 30 minutes without any assistance. This level of service is unacceptable. Our time as customers is equally valuable and should be respected.

I strongly urge your management to look into this matter and ensure that proper communication and responsiveness are maintained moving forward.

My Son started with Swim Monkey when he was 4+ years. While he was afraid of water before, his coaches (specially coach Ross and coach Zane) has been very supportive to guide him and bring him to a stage where now he's learning and progressing confidently within few months. Every week he eagerly waits for the swimming session to start. Coaches have been very good and friendly with kids, patiently attending to them and knows the techniques to keep the attention of the kids during the lessons.
I wouldn't hesitate to recommend Swim Monkey school to any parent who is looking for a convenient, professional and flexible swimming class for their children.

We had a very disappointing experience with this swim school. Over the years, my son’s coach kept changing, which made it hard for him to build rapport and progress consistently.

The final straw was when they abruptly stopped lessons with my son just because we wanted to take more time to reconsider whether he was ready for their swim test. This decision felt unreasonable and not in the best interest of a young learner.

I would have expected more stability, understanding, and professionalism from a school entrusted with children’s learning and confidence. Unfortunately, we will not be returning and cannot recommend them to other parents.

If your child misses a class for any reason like illness, holiday or bad weather, you’re required to do a make-up lesson within the same month. If not, the lesson is forfeited.

You’re also expected to give one month’s notice in advance if you plan to skip a lesson. This is pitched as being in the best interest of the child and ensuring their ongoing learning. However, the same level of commitment from the instructor does not always apply.

They often cancelled with just a couple of days’ notice for unspecified reasons or to teach a group class. You’re then left chasing the instructor to arrange a make-up lesson, which can be very frustrating because you’ll often find there are no convenient times left and you’re expected to fit into their schedule.

If you can’t, you’re left with the option to extend future lessons by 15–25 minutes—which isn’t always the most sensible thing for a young kid in the Singapore heat that can’t swim or touch the bottom in a deep pool.

You pay upfront, adhere to their numerous one-way T&C’s, and work around your individual instructor’s whims and availability. That might be fine in a cheaper group class, but at this price point for private lessons, I expected more flexibility and respect for customers’ time and circumstances.

EDIT: 19-04-26. In response to the owner's reply below:

My full name is at the top of the review. Anyone reading this can scroll up and see it.

Sorry I won’t be posting my child’s name on a public Google page thanks. A business that works with children asking a parent to do so speaks for itself.

The review raised specific points about cancellations, notice periods, and one-sided terms. None of them have been addressed. I’ll leave readers to draw their own conclusions.

My son has been swimming with Coach Ross and Coach Baby for over 3 years now. We have be extremely happy with his progress. Under their guidance he became a proficient swimmer in a very short period of time and has enjoyed the camaraderie built by both Coaches with the other children. They also taught my son to swim in Open Water and he has competed in aquathlons which has been a great experience. The dedication that Coach Ross and Coach Baby give to the children is second to none. I highly recommend them.

Coach Ross has been an inspiration for my 8-year-old daughter.  She had lessons in the past but never really managed to swim properly.  In 6 short months, Coach Ross got her swimming laps confidently with techniques that look like a seasoned swimmer.  I will not hesitate to recommend this swim school to anyone who is looking for one! ~ Sheryl Goh

place_name: Kumon Learning Centre - Great World (Maths, English and Chinese Enrichment)
review_text: So far I'm very happy with my child's progress here. The teachers and staff working here are all very friendly.

place_name: Kumon Learning Centre - Great World (Maths, English and Chinese Enrichment)
review_text: We have grown together with Kumon and have learnt to appreciate the benefits it has for our child. The teachers are approachable and responsible!

place_name: Kumon Learning Centre - Great World (Maths, English and Chinese Enrichment)
review_text: Through Kumon, it's easy to establish a good learning routine. All the teachers are very kind as well. Thanks a lot :)
Nice community library near benches at void deck

So glad to see it being filled up since the last reviews! Hope that this will grow into a hugggggeeeee community library someday

Just left a stack of books and hope that they will be read well by the community :)
Good solution interim before Tengah gets own library :)
Great idea 👍

Passed by here for a transit. It was interconnected, with a nice walk to the respective MRT lines. Everything there is clean and polished. The toilet is immaculate too, with enough cubicles for a few.

The transiting is just a few minutes walk, which is a pretty good exercise. There's also a travelator to walk faster if you're in a rush. Overall, a tranquil environment for a hustling MRT station. Nice MRT station!

Its a lively street in the evenings. Singapore sleeps early so these streets also sleep off 😴.  Visit during Daytime and evenings eat the Bread Ice-cream and have a nice time . Some road side music too .

I love this new Brown Line Mrt, Orchard Boulevard, huge clean Area, really easy to get to Wheelock place, Shaw Center, Paterson through underground, few stops from Orchard to Marina Bay Sands, from Marina to Changi Airport only 1 stop amazing, really easy to travel to the Airport.

🟫 The Thomson–East Coast Line ( "TEL" ) is a high-capacity Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) line in Singapore.

🟫 Coloured brown on the MRT rail map, it is fully underground. ✔️When fully completed, the 6th line on Singapore's MRT network will serve 32 stations.

🤎  The TEL line runs from Woodlands in the North, down to Orchard Road area, down to Shenton Way financial district area, and then it goes all the way to Katong and East Coast side ( the eastern areas of Singapore).

✔️🟤 To access the Orchard MRT Station's TEL "Thomson East Coast line"- ✅️ you go to B1 level of Ion mall and the exits and entrances of the TEL line are there.

🟤🤎 The Orchard TEL ( Thomson East-Coast line ) station is connected directly to B1 level of Ion mall.

***************************************
🟫 From Orchard MRT Station, I usually take this brown TEL line back to Caldecott Station which is at perimeters of Toa Payoh Town ( where I live).

Been here more in the last 10 days than anywhere else in Singapore! It is our gateway to exploration of the city.
The station is clean and very well maintained.
Signage is clear and not confusing, especially after you’ve been a few times.
TIP: make sure you take note of entry points from street level so you know how to get back out and back to your hotel
Also, if you have a travel money card with Mastercard or Visa capability then just use it to tap through the turnstiles rather than buying a travel card or tourist pass. So far we have paid about SD$17.60 in 10days.
The train system is amazing, reliable and constant. Longest wait time to board a train has been 4mins!
a very useful bus stop as it not only serves four bus services, but is also conveniently located near many houses, allowing travelling to be easy for those who love nearby!
Across the road is Valley Point Mall, and there are many people waiting at the bus stop.

This new bus stop can be a bit disorienting for those unfamiliar with the River Valley area. While it seems conveniently located, I was surprised by the distance to Great World City after disembarking.

However, the interconnected underground pathways accessible from the bus stop provide a well-established network, offering convenient access to various destinations, including Great World City (Exit 3).

The people there are very nice, also with the AC, the place is very comfortable.
One of the best food courts in Singapore Poly
The western food store not bad. Had grilled chicken breast plus two sides. Good quality food value for money
I got the cheese omelette curry rice for only $4! The food here is cheap, affordable and tasty!
The Japanese curry rice was very good and worth the money. Katy’s is crispy and curry is taste. 😋
Really good food court. I get iced milo from there every day :) and has air con ! Could have more halal food options though.

Really nice place!! The atmosphere was super cool and cozy and the food was great as well! I think the waffles were so amazing :))

Super nice and friendly staff too! Definitely come try it out 😋😋
We keep coming back to Vibez, the food is great with some unique menu items. Especially enjoyed the drunken noodles, pineapple rice and tom yam soup.

Visited on a Sunday afternoon. Was not crowded and very chill. Vibes in the place is really chillest with the right music to match.

Food here is Thai Fusion with alot of healthy and vegan options. Tried their Thai food, spicy stir fried noodles and ladna. Both very tasty. We also had Thai Ceviche, which was perfect for a hot afternoon.

Service could have been a little more attentive, but that was by no means a negative point, it was a lazy Sunday afternoon after all.
Will visit again for sure.

We ordered mostly vegetarian. This restaurant serves lots of vegetarian options!
Food was flavorful and portions are good for sharing. Even the basque burnt cheesecake was delicious.

Stumbled upon Vibez while looking for a cozy spot for a girls’ lunch, and it was such a lovely find. The Thai restaurant has a modern yet inviting interior, a relaxed ambience, and genuinely warm service. I had the Basil Chicken with Rice, which was delicious, and their set lunches are great value. Perfect place for a casual catch-up, will definitely be back!

Paragon has been a familiar hangout spot for me for many years, and it’s still one of Orchard Road’s most dependable malls. It has a classy, spacious feel and remains a great place to spend time whether you’re shopping, meeting friends, or just taking a slow walk indoors

There’s a strong mix of retail stores, good eateries, and practical conveniences all under one roof. I also like that it has Cold Storage Gold / Fresh for quality groceries, which makes it more than just a shopping mall. The presence of medical clinics adds to the convenience too

This is a modern upscale 6 level shopping mall right in the heart of Orchard Road. Most people who shop here knows what they want and they just go straight to the shop. Others come here for their lunch or simply have tea here for nice chit chats. Now’s the time for Chinese New Year shopping so the mall is beautifully decorated this year !! I love the beautiful lights !!! And can feel the Chinese New Year vibes and mood !!! I came here to buy my CNY clothes at Metro Paragon on level two. It’s been a wonderful and fruitful trip !! Bought 6 pieces 👍👍👍💗💗💗

A wonderful and good shopping experience having good brands like Prada and Gucci in them. It also has amazing food and restaurant options on the basement level. The best food you could find is the fish and Coast company which serves authentic fish to perfection and also the area has some good Italian food joints, and some nice Asian and local cuisine as well. It also has a nice supermarket to buy daily essentials and fresh fruits.

Stepping into Paragon feels like entering a high-end gallery. The layout is spacious and intuitive, featuring polished marble floors and a glass-fronted facade that has become a landmark on Orchard Road.

​Key Highlights
-​Luxury Fashion: The mall is synonymous with high fashion, housing iconic multi-story flagships for Gucci, Prada, and Givenchy, alongside luxury heavyweights like Saint Laurent, Balenciaga, and Loewe.

-​The "Junior" Floor: Level 5 is a dedicated sanctuary for families, featuring high-end children’s labels like Armani Junior and Petit Bateau, complemented by a free indoor playground and top-tier enrichment centers.

-​Exquisite Dining: From the Michelin-starred Crystal Jade Golden Palace to the legendary Imperial Treasure Super Peking Duck, the culinary offerings lean heavily toward quality. For something more casual yet chic, PS.Cafe and the basement's Din Tai Fung are perennial favorites.

-​Medical Wellness: Uniquely, the mall is connected to Paragon Medical, a premium tower housing world-class specialists and aesthetic clinics, allowing visitors to combine wellness appointments with luxury retail.

Paragon Shopping Centre is one of my favourite malls along Orchard Road when I feel like doing some upscale shopping. I enjoy browsing the luxury brands here—it’s elegant but still comfortable to walk around. The basement has plenty of nice food choices too, whether it’s grabbing a quick bite or sitting down for a proper meal. I also like that the mall has a supermarket and even a medical wing with specialist clinics, so it’s really convenient for almost everything.

Nicely renovated and aisles are wider than before. The vegetable and fruits section has a wider selection too. But this is quite a small Fairprice outlet so don't expect to have multiple brands of the same kind. So if you are looking to make a quick dash to get something that you forgot to buy earlier as part of your cooking experiment, this place is a good stop.
Good place that you can find everything you need..good and clean vegetables and fruits!! Many options and brands

This morning go buy curry leaves,l can't find uncle told me at the other corner,l saw a wrong indicated there lime,l though don't have ask uncle ,that uncle very fierce shout at me say ,you can't see that curry leaves 😞.

Caters to those who doesn't do much wet Market shopping.
More expensive than other Fairprice outlet.
Terrible to pay more for the same goods & worse, from the same company.
It is best to source around before shopping here, this place is for those high life living nearby who bring their bag for life with them.

Tips: The cheaper option is just 6 blocks down the road.
ur butcher put away for 10min i wait 15min also nvr come back knn if need longer than put a time la dun waste ppl time
Unethical staff sold my boy a cup of slurpee that is not even ready. He came back with a cup of syrup and we live just 2 blocks away so how can it melt so fast?! It just wasn’t ready.
They used to sell good meadows chips here.My favourite.Almost came here to but it every week then a few months passed and the amount of chips,the quality them has downgraded. So disappointing.
Very kind hearted staff. Let me take warm water.
The staffs here are very kind. I live near this store, this is my canteen.
Impeccably kept with cheerful cashier...wares arranged neatly and attractively !

Went here at 12.30AM on Sunday, 7 March 2022. ITS CLOSED. NOT 24HRS.

just posting to help! ☺️

In a difficult situation, you helped me right away.
I am grateful for the generosity and kindness of the staff here.
Thanks to you, I am leaving with a very positive impression of Singapore.
Thank you.

Quiet location. Bought umbrella here as it was raining after lunch.
The lady is nice
Rude staff. Unbelievable. Locks door and ignores customers. Leaving the store in the middle of day without a word!!!!! Crazy woman.
The lady working today was so lovely!
The all item very fresh in the market

Probably the last vestiges of a generation lost in memory, this market is wet alright. No frills. No thrills. Just stuffs that only Merdeka and Pioneer generation aunties will buy.

That's only if you cook and don't have a helper at home. Decidedly utilitarian thus is how a market was in the 60s

Hawker food. An indian stall selling tosei, appom and variety of food. There is a corner malay stall selling lots of kuih2, mee siam, also prata and some others. A few stall not open yet. These 2 stall open as early as 5.30am.
Nostalgic experience,old vibes nice food.if you are a mee goteng fanatic this is a place to go.variety of malay foods amd chinese foods.highly recomend
Went there for breakfast. How food prices has gone up after the year end clean up of the market and food centre from my last visit last year.

4 stars because have Sheng Siong. Other than the 2 mini-mart like market stall (one run by Chinese aunty and another by an Indian auntie), others sell vegetables or typical produce. Also available are the stalls selling Chinese prayer items. Used to have a halal meat produce stall that sells marinated satay meat, but since renovation can't seem to find the stall anymore. Some previous vendor like the stall selling fresh noodles and tofu have shifted to the hawker/market at Bangkit.

Fajar wet market is relatively small with 2 fresh pork stalls, 2 vegetable stall, a fresh YTF stall, 1 Indian and 1 Chinese provision shop. Here's also the Fajar SC with Sheng Siong and Giant, both 24hr; Macdonald and 7eleven. more shops... selling cake n bread, mobile phone, medical hall, ABC shop, a coffee shop and a AC food court. SG Poolz, music school at the 2nd level
Got 2 supermarket n wet market. Comprehensive choice of groceries n 2 coffeeshops for hawker food.
A neighborhood convenient center that has food centre, supermarket, wet market, hard ware shop and many more
Went for service koufu's automatic door

My 3 kids “graduated” from swim lessons with AQZOG swim school with Coach Raymond at Delta pool. we only started in September 2024, in less than 2 years. No choice..nobody to bring them to pool, sigh, temporary pause.... But at least from novices who didn’t know how to swim, (2 of them didn’t even dare jump into the pool), all could swim already. The basic strokes, survival strokes… Can have fun in the middle of deep pool comfortably… good enough. In fact they skipped direct to test Swimsafer stage 2 (

Pool facilities and infrastructure are pretty good with 1 Olympic lap pool and 1 teaching pool. Toilets are mostly clean with around 12 showers (Men) and for an entry price of $1 or monthly passes for $10, it is a pool accessible to the public. The staff who man the entrance/exit are friendly and greet you when you enter/exit and there’s 3-4 lifeguards observing the pools constantly.

The people using the pool on the other hand leave much to be desired. Between the Mr Magoo lookalike who walks across lanes at the end of the Olympic pool instead of using the teaching pool, the people who see a busy lane and insist on not just joining that lane but not swimming around the people already in the pool, the old people who cut the queues at the entrance when the pool first opens and the kids doing swimming lessons who can start in lane 2 and swim diagonally to lane 4 in a single lap, you would think that half the users are either blind/vision impaired or have no social awareness

Also, do note that the pool crowd website is not accurate for use as the pool had around 40 people in it when the website showed 13% full. 100% occupancy would thus be around 300 people (~38 people per lane), this means the pools would be practically unusable at 30%+ capacity as you’d be dodging and weaving around people every lap you swim.

Before deciding to swim here do take note that the pools get crowded and the people who use it are not the most civic minded with minimal social awareness.

Very decent, neatly maintained community pool. Toilets are clean and it has separate changing rooms for kids and adults.

As like any other place, based on the crowd and people your experience might vary, as there will be some folks who would be abusing or misusing everything. Similarly can get too crowded during weekends and there will be too many coaching classes on-going.

3 different pools, one for toddlers and young ones. One lap pool and a pool seperate for learning.
Great pool and facilities. Whoever put in the floors in the Men’s changing room did a bad job, water collects in puddles. I will be back for another swim soon.

Lived in Redhill for many years and have witnessed the gym, pool, running track undergo improvements. Kudos to the relevant authorities for upgrading the facilities here. The entire complex was torn down & rebuilt. It reopened in 2023. It even has pickle / padel ball courts now. Bonus for getting a MacDonald’s in.

Been living her 6 years and it’s amazing although it may be next to a road and can be noisy sometimes if your below the 8th floor it’s got good facilities and is close to the biggest and best shopping areas in Singapore being orchard road

Bizzare policies in this condo. Their gym opens at 7 am. If you are a busy professional who wants to start their day off with exercise and / or are a working mom who likes to get a healthy start to your day, this isn't the place for you to consider living in. Someone keeps complaining of noise from the gym but really it is strange because I see other people working out with the gym doors open and somehow that isn't the rootcause of the noise issue. 🤯🤯

Management is not thinking properly before appeasing these constant nuisance complaints. No idea if the person complaining non stop is somehow special and deserves an extreme reaction.

I'll take down this if gym timings go back to reasonable hours.
Not a good place to live in as near main road.  Noisy and pollution there.
My second home in Singapore. Again, amazing and comfortable residencece.
Can i have the contact no of the guard in entrance pls.

Finally caught a movie in the theatre again and decided to try Gold Class — a great experience, especially for good, long movies. The seats are extremely comfortable and spacious, making it easy to fully relax and enjoy the show

I like that you can order food either at the Gold Class lounge beforehand or have it served directly in the theatre during the movie. Complimentary water and snacks are a nice touch and add to the premium feel

Seats are average and not that comfortable going into long movies.
No food on the list for people with food allergies and unable to bring your own food in if you suffer from these type of health concerns (gluten-free)

Aerin's birthday called for a celebratory dinner and movie, and Great World City seemed the perfect place. Tajimaya Yakiniku delivered a delicious feast, setting the stage for a relaxing evening. The Golden Village Grand, conveniently located within the mall, was our next stop. We’d initially planned on a different, slightly more mature film, but Aerin’s age dictated a change of plans, leading us to a family-friendly Marvel movie, a choice she surprisingly didn't mind, even though she'd already seen it with her friends. The cinema itself was a welcome respite. Comfortable seats, a crisp, clear screen, and excellent sound quality all contributed to a comfortable viewing experience. However, the nacho set, even with extra cheese, was a major letdown. The cheese portion was surprisingly small. It left us feeling a little shortchanged. Despite this minor hiccup, the overall experience was pleasant, and Aerin seemed to enjoy herself. The location alone makes it worthy of a return trip, just maybe we'll skip the nachos next time.

The GV gold class here seems to be very lowly staffed. The food and drinks all came in very late.

Before the movie, we came in early to order some food, but there was no one at the cashier/ reception. The time came and he had to head in for our movie.

Ordered some food at 2030, and some drinks at 2050, the food only came at 2130. What more, we are watching Gold Class. Naturally we do expect much better services. I pressed the bell multiple times and No one, Not one person came to attend to us. I caught one staff while he was serving drinks to another. I asked about my order at 2030, and she just says the food is still being prepared. I honestly don’t know what to say. 1 hour? And the food is not even prepped?

I have been to other outlets and I could say the services in other GV Gold Class are much much better.

We saw that all other customers received their food at the same time, and the food I ordered, was alr somewhat cold. They seem to hold the food, bring them all at up at a certain time, sort them at the door and serve them all at once.

Honestly, I don’t think this makes a good experience for everyone.
Shouldn’t the food get prepared, and served promptly upon customer order?

This would be my last time here!

The cinema is reopening back at level 3 of Great World City.

It also offers the luxurious cinema hall with premium tickets.

I used to watch movies here in early 2000s.

Check out for the upcoming promotion available from 27 August 2023 onwards.

There were a bunch of rude old seniors who attempt to do taiji quan every Friday night. They refused to move and were hogging the basketball court when they could have done their activities at the empty badminton court next door. Despite me and my friend's attempts to negotiate with them and request for them to share the space, they possessed an entitled mentality and told us we had to move as they "were going to take out the weapons soon". They also discriminated against my dyslexic friend. When we tried to discuss the issue with them, they became rude and threatening, saying they would upload pictures of us. They did take multiple photos of us against our consent and showcased xenophobia by stating we were foreigners. To top it off, they then questioned our Singaporean identity. These senile individuals should not be allowed to occupy the CC and I request for the management to curtail their activities and tell the organisers to manage the behaviour of their members. On top of this, we had a valid booking for the court which they disregarded. What a waste of a lovely basketball facility

I visit this CC only once every 4 yrs. Since my son was toddler until now, he goes by himself now.

Good memories....
Once again, we r back to the World Cup, Qatar.

Thanks to the CC n members for organising this, esp for those who can't afford the hefty cable TV costs.

Special shout out also to the helpers who were friendly and patient to us football fanatics. 👍👍👍

Offer very useful and fun tuinl service; there is a post office, Pops parcel receiving and sending, community club services like dance classes, badminton hall and basketball courts. There is also a electronic recycle dropoff box here which use regularly. The building looks a bit dated but it still serves the residents living here well.

Bukit Timah Community Club is located at 20 Toh Yi Drive. Offering a host of activities and facilities for your use at an affordable price or courses available for all age groups. With additional school holiday program for the young to spend some healthy, creative with play and learn science.
Went for my Covid vaccination. It was well organized. I arrived at 8.20pm and was out at 9.30pm.
Beautiful condo with phenomenal facilities. My Uncle own an apartment here. We go there frequently to spend quality time.

Good condo to stay in
Conducive for kids due to the garden walk and playground. Very clean and well maintained.
Large apartments, attached ensures in every rooms.
This place is awesome I loved it here!!!!

Best security team day or night

Never run into problems with any of the team when running errands and deliveries for the residents

Alert but easygoing and good with contractors

This 4 bedroom unit is facing Orchard with almost unblock view. Located in prime location,  Spacious unit layout but sign of ageing showing up within the development and facilities, considering it is just about 10 year out.
A beautiful condo with all the facilities u need.

place_name: Riverside Terrace at Grand Copthorne Waterfront Hotel
review_text: As part of the Culinary Compass, came to discover this riverside cafe for a lite lunch. Ordered with the recommendation of the front of house Raj the Grilled Octopus Salad and Seafood Aglio Olio. He mentioned all things grilled were excellent choices in the Riverside Terrace. Having been seated close the the grill, the aroma of grilled seafood does indeed make for a unique lunch setting. The breeze was lite and cooling outdoors. The octopus salad was excellent, the aglio olio, though small in portion packed quite a lot in quality. Decided to use my complimentary drink / dessert voucher from Culinary Compass. Was given the best plated and well tasting dessert I've had since going on this journey. Big kudos to the team here, didn't know that there was good food and service here. Will come back for more in the coming months.

place_name: Riverside Terrace at Grand Copthorne Waterfront Hotel
review_text: The restaurant looks dated, but the food was great. Fries was unstoppably good! Ordered Norwegian Salmon, Jumbo King Prawn n Chicken Satay.  All three dishes were very well done.

place_name: Riverside Terrace at Grand Copthorne Waterfront Hotel
review_text: We came here on Saturday afternoon lunch. Great place, we chose indoor seating and we were the only table. We had Hokkien Mee and Chicken Pesto Linguine. Linguine not al dente. Hokkien mee was flavourful. We will try the grill next time as they have Josper grill.

place_name: Riverside Terrace at Grand Copthorne Waterfront Hotel
review_text:
Lovely outdoor eatery at the Grand Copthorne Waterfront Hotel.  Great place to chill by the riverside with a glass of wine.

The food is decent too - steaks grilled to perfection.

Special mention to staff member Candy who was attentive and helped to made sure the kitchen prepared my order before it got too busy with an upcoming function.

place_name: Riverside Terrace at Grand Copthorne Waterfront Hotel
review_text:
I had a wonderful experience at River Terrace. The food was excellent, with a great variety of dishes that were fresh and full of flavor. The environment was very pleasant and relaxing, making it a perfect place to enjoy a meal with family or friends.

What stood out the most was the staff’s attitude—they were friendly, attentive, and very welcoming throughout the visit. The service was smooth and efficient, which made the overall dining experience even better.

Overall, River Terrace offers great food, a comfortable atmosphere, and excellent service. I would definitely recommend it and look forward to visiting again.
